messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the radius-server service-type in-access-request command do?

The radius-server service-type in-access-request command this configuration will include the Service-Type RADIUS attribute in Access-Request packets sent to a RADIUS server. The value of the attribute will always be NAS-Prompt-User. The no form of this command disables this feature. Syntax: no radius-server service-type in-access-request.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the radius-server timeout [<1-60>] command do?

The radius-server timeout [<1-60>] command specifies the number of seconds to wait for a response from the RADIUS server before trying the next RADIUS server. If a value is not specified, a default value of 5 seconds is used. You can override this value with a fine-grained per server timeout configured for individual servers. The no form of this command resets the RADIUS global authentication timeout to the default of 5 seconds. Specifies the timeout interval of 1 to 60 seconds. Default: 5 sec onds. Syntax: no radius-server timeout [<1-60>].

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the router rip <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command do?

The router rip <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command creates RIP process if not already created and enters the router rip <PROCESS-ID> context for the VRF mentioned. If no VRF is mentioned, a default is used. Only one RIP process is allowed per VRF. The no form of this command deletes the RIP instance for the VRF. If no VRF is mentioned the default is deleted. Specifies name of the RIP process ID. Range: 1-63. <PROCESS-ID> vrf <VRF-NAME> Specifies VRF name. Syntax: no router rip <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>].

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ip rip command do?

The ip rip command enables RIP process on an interface. The no form of this command deletes the RIP process from an interface. Public RIP v2 commands 2865 ip rip <PROCESS-ID> Specifies RIP process ID. Range: 1-63. all-ip ip-address. Syntax: ip rip <PROCESS-ID> {all-ip | ip-address}.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the clear ip rip statistics command do?

The clear ip rip statistics command clears RIP event statistics. Specifies RIP process ID. Range: 1-63 <PROCESS-ID> all-vrfs vrf <VRF-NAME> Clears statistics for all VRFs. Selects VRF to clear statistics for. Specifies VRF name. Public RIP v2 clear commands 2880 Enables RIP process for RIP enabled IP address configured on interface. The no form of this command disables RIP process on interface. Disables RIP process for RIP enabled IP addresses configured on interface. The no form of this command enables RIP process on interface. Disables an interface from sending RIP packets for a specific IP address. The no form of this command enables interface for sending RIP packets for a specific IP address. Disables interface from receiving RIP packets for a specific IP address. Syntax: clear ip rip [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>].

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show ip aspath-list command do?

The show ip aspath-list command shows the configuration details of the AS path list. Specifies name of the AS path list. <NAME> vsx-peer Shows the configuration details of the community-list. Specifies name of the community-list. Shows the output from the VSX peer switch. If the switches do not have the VSX configuration or the ISL is down, the output f rom the VSX peer switch is not displayed. This parameter is avai lable on switches that support VSX. <NAME> vsx-peer Shows the configuration details of the IP prefix lists. Specifies name of the IP prefix lists. <NAME> vsx-peer Shows the output from the VSX peer switch. If the switches do not have the VSX configuration or the ISL is down, the output f rom the VSX peer switch is not displayed. This parameter is avai lable on switches that support VSX. Syntax: show ip aspath-list [<NAME>] [vsx-peer].

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show mac-notify command do?

The show mac-notify command displays whether the MAC notification feature in the SNMP module is enabled or not. It also displays the trap notification types configured on the Layer 2 ports in the system. Syntax: show mac-notify 3087.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show rmon alarm command do?

The show rmon alarm command displays the RMON alarm configurations. Specifies the RMON alarm index. Range: 1 to 20. Displays SNMP agent UDP port number. Displays a list of all configured SNMP v1/v2c communities. Displays all configured SNMP contexts. Displays the configured SNMP v3 snmp engine-id. If the SNMP v3 engine-id is not configured, by default a unique engine-id is created by the switch using a combination of the enterprise OID value and the switch's MAC address. Displays the configured SNMP v3 security level. Displays SNMP description, location, and contact information. Displays all configured SNMP traps/informs receivers. Displays all configured SNMP v3 users. For more details on the user enabled status, see snmpv3 security-level. Displays the list of all the configured SNMP views. Syntax: show rmon alarm [index <INDEX>].

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the snmp-server community <STRING> [view <VIEW-NAME>] command do?

The snmp-server community <STRING> [view <VIEW-NAME>] command associates an SNMP MIB view with the SNMP community. The no form of this command removes the associated SNMP MIB view from the SNMP community. Specifies the SNMP v1/SNMP v2c community string. Range: 1 to 32 printable ASCII characters, excluding space and question ma rk. Specifies the view name for the SNMP MIB view. Accepts a maxi mum of 32 characters. <STRING> <VIEW-NAME>. Syntax: no snmp-server community <STRING> [view <VIEW-NAME>].

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the snmp-server community <STRING> command do?

The snmp-server community <STRING> command adds an SNMP v1/SNMP v2c community string. A community string is like a password that controls read/ write access to the SNMP agent. A network management program must supply this name when attempting to get SNMP information from the switch. A maximum of 10 community strings are supported. Once you create your own community string, the default community string (public) is deleted. The no form of this command removes the specified SNMP v1/SNMP v2c community string. When no community string exists, a default community string with the value public is automatically defined. <STRING> Specifies the SNMP v1/SNMP v2c community string. Range: 1 to 32 printable ASCII characters, excluding space and question ma rk. Syntax: no snmp-server community <STRING>.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the snmp-server snmpv3-only command do?

The snmp-server snmpv3-only command accepts SNMP v3 messages only, SNMP v1 and SNMP v2c will be disabled. By default SNMP v1, SNMP v2c and SNMP v3 will all be enabled. The no form of this command restores the default setting and reenables SNMP v1 and SNMP v2c. Syntax:
- no snmp-server snmpv3-only
- snmp-server snmpv3-only 3133.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the snmp-server trap aaa-server-reachability-status command do?

The snmp-server trap aaa-server-reachability-status command enables the SNMP trap for AAA server status. When enabled, traps are sent whenever AAA server (RADIUS, TACACS) status changes from reachable to unreachable and vice versa. The no form of this command disables sending SNMP trap for AAA server status. Syntax:
- no snmp-server trap aaa-server-reachability-status
- snmp-server trap aaa-server-reachability-status 3134.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the snmp-server trap configuration-changes command do?

The snmp-server trap configuration-changes command enables sending SNMP traps whenever the configuration changes. Configuration trap generation is disabled by default. The no form of this command disables sending SNMP traps for configuration changes. configuration-changes Specifies SNMP traps for configuration changes. Syntax:

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the snmp-server trap vsx command do?

The snmp-server trap vsx command enables sending the SNMP traps for VSX related events. VSX trap generation is disabled by default. The no form of this command disables sending the SNMP traps for VSX related events. The trap support is available for the following VSX events: • ISL up and down Public snmp-server trap vsx 3139 • KA up and down • MCLAG up and down Specifies SNMP traps for VSX events. Syntax: no snmp-server trap vsx.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the snmp-server trap command do?

The snmp-server trap command enables the SNMP traps. The SNMP traps are enabled by default. The no form of this command disables the SNMP traps. cpu-utilization Enables the CPU utilization traps. memory-utilization Enables the memory utilization traps. rmon-events Enables the RMON event traps. Syntax: snmp-server trap {cpu-utilization | memory-utilization | rmon-events}.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the snmp-server view <VIEWNAME> command do?

The snmp-server view <VIEWNAME> command configures an SNMP MIB view. Public snmp-server view 3142 The no form of this command removes the specified SNMP MIB view. Specifies the name of the SNMP MIB view. Supports up to a ma ximum of 32 characters. Specifies the OID tree to be included or excluded in SNMP MIB view. Specifies the OID mask value. The values must be in hexadecim al character separated with: (colon). Specifies the OID tree that is included in or excluded from the S NMP MIB view. <VIEWNAME> <OID_TREE> <MASK> <included/excluded>. Syntax: no snmp-server view <VIEWNAME>.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ip route <DEST-IPv4-ADDR> command do?

The ip route <DEST-IPv4-ADDR> command adds an IPv4 static route on the default VRF. The no form of this command deletes a IPv4 static route. NOTE You can configure a maximum of 32 next hops per route. Specifies the IPv4 route destination. <DEST-IPv4-ADDR>/<NETMASK> Specifies the number of bits in the address mask in CIDR format (x), where x is a decimal number from 0 to 32. Public ip route 3191 <NEXTHOP-ADDR> <NEXTHOP-PORT-LAG-VLAN> nullroute reject Specifies the next hop address for reaching the destination in I Pv4 format (x.x.x.x), where x is a decimal number from 0 to 25 5. Specifies the next hop as an outgoing interface. Specifies that packets matching the destination route are silentl y discarded and no ICMP error notification is sent to the sender. Syntax: no ip route <DEST-IPv4-ADDR>.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the interface lag <LAGNUM>.<ID> command do?

The interface lag <LAGNUM>.<ID> command creates a subinterface on an L3 interface and enters subinterface configuration mode. The subinterface name consists of the parent interface name (for example, 1/1/1) followed by a period and a unique ID number. Public interface 3222 The no form of these commands deletes a subinterface from an L3 interface. NOTE • 8325, 8325H, 8325P, and 10000 Switch series require an internal VLAN for every subinterface, in addition to the internal VLANs used for other purposes. For example, to support 1024 subinterfaces, an internal VLAN range that is at least that large must be reserved using the system internal- vlan-range command. 8325, 8325H, 8325P, and 10000 Series switches support a maximum of 1024 subinterfaces resources. Syntax: no interface lag <LAGNUM>.<ID>.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ssh certificate-as-authorized-key command do?

The ssh certificate-as-authorized-key command enables SSH enforcement that the username must be present in the certificate that is being used for authorization. This configuration alters how certificate-based authentication maps to a user account. When this is enabled, SSH will not require local user association with an authorized-key and instead enforces that the username used to log in is present within the certificate. The SSH server will check for the username in certificate fields Common Name or User Principle Name for a match. If a certificate is not used for authentication then this configuration has no effect on SSH authentication. The no form of this command disables the SSH enforcement of username in the certificate. Syntax: no ssh certificate-as-authorized-key.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show user-group command do?

The show user-group command shows user group information for the built-in groups plus any user-defined local user groups. When entered without <GROUP-NAME>, summary information is shown for all groups. Narrows the show command output to that of the specified gro up, and for local user groups, adds the User Group Rules list. Shows the output from the VSX peer switch. If the switches do not have the VSX configuration or the ISL is down, the output f rom the VSX peer switch is not displayed. This parameter is avai lable on switches that support VSX. <GROUP-NAME> vsx-peer Shows the following information for the logged-in user: • User name. • User authentication type: local, RADIUS, or TACACS+. • User group: administrators, operators, or <GROUP-NAME>. Syntax: show user-group [<GROUP-NAME>] [vsx-peer].

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the vlan <VLAN-LIST> command do?

The vlan <VLAN-LIST> command creates a VLAN and changes to the config-vlan-id context for the VLAN. By default, the VLAN is enabled. To disable a VLAN, use the shutdown command. If the specified VLAN exists, this command changes to the config-vlan-id context for the VLAN. If a range of VLANs is specified, the context does not change. NOTE VLANs used for internal purposes using the command system internal vlan range cannot be used for any other (L2) purposes. The no form of this command removes a VLAN. VLAN 1 is the default VLAN and cannot be deleted. Specifies a single ID, or a series of IDs separated by commas (2, 3, 4), dashes (2-4), or both (2-4, 6). Range: 1 to 4040. <VLAN-LIST> Creates an access interface and assigns an VLAN ID to it. Only one VLAN ID can be assigned to each access interface. Syntax: vlan access <VLAN-ID>.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the neighbor <IP-ADDRESS> vsx-sync-exclude command do?

The neighbor <IP-ADDRESS> vsx-sync-exclude command excludes the neighbor configurations from VSX sync operations at run time. When VSX sync is configured for BGP, the VSX secondary peer cannot have any configuration of its own. If the VSX primary has a configuration, that will always get synchronized to the VSX secondary. Any modification done in the synchronized configurations on the VSX secondary will be overwritten by the VSX primary configuration after a VSX config sync. If there is a new configuration on the VSX secondary, then that configuration also gets deleted after a VSX config sync. On the VSX primary, a configuration with vsx-sync-exclude enabled excludes the neighbor configurations from synchronizing to the VSX -secondary, allowing the VSX secondary can have it own entry for the corresponding neighbor configurations. Syntax: neighbor <IP-ADDRESS> vsx-sync-exclude.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show lacp interfaces multi-chassis command do?

The show lacp interfaces multi-chassis command shows all configured VSX remote interface details. The interface that has the ALFNCD status has been synced with the partner and is ready for flow distribution. Specifies the VSX interface name. Optional. <IFNAME> vsx-peer Displays all configured interface commands, including VSX commands. Displays the configured VSX commands. Displays the lines of running-configuration that VSX configuration synchronization is enabled on. The command also provides a rolled-up view of configuration expected to be synced. This command can be run from the primary or secondary peer. Displays the difference between the configuration of features enabled for VSX synchronization on the primary and secondary switches. Syntax: show lacp interfaces multi-chassis [<IFNAME>] [vsx-peer].

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the vsx command do?

The vsx command creates the VSX context on the switch. The no form of this command disables the VSX context on the switch and removes all related configuration settings. Public vsx 3634. Syntax: no vsx.

The vsx active-forwarding command configures VSX active-forwarding on an interface VLAN. The no form of this command unconfigures VSX active-forwarding on a VLAN interface. Public vsx active-forwarding 3635. Syntax: no vsx active-forwarding.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the vsx shutdown-on-split command do?

The vsx shutdown-on-split command shuts down the configured non-VSX interfaces on the VSX secondary along with VSX interfaces during a VSX split. The no form of this command resumes the non-VSX interfaces that are shut down during the VSX split. NOTE This command has no effect on the VSX primary during a split. However, when applied on the VSX primary, the command will bring down the non-VSX interfaces until linkup delay timer expires during the VSX primary reboot. Syntax: no vsx shutdown-on-split.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the vsx-sync {[active-gateways] [policies]} command do?

The vsx-sync {[active-gateways] [policies]} command enables VSX sync of active gateways or policies associated under an interface. To synchronize the associations, you must configure the same interface vlan on the peer switch. The no form of this command removes VSX synchronization for active gateways or policies associated under an interface, but it does not remove the feature configurations from the secondary peer switch. Public vsx-sync (config-vlan-if context) 3647 {[active-gateways] [policies]} access-gateways policies. Syntax: no vsx-sync {[active-gateways] [policies]}.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the vsx-sync aaa command do?

The vsx-sync aaa command enables VSX synchronization of all AAA configurations, including user, RADIUS server, and TACACS+ server, on the primary VSX node to the secondary peer switch. The no form of this command removes VSX synchronization of global AAA configurations, but it does not remove the existing global AAA feature configurations from the secondary peer switch. Syntax: no vsx-sync aaa.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the vsx-sync bfd-global command do?

The vsx-sync bfd-global command enables syncing of global BFD configurations, such as echo-src-ip-address, detect-multip lier, min-transmit-interval, and min-receive-interval, on the primary VSX node to the secondary peer. NOTE This command enables VSX synchronization only at the top level and not at the context level. The no form of this command disables the syncing of global BFD configurations to the secondary peer, but it does not remove the existing global BFD feature configurations from it. Syntax: no vsx-sync bfd-global.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the vsx-sync bgp command do?

The vsx-sync bgp command enables syncing of BGP configurations on the primary VSX switch to the secondary peer switch. The no form of this command disables syncing BGP, as path lists, community lists, prefix lists, and route map configurations to the secondary peer, but it does not remove the previously synced configurations from the secondary peer switch. Syntax: no vsx-sync bgp.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the vsx-sync dhcp-server command do?

The vsx-sync dhcp-server command enables VSX synchronization of all DHCPv4 server configurations, including external storage configurations, on the primary VSX node to the secondary peer. Only the primary VSX node answers DHCP service requests, and leases can only be exported from the primary VSX node. The no form of the command disables VSX synchronization of DHCPv4 server configurations to the secondary peer; however, it does not remove the existing DHCPv4 server feature configurations from the secondary peer. Syntax: no vsx-sync dhcp-server.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the vsx-sync evpn command do?

The vsx-sync evpn command enables syncing of all EVPN context-related configurations on primary VSX node to the secondary peer switch. The no form of this command disables syncing EVPN configurations to the secondary peer, but it does not remove the previously synced configurations from the secondary peer switch. NOTE As a prerequisite, VLAN vsx-sync must be enabled separately for the VLAN configurations inside EVPN context to get synced. Syntax: no vsx-sync evpn.

However, it does not remove the existing IP ICMP configurations from the secondary VSX peer. Syntax: no vsx-sync icmp-tcp.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the vsx-sync keychain command do?

The vsx-sync keychain command enables synchronizing of key chain configurations on primary VSX node to the secondary peer. There is no configuration synchronization from secondary to primary peer. If any additional modification or configuration is made on the primary for the key chain set of features, the features will be auto-synchronized. The no form of the command disables synchronizing key chain configurations to the secondary peer. But it does not remove the previously synchronized configurations from the secondary peer. Syntax: no vsx-sync keychain.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the vsx-sync mclag-interfaces command do?

The vsx-sync mclag-interfaces command enables the VSX synchronization of VSX LAG interface associations and attributes on the primary VSX switch to the secondary peer switch. The Usage section in this topic provides a listing of specific associations and attributes that are synchronized to the secondary switch. The no form of this command removes VSX synchronization of global VSX LAG and attributes, but it does not remove the existing VSX LAG feature configurations from the secondary peer switch. Syntax: no vsx-sync mclag-interfaces.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the vsx-sync nd-snooping command do?

The vsx-sync nd-snooping command enables VSX synchronization of ND snooping configurations on the primary VSX node to the secondary peer switch. To synchronize ND snooping configurations associated with a particular VLAN and interface, configure the same VLAN and interface on the peer device. When RA guard policy is enabled, this command also synchronizes RA guard policy related configurations. The no form of this command disables syncing ND snooping configurations to the secondary peer, but it does not remove the previously synced configurations from the secondary peer. Syntax: no vsx-sync nd-snooping.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the vsx-sync neighbor command do?

The vsx-sync neighbor command enables VSX synchronization of IPv4 and IPv6 static neighbors configuration on primary VSX node to the secondary peer. There is no configuration sync from secondary to primary peer. If any new modification or additional configuration is made on the primary node for IPv4 and IPv6 static neighbors configuration, they will be auto-synced. The no form of this command VSX synchronization of IPv4 and IPv6 static neighbors configurations to the secondary peer, but it does not remove the previously synced configurations from the secondary peer switch. Syntax: no vsx-sync neighbor.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the vsx-sync ospf command do?

The vsx-sync ospf command enables syncing of OSPF (including OSPFv2 and OPSFv3), route map, and key chain configurations on the primary VSX switch. There is no configuration sync from secondary to primary peer. To synchronize OSPF configurations at the port level context, configure the same port on the peer device. Public vsx-sync ospf 3674 The no form of this command disables syncing of OSPF, route map, and key chain configurations to the secondary peer. But it does not remove the previously synced configurations from the secondary peer switch. NOTE The OSPF router ID is not synchronized. This exclusion is needed because the router ID uniquely identifies the router. The two OSPF routers with the same router ID do not form an adjacency between them. Syntax: no vsx-sync ospf.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the vsx-sync qos-global command do?

The vsx-sync qos-global command enables the VSX synchronization of global QoS configurations, such as CoS map, DSCP map, and trust policy, on the primary VSX node to the secondary peer switch. To enable VSX synchronization at the context level for this feature, enter the vsx-sync qos command at the context level. The no form of this command removes VSX synchronization of global QoS configurations, but it does not remove the existing global QoS feature configurations from the secondary peer switch. Syntax: no vsx-sync qos-global.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the vsx-sync route-map command do?

The vsx-sync route-map command enables syncing of all As Path lists, community lists, prefix lists, and route map configurations on primary VSX node to the secondary peer switch. There is no configuration sync from the secondary to primary peer. Public vsx-sync route-map 3678 The no form of this command disables syncing of As Path lists, community lists, prefix lists, and route map configurations to the secondary peer, but it does not remove the previously synced configurations from the secondary peer switch. NOTE When vsx-sync for BGP or OSPF is configured, route map configurations are synchronized from primary VSX node to the secondary peer due to the dependency in configurations. Syntax: no vsx-sync route-map.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the vsx-sync vrrp command do?

The vsx-sync vrrp command enables VSX synchronization of all VRRP configurations on the primary VSX node to the secondary peer switch. There is no configuration sync from secondary to primary peer. To synchronize VRRP configurations at the port level context, the same port must be configured on the peer device with IP address. Public vsx-sync vrrp 3690 The no form of this command disables syncing VRRP configurations to the secondary peer, but it does not remove the previously synced configurations from the secondary peer. NOTE BFD IP is the IP address of VRRP peer device. Hence it cannot be synced. In the owner scenario, in case the priority is synced, both VSX primary and secondary devices will have 255 as their priority. Syntax: no vsx-sync vrrp.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show interface vxlan vteps command do?

The show interface vxlan vteps command displays detailed VTEP information for the VXLAN interface. VTEP states can be one of the following: • operational: Tunnel endpoint is fully programmed on the switch hardware. • configuration_error: Tunnel endpoint programming in the switch hardware failed due to misconfiguration. • no_hw_resources: Tunnel endpoint programming failed in the switch hardware due to insufficient resources. • activating: Initial state of tunnel endpoint when it is configured. When a tunnel endpoint is a directly connected via nexthop, then nexthop reachability appears empty (--). Show detail VTEP information. Specifies the IP address of a VTEP peer in IPv4 format (x.x.x.x), where x is a decimal number from 0 to 255. Syntax: show interface vxlan vteps [detail | <IP-ADDR>].

For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, how do you configure an isolated network?

To configure an isolated network, follow the documented procedure: 1. Create the container context 2. Configure the container image location 3. Configure the container mount 4. Configure the container mount source 5. Configure the container mount destination 6. Enable the container 7. Validate the container status. Commands shown in the procedure include: show container <NAME>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, how do you configure the isolated network?
To configure the isolated network, follow the documented procedure: 1. Configure the isolated network 2. Create the virtual interface 3. Set the IP address for the interface.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the container <NAME> exec <PARAMS> command do?

The container <NAME> exec <PARAMS> command allows the execution of an endpoint script in the container. The location of this endpoint is provided to the container manager infrastructure through a manifest file in the image file system of the container. This manifest file provides metadata related to the container application. When the exec command runs, the manifest information is used to determine the endpoint to execute and the user parameters are passed directly to the endpoint. The output of such execution is provided directly to the user through the CLI. In case the manifest information or the endpoint file are missing an error is presented to the user. The user can interrupt the execution by pressing Ctrl+C. Syntax: container <NAME> exec <PARAMS>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the container <NAME> exec <PARAMS> command?
The syntax of the container <NAME> exec <PARAMS> command is: container <NAME> exec <PARAMS>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the default-gateway <ip-addr> command do?

The default-gateway <ip-addr> command configures a default gateway IP address for the isolated network. The default gateway is used by containers to route traffic outside the local network provided by the virtual interfaces. After completing the configuration, use the show container isolated-network command to verify that the default gateway is valid. It will automatically check the IP address is within the subnet of at least one configured virtual interface and report any error. The no version of this command removes the default gateway configuration. Public default-gateway 20 The default gateway used by a containers in isolated network m ode to route traffic outside the local network provided by the vi rtual interfaces. Syntax: default-gateway <ip-addr>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the default-gateway <ip-addr> command?
The syntax of the default-gateway <ip-addr> command is: default-gateway <ip-addr>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the enable command do?

The enable command a container is disabled by default when it is created. Use the enable command to enable the container. The no version of this command disables the container. When an enabled container is disabled, system resources are released but the container configuration settings are stored. Public enable 21. Syntax: enable.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the enable command?
The syntax of the enable command is: enable.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ip address command do?

The ip address command configures an IPv4 address and subnet mask for a virtual interface for an isolated network. Each virtual interface must have a unique, non-overlapping subnet. After completing the configuration, use the show-c ontainer-isolated-network command to verify that the subnet assignments are valid. This will automatically check for overlapping or duplicate subnets and report any detected conflicts. The no version of this command removes the IP address configuration from the virtual interface. NOTE Do not assign the same IP address to both a virtual interface and an SVI (interface VLAN) in any VRF that uses the same VLAN. Syntax: ip address <ipaddr>/<submask>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the mdns enable command do?

The mdns enable command allow a container in isolated network mode to use the multicast DNS (mDNS) protocol for the isolated network. When mDNS is enabled, containers can look up names on a local network without a regular DNS server. This allows devices on the same VLAN or subnet find container services and match hostnames to IP addresses using multicast. The no version of this command disables this feature. Syntax: mdns enable.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the mdns enable command?
The syntax of the mdns enable command is: mdns enable.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the network isolated-network <name> command do?

The network isolated-network <name> command configure the isolated network to be used by the container application operating in isolated-network mode. The no version of this command ensures that the container no longer uses the indicated isolated-network as its network mode. Name of the new isolated network Associate the container app with an isolated network named net1. switch (config-container-app)# network isolated-network net1. Syntax: network isolated-network <name>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the network isolated-network <name> command?
The syntax of the network isolated-network <name> command is: network isolated-network <name>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the network vrf <VRF> command do?

The network vrf <VRF> command use this command to associate a VRF with the container and allow connectivity through the VRF. When configured, the VRF will be accessible to the container through a network namespace. Syntax: network vrf <VRF>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the network vrf <VRF> command?
The syntax of the network vrf <VRF> command is: network vrf <VRF>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the port-map host-port <PORT> container-port <PORT> protocol (tcp | udp) command?
The syntax of the port-map host-port <PORT> container-port <PORT> protocol (tcp | udp) command is: port-map host-port <PORT> container-port <PORT> protocol (tcp | udp)
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the restrict cpu <PERCENTAGE> command do?

The restrict cpu <PERCENTAGE> command configures limitations for the container CPU usage. The CPU constraint is set as a percentage of the total switch CPUs. One container or any combination of containers configured on a switch can use up to 20% of the total CPU capacity of the device. NOTE Configuring the CPU constraint for an already operational container will cause the container to restart if the container has already been enabled. The no form of this command removes restrictions on the CPU usage, and reverts back to the default limitation of 10%. Specifies percentage for the container CPU usage, The default value is 10%. <PERCENTAGE>. Syntax: restrict cpu <PERCENTAGE>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the restrict cpu <PERCENTAGE> command?
The syntax of the restrict cpu <PERCENTAGE> command is: restrict cpu <PERCENTAGE>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the restrict memory <MB> command do?

The restrict memory <MB> command configures limitations for memory usage of the container. The memory constraint is set in MB, and the maximum 20% of the capacity of the device can be configured for any combination of containers running on the switch. Configuring the memory constraint for an already operational container restarts the container if the container has already been enabled. The no form of this command removes restrictions on the memory usage and reverts back to the default limitation of 256 MB. Specifies the maximum memory usage in MB. The default value i s 256 MB. <MB> Public restrict memory 38. Syntax: restrict memory <MB>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the restrict memory <MB> command?
The syntax of the restrict memory <MB> command is: restrict memory <MB>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show container command?
The syntax of the show container command is: show container [<CONTAINER-NAME>]|[isolated-network <name>]
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show capacities containers command do?
The show capacities containers command shows the maximum number of containerized applications that can be configured in the system. Syntax: show capacities containers.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show capacities containers command?
The syntax of the show capacities containers command is: show capacities containers.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show capacities-status containers command do?
The show capacities-status containers command reserved for future use. Syntax: show capacities-status containers.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show capacities-status containers command?
The syntax of the show capacities-status containers command is: show capacities-status containers.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show running-config container command?
The syntax of the show running-config container command is: show running-config container.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the virtual-interface vlan <1-4094> command do?

The virtual-interface vlan <1-4094> command sets up a virtual interface in an isolated network for a container in isolated-network mode. This virtual interface is linked to a VLAN ID and works as a network connection for the isolated network. It lets containers communicate with external devices connected to the switch's front panel ports within the same VLAN. A VLAN ID. Each isolated network virtual interface must be ass ociated with unique VLAN ID, and that VLAN must already exist on the switch. Syntax: virtual-interface vlan <1-4094>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the virtual-interface vlan <1-4094> command?
The syntax of the virtual-interface vlan <1-4094> command is: virtual-interface vlan <1-4094>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, how do you configure CoPP?

To configure CoPP, follow the documented procedure: 1. Configure the default CoPP policy, edit an existing policy, or create a policy with the command copp-p 2. Add, edit, or remove classes in the policy with the command class 3. Review the CoPP policy configuration settings with the command show copp-policy.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the apply copp-policy command do?

The apply copp-policy command applies a CoPP policy to the switch, replacing the policy that is in effect. There may be a brief interruption in traffic flow to the management processor while the switch implements the change. Enter the no apply copp-policy <NAME> command with the name of a CoPP policy to unapply a CoPP policy and apply the default CoPP policy. This will only take effect if the specified policy is actively applied. Since there must always be a CoPP policy applied, this command effectively attempts to replace the applied CoPP policy with the default CoPP policy. The default CoPP policy cannot be unapplied using this command. Specifies the name of the policy to apply. Length: 1 to 64 chara cters. <NAME> default. Syntax: apply copp-policy { <NAME> | default }.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the apply copp-policy command?
The syntax of the apply copp-policy command is: apply copp-policy { <NAME> | default }.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the class command?
The syntax of the class command is: class <CLASS> {drop | priority <PRIORITY> rate <RATE> [burst <BURST>]}.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the clear copp-policy statistics command do?
The clear copp-policy statistics command resets statistics for all CoPP classes to zero. Syntax: clear copp-policy statistics.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the clear copp-policy statistics command?
The syntax of the clear copp-policy statistics command is: clear copp-policy statistics.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the copp-policy {<NAME> | default [revert]} command?
The syntax of the copp-policy {<NAME> | default [revert]} command is: copp-policy {<NAME> | default [revert]}.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show tech copp command do?
The show tech copp command displays the output of all show commands supported by CoPP. Syntax: show tech copp.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show tech copp command?
The syntax of the show tech copp command is: show tech copp.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the diag event-trap-disable command do?

The diag event-trap-disable command prevents the specific event notifications from being sent as traps to the SNMP management stations. The no form of this command disables this feature and reenables the notification of events to be sent as traps. Specify up to five comma separated Event IDs To modify the traps to be disabled, such as changing the existing list of event IDs or removing an event ID from the blocked trap list, first completely remove previously configured disabled traps using the CLI command no diag event-trap-disable <EVENT-ID>. After that, reconfigure the new event IDs or set of event IDs for the changes to take effect. The command to disable traps cannot be issued via network management systems such as SNMP, the REST API, HPE Aruba Networking Fabric Composer or HPE Aruba Networking Central. Syntax: diag event-trap-disable <EVENT-IDS>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the diag event-trap-disable command?
The syntax of the diag event-trap-disable command is: diag event-trap-disable <EVENT-IDS>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show debug command do?

The show debug command displays the enabled debug types. Shows the output from the VSX peer switch. If the switches do not have the VSX configuration or the ISL is down, the output f rom the VSX peer switch is not displayed. This parameter is avai lable on switches that support VSX. Syntax: show debug [vsx-peer].
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show debug command?
The syntax of the show debug command is: show debug [vsx-peer]
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show debug destination command do?

The show debug destination command displays the configured debug destination and severity. Shows the output from the VSX peer switch. If the switches do not have the VSX configuration or the ISL is down, the output f rom the VSX peer switch is not displayed. This parameter is avai lable on switches that support VSX. Syntax: show debug destination [vsx-peer].
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show debug destination command?
The syntax of the show debug destination command is: show debug destination [vsx-peer]
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the guide say about IPv6 Example?

IPv6 Example: switch(config)# logrotate target tftp://2001: db8: 0: 1: : 128 Public Resetting the time frequency to daily 32 I d e n t i f y i n g a r e m o t e h o s t f o r r e c e i v i n g r o t a t e d l o... Remote transfer of rotated log files Only the TFTP protocol is supported for remote transfer, and both IPv4 and IPv6 addresses are supported. Only newly rotated log files are transferred to the remote host during the log rotation. Previously rotated log files are not transferred. After a file is successfully transferred, it is removed from the switch local path. Packet level failures with TFTP are handled in the protocol itself. With each TFTP session failure, TFTP retries the file transfer three times. Retries have a timeout of five seconds.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the logrotate target <URI> [vrf <VRF_NAME>] command do?

The logrotate target <URI> [vrf <VRF_NAME>] command using TFTP, sends the rotated log files to a specified remote host identified by Universal Resource Identifier (URI). The no form of this command resets the target to the default, which stores the rotated and compressed log files locally in /var/log/. Specifies the URI of the remote host. The default directory is l ocal. tftp://{{<IPV4_ADDR>|IPV6_ADDR>}|HOST}[/ <DIRECTORY>] <VRF_NAME> Specifies the VRF name (Default: default). Syntax: logrotate target <URI> [vrf <VRF_NAME>].
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the logrotate target <URI> [vrf <VRF_NAME>] command?
The syntax of the logrotate target <URI> [vrf <VRF_NAME>] command is: logrotate target <URI> [vrf <VRF_NAME>]
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the guide say about Redundant Management communication?

Redundant Management communication: timeout The standby management module has taken over from an un responsive active management module. Redundant Management election timeout A failure to elect a standby management module in the alloted t ime. Critical service fault (error) A daemon critical to switch operation has stopped functioning. An extra error string may be present to describe the error in d etail.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the guide say about VSX software update?

VSX software update: Reset triggered by a VSX software update. Chassis critical temperature Chassis operating temperature exceeded. Chassis insufficient fans Insufficient fans to cool the chassis. Chassis unsupported PSUs/fans Unsupported or misconfigured PSUs or system fans. Management module critical temperature Management module operating temperature exceeded. Uncontrolled reboots • ops-switchd crashed • ovsdb-server crashed • Reset ◦ Software thermal reset ◦ Power on reset ◦ Watchdog reset ◦ CPU request reset ◦ cold reset ◦ Long press reset Public Reboot reasons 44 ◦ Jumper reset NOTE The resets are not applicable for 8320, 8325, and 9300/9300S Switch series. • switchd_agent crashed Event Logs Event logging logs events generated by daemons, processes, and plug-ins running within the switch software. The event logging framework captures the event logs in a system journal by updating the journal fields and meta data. Subtopics Showing and clearing events Showing and clearing events The clear events command is used to clear the event log of all events.

section: Validates VSX configuration synchronization between VSX pee

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the guide say about Validates VSX configuration synchronization between VSX pee?

Validates VSX configuration synchronization between VSX pee: rs for VSX enabled features. Public Network configuration validation commands 47 Parameter format Examples Description Specifies the results display format. The default is cli. Running configuration validation with switches for the vsx feature. switch (config)# switch config-validator config running-config feature vsx.

topic: Validates VSX configuration synchronization between VSX pee

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the split [<COUNT>][<SPEED>][confirm] command do?

The split [<COUNT>][<SPEED>][confirm] command splits a port into multiple interfaces. Only ports capable of supporting breakout cables or SR4/eSR4/eDR4 optics can be split. Specifies the number of child interfaces to activate upon splittin g the port. Default: 4. <COUNT> <SPEED> confirm 8360-12C switch Port info 25-26 (40G or 100G) 8360-24XF2C switch 25-26 (40G or 100G) 8360-24XF2C switch 9300-32D 32p 100/200/400G QSFP-DD p 10G SFP+ Switch Porte 1-32 are splittable. 400G SR8 can split two, four, or ei ght ways (8-way splitting availab le on the 9300-32D only). Some ports are splittable dependi ng on the interface profile use. See the Getting Started guide for mo re information. 9300S Switch Series • S0F95A • S0F96A 9300S 32P QSFP28 100G 8p QSF P-DD 400G TAA Switch 9-12. 17-24, and 29-32 por ts are splittable. Syntax: split [<COUNT>][<SPEED>][confirm].
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the split [<COUNT>][<SPEED>][confirm] command?
The syntax of the split [<COUNT>][<SPEED>][confirm] command is: split [<COUNT>][<SPEED>][confirm]
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the diag cable-diagnostic command do?

The diag cable-diagnostic command provides information about the cable health after running a diagnostic test on an interface. If you run a new cable diagnostic command when a cable diagnostic is in progress for the interface, the new cable diagnostic command fails to execute. In such a scenario, an error message is displayed. On executing a cable diagnostic test command, it automatically clears the old test results before the new test starts. Specifies the name of the interface. <IF-NAME> test <IF-NAME> Runs a cable diagnostic test on an interface. show <IF-NAME> clear <IF-NAME>. Syntax: diag cable-diagnostic.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the diag cable-diagnostic command?
The syntax of the diag cable-diagnostic command is: diag cable-diagnostic.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the copy checkpoint command do?

The copy checkpoint command copies the checkpoint using TFTP, SFTP, SCP, or USB. Specifies the checkpoint name. <CHECKPOINT-NAME> {<STORAGE-URL> | <REMOTE- URL>} Select either the storage URL or the remote URL for the destin ation of the copied command output. Required. <STORAGE-URL> <REMOTE-URL> Specifies the USB to copy command output. • {sftp:// | scp:// <USER> @}{ <IP> | <HOST> }[: <PORT> ]/ <FILE>. Syntax: copy checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> {<STORAGE-URL> | <REMOTE-URL>}.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the copy checkpoint command?
The syntax of the copy checkpoint command is: copy checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> {<STORAGE-URL> | <REMOTE-URL>}.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the copy core-dump dsm command do?

The copy core-dump dsm command copies the core-dump from the selected distributed-services-module using TFTP, SFTP, SCP, USB, or local. Slot ID of the distributed services module Specifies the name of the daemon. Required. [: <INSTANCE-ID>] Specifies the instance of the daemon core dump. Optional. <REMOTE_URL>. Syntax: copy core-dump dsm <slot-id> <daemon-name>[: <instance-ID>].
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the copy core-dump dsm command?
The syntax of the copy core-dump dsm command is: copy core-dump dsm <slot-id> <daemon-name>[: <instance-ID>]
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the copy core-dump kernel <STORAGE-URL> 69 command do?

The copy core-dump kernel <STORAGE-URL> 69 command copies the kernel core dump to a USB drive or local. <STORAGE-URL> Specifies the USB to copy command output. Required. Syntax: copy core-dump kernel <STORAGE-URL>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the copy core-dump kernel <STORAGE-URL> 69 command?
The syntax of the copy core-dump kernel <STORAGE-URL> 69 command is: copy core-dump kernel <STORAGE-URL>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the copy <IMAGE> command do?

The copy <IMAGE> command copies the image using TFTP, SFTP, SCP, or USB. Specifies the image. {<STORAGE-URL> | <REMOTE- URL>} Select either the storage URL or the remote URL for the destin ation of the copied command output. Required. Specifies the USB to copy command output. {usb}: / <FILE> <REMOTE-URL> <FILE-NAME>. Syntax: copy <IMAGE> {<STORAGE-URL> | <REMOTE-URL>} <FILE-NAME> [vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the copy <IMAGE> command?
The syntax of the copy <IMAGE> command is: copy <IMAGE> {<STORAGE-URL> | <REMOTE-URL>} <FILE-NAME> [vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the guide say about 1/1 Ingress Global Policy Lookup?

1/1 Ingress Global Policy Lookup: Ingress TCAM Entries 35 2048 Total Ingress TCAM Entries 35 2048 18432 Egress TCAM Entries 0 0 8192 Ingress Lookups 1 8 Ingress Flex Lookups 0 1 Egress Lookups 0 4 Ingress Policers 0 2047 Egress Policers 0 2047 Hardware resources will be consumed on each module that has a policy applied to an interface. Ingress and egress policies consume separate hardware resources. The following example shows the output of a show resources command issued on a 6300 switch with a policy applied to a physical interface in both in and out directions: 6300(config)# show resources Resource Usage: Mod Description Resource Used Reserved Free ---------------------------------------------------------------------------- --.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the guide say about VLAN: 1?

VLAN: 1: VRF: default Source IP: 101.0.0.2 Destination IP: 201.0.0.2 L2 Warning: Port is routing enabled. Please use the 'show forwarding-info ip' command. Egress interface: ECMP 1/1/17 Showing forwarding information with ROP and SVI: Public show forwarding-info 122 interface 1/1/4 no routing no shutdown vlan trunk native 1 vlan trunk allowed all interface 1/1/6 no shutdown ip address 10.10.10.2/24show ip route Displaying IPv4 routes selected for forwarding Origin Codes: C - connected, S - static, L - local R - RIP, B - BGP, O - OSPF, D - DHCP Type Codes: E - External BGP, I - Internal BGP, V - VPN, EV - EVPN IA - OSPF internal area, E1 - OSPF external type 1 E2 - OSPF external type 2 VRF: default.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the guide say about L2 Interface?

L2 Interface: Destination MAC 72.1.1.1 lag72 lag72->1/1/3 b8d4e7: de: f1: 00 Leaf2# Sample Show command - Bridging v6 Showing command output - Bridging v6 Leaf2# show forwarding-info mac-ip ingress-interface 1/1/11 source-mac-address 00: 12: 01: 00: 00: 01 destination-mac-address 00: 13: 01: 00: 00: 01 source-IPv6-address 20: 1: : 21 destination-IPv6-address 20: 1: : 31 ethertype 0x86dd vlan 1001 vrf VRF_1 Ingress-interface: 1/1/11 Source mac-address: 00: 12: 01: 00: 00: 01 Destination mac-address: 00: 13: 01: 00: 00: 01.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the guide say about reboot - Reboots the Management Module?

reboot - Reboots the Management Module: password - Set the admin account password secure-mode - Sets or retrieves the secure mode setting sh - Launch support shell umount - Unmounts a storage device update - Update a product image version - Prints ServiceOS release version information cat - Prints files to stdout cp - Copy files and directories du - Estimate file space usage ls - List directory contents md5sum - Compute and check md5 message digest mkdir - Make directories mv - Move (rename) files rm - Remove files or directories Public Service OS CLI command list 155 rmdir - Remove empty directories tftp - Allows transfer of files to/from a remote machine exit - Logout Enter '<command> --help' for more info Service OS CLI features and limitations The Service OS CLI provides basic shell functionality that allows you to execute commands and pass arguments to those commands only.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the boot command do?
The boot command presents you with the boot menu prompt. You can then specify which boot profile: primary, secondary, or Service OS console. Syntax: boot.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the boot command?
The syntax of the boot command is: boot.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the cat <FILENAME/DIRECTORY-NAME> command do?

The cat <FILENAME/DIRECTORY-NAME> command prints the contents of a file to the console. The Service OS does not allow command output redirection, so this command is only useful for reading short text files. Shows the contents of the specified file or directory. <FILENAME/DIRECTORY-NAME> Public cat 158. Syntax: cat <FILENAME/DIRECTORY-NAME>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the cat <FILENAME/DIRECTORY-NAME> command?
The syntax of the cat <FILENAME/DIRECTORY-NAME> command is: cat <FILENAME/DIRECTORY-NAME>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the cd path command do?
The cd path command changes the current working directory. Syntax: cd path.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the cd path command?
The syntax of the cd path command is: cd path.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the cp [options] <SOURCE-FILENAME/SOURCE-DIRECTORY> command do?

The cp [options] <SOURCE-FILENAME/SOURCE-DIRECTORY> command copies files or directories. Selects the options for the command. Specifies the preservation of symlinks (default if -R). Same as -dpR. Specifies recursiveness, all files, and subdirectories are copied. Specifies the following of all symlinks. Specifies the following of symlinks on command line. Specifies the preservation of file attributes if possible. Public cp 161 Specifies the overwriting of a file or directory. Specifies the prompting before an overwrite. Specifies the creation of (sym) links. Specifies the name of the source file or directory. Specifies the name of the destination file or directory. Syntax: cp [options] <SOURCE-FILENAME/SOURCE-DIRECTORY>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the cp [options] <SOURCE-FILENAME/SOURCE-DIRECTORY> command?
The syntax of the cp [options] <SOURCE-FILENAME/SOURCE-DIRECTORY> command is: cp [options] <SOURCE-FILENAME/SOURCE-DIRECTORY>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the erase zeroize command do?

The erase zeroize command securely erases any user data contained on the SSD or other storage devices on the management module. NOTE Back up all data before running this command or all user/config data will be lost. Syntax: erase zeroize.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the erase zeroize command?
The syntax of the erase zeroize command is: erase zeroize.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the format command do?

The format command configures the primary storage device with the correct partition and file system formatting. This command removes all pre-existing data on the primary storage device. Syntax: format.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the format command?
The syntax of the format command is: format.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the identify command do?
The identify command prints the version of the SVOS and of the UEFI BIOS. Syntax: identify.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the identify command?
The syntax of the identify command is: identify.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ip command do?

The ip command shows or configures the port with a static IP address (IPv4 only) or enables the DHCP client on the port. An address is set only if a DHCP server is available to provide one. {show | dhcp | disable | addr <ADDR- NETMASK-GATEWAY>} show dhcp Selects the options for the OOBM port. Shows the OOBM port. Configures the port with a DHCP address. disable Disables the OOBM port. Public ip 169 addr <ADDR-NETMASK- GATEWAY> Configures the port with a static IP address (IPv4 only). Specify address, netmask, and gateway as A. B. C. D. Syntax: ip {show | dhcp | disable | addr <ADDR-NETMASK-GATEWAY>}.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the ip command?
The syntax of the ip command is: ip {show | dhcp | disable | addr <ADDR-NETMASK-GATEWAY>}.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ls command do?

The ls command this command lists directory contents. | Parameter | | Description | Specifies options for the command. <OPTIONS> Shows one-column output. -1 | -a | | Shows entries which start with a period (.). | Shows output similar to -a, but excludes a period (.) and a dou -A ble period (..). | -C | | Shows output list by columns. | Shows output list by lines. -x | -d | | Shows listing of directory entries instead of contents | Follows symlinks. -L | -H | | Follows symlinks on the command line. | Recurse. -R | -p | | Appends a slash (/) to directory entries. | Appends an indicator to entries. An indicator can be as an aster -F isk (*) or slash (/) or equal sign (=) or at sign (@) or pipe (|). Public ls 171 -l -i -n -s -e -h -r -S -X -v -c -t -u -c Shows the output in a long listing format. Syntax: ls [<OPTIONS>] [<FILE-NME>].
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the ls command?
The syntax of the ls command is: ls [<OPTIONS>] [<FILE-NME>]
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the md5sum [-c | -s | -w] [<FILE-NAME>] command do?

The md5sum [-c | -s | -w] [<FILE-NAME>] command this command computes and checks the MD5 message digest. Selects the options for the command. -c -s -w Specifies to check the sums against the list in files. Specifies not output anything, status code shows success. Specifies to warn about improperly formatted checksum lines. Specifies the file name to run the checksum against. Public md5sum 173. Syntax: md5sum [-c | -s | -w] [<FILE-NAME>].
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the md5sum [-c | -s | -w] [<FILE-NAME>] command?
The syntax of the md5sum [-c | -s | -w] [<FILE-NAME>] command is: md5sum [-c | -s | -w] [<FILE-NAME>]
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the mkdir [-m | -p] [<DIRECTORY-NAME>] command do?

The mkdir [-m | -p] [<DIRECTORY-NAME>] command this command makes directories. Specifies the options for the command. Specifies the mode. Public mkdir 174 Specifies to make parent directories as needed with no errors fo r pre-existing directories. Specifies the directory to create. Syntax: mkdir [-m | -p] [<DIRECTORY-NAME>].
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the mkdir [-m | -p] [<DIRECTORY-NAME>] command?
The syntax of the mkdir [-m | -p] [<DIRECTORY-NAME>] command is: mkdir [-m | -p] [<DIRECTORY-NAME>]
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the mount <DEVICE> command do?

The mount <DEVICE> command this command mounts the SSD partitions to the following locations: /coredump, /logs, /nos, /selftest, and mounts the USB device to /mnt/usb. Users can mount USB flash drives formatted as either FAT16 or FAT32 with a single partition. Public mount 175 Specifies the device to be mounted. Supported device options i nclude all and usb. <DEVICE>. Syntax: mount <DEVICE>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the mount <DEVICE> command?
The syntax of the mount <DEVICE> command is: mount <DEVICE>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the mv [-f | -i | -n] <TARGET-DIRECTORY> command do?

The mv [-f | -i | -n] <TARGET-DIRECTORY> command this command moves (renames) files. Public mv 176 -f -i -
- Specifies not to prompt before overwriting. Specifies to prompt before overwriting. Specifies to not overwrite an existing file. Syntax: mv [-f | -i | -n] <TARGET-DIRECTORY>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the mv [-f | -i | -n] <TARGET-DIRECTORY> command?
The syntax of the mv [-f | -i | -n] <TARGET-DIRECTORY> command is: mv [-f | -i | -n] <TARGET-DIRECTORY>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the password command do?

The password command sets the admin user account password for both Service OS and AOS-CX once the user boots into AOS-CX and saves the configuration. This will overwrite the previous password if one exists. User input is masked with asterisks. This command is not available if enhanced secure mode is set. Public password (svos) 177. Syntax: password.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the password command?
The syntax of the password command is: password.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the pwd command do?
The pwd command displays the current working directory. Syntax: pwd.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the pwd command?
The syntax of the pwd command is: pwd.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the reboot command do?
The reboot command reboots the Management Module. Public pwd 180. Syntax: reboot.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the reboot command?
The syntax of the reboot command is: reboot.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the rm command do?

The rm command removes files or directories. | Parameter | | Description | [-f | -i | -R | -r] Selects the options for removing files or directories. | -f | | Never prompt before removing files or directories. | | -i | | Always prompt before removing files or directories. | | -R | -r | | Recursive. | Public rm 181. Syntax: rm [-f | -i | -R | -r] <FILE-NAME>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the rm command?
The syntax of the rm command is: rm [-f | -i | -R | -r] <FILE-NAME>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the rmdir command do?
The rmdir command removes empty directories. Specifies to remove parent directories. Public rmdir 182. Syntax: rmdir [-p] <DIRECTORY-NAME>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the rmdir command?
The syntax of the rmdir command is: rmdir [-p] <DIRECTORY-NAME>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the sh command do?
The sh command launches a bash shell for support purposes. To quit bash, enter exit. This command is not available if enhanced secure mode is set. Syntax: sh.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the sh command?
The syntax of the sh command is: sh.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the system serviceos password-prompt command do?

The system serviceos password-prompt command use this command to enable password authentication for ServiceOS. By default, the ServiceOS shell (accessible only from the local switch console port) requires no password to login as an admin use. When this setting is enabled, the same password used to authenticate the admin user in the AOS-CX CLI or WebUI can be used to log in to the ServiceOS shell. If this setting is enabled, a forgotten admin user password cannot be reset using ServiceOS; if there are no other local or RADIUS/TACACS user accounts with administrator-level access, the switch must be zeroized by entering the username zeroize command at the ServiceOS login prompt to restore administrator access. serviceos password-prompt cannot be enabled if the built-in admin user has been disabled. Syntax: system serviceos password-prompt.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the system serviceos password-prompt command?
The syntax of the system serviceos password-prompt command is: system serviceos password-prompt.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the umount <DEVICE> command do?

The umount <DEVICE> command unmounts the SSD partitions mounted to the following locations: /coredump, /logs, /nos, /selftest, and unmounts the USB device mounted to /mnt/usb. Specifies the device to be unmounted. Supported device option s include all and usb. <DEVICE>. Syntax: umount <DEVICE>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the umount <DEVICE> command?
The syntax of the umount <DEVICE> command is: umount <DEVICE>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the update {primary | secondary} <IMAGE> command do?

The update {primary | secondary} <IMAGE> command verifies and installs a product image. The user can select the primary or secondary boot profile to update and the location of the file. {primary | secondary} Selects either the primary or secondary image. Specifies the image name. <IMAGE>. Syntax: update {primary | secondary} <IMAGE>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the update {primary | secondary} <IMAGE> command?
The syntax of the update {primary | secondary} <IMAGE> command is: update {primary | secondary} <IMAGE>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the tftp {-b | -g | -l <LOCAL-FILE> | -p | -r <REMOTE-FILE>} host [<PORT>] command do?

The tftp {-b | -g | - - <LOCAL-FILE> | -p | -r <REMOTE-FILE>} host [<PORT>] command transfers files to and from a remote machine (TFTP a file). Public tftp 189 {-b | -g | -l | -p | -r <REMOTE-FILE>} Selects the options for transferring a file. -b -g -l -p Specifies the transfer blocks of size octets. The default blocksi ze is set to 1468, which can be overridden with the -b option. Specifies to get a file. Specifies a local file. Specifies to put a file in remote location. -r <REMOTE-FILE> Specifies a remote file. Specifies the port for transfer. If no port option is specified, TFT P uses the standard UDP port 69 by default. <PORT>. Syntax: tftp {-b | -g | - - <LOCAL-FILE> | -p | -r <REMOTE-FILE>} host [<PORT>].
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the tftp {-b | -g | -l <LOCAL-FILE> | -p | -r <REMOTE-FILE>} host [<PORT>] command?
The syntax of the tftp {-b | -g | - - <LOCAL-FILE> | -p | -r <REMOTE-FILE>} host [<PORT>] command is: tftp {-b | -g | - - <LOCAL-FILE> | -p | -r <REMOTE-FILE>} host [<PORT>]
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the version command do?
The version command displays the following build strings: • Version. • Build date. • Build time. • Build ID. • SHA. Syntax: version.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the version command?
The syntax of the version command is: version.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show arp vlan command do?

The show arp vlan command displays IPv4 neighbors learnt on L2 VLAN interfaces only when arp-suppression-extended is enabled. Specifies the VLAN ID. Shows information for all VLANs. If global arp-suppression-extended is enabled and per-VLAN arp-suppression-extended is disabled, neighbors learnt on that VLAN are not displayed. If global arp-suppression-extended is disabled and per-VLAN arp-suppression-extended is enabled, neighbors learnt on that VLAN are displayed. Syntax: show arp vlan {VLAN-ID|all}.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show arp vlan command?
The syntax of the show arp vlan command is: show arp vlan {VLAN-ID|all}.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show evpn ethernet-segment command do?

The show evpn ethernet-segment command shows information about EVPN ethernet segments. Ethernet Segment Identifier. Syntax: 00XX. XXXX. XX XX. XXXX. XXXX Public show evpn ethernet-segment 181. Syntax: show evpn ethernet-segment {ESI}.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show evpn ethernet-segment command?
The syntax of the show evpn ethernet-segment command is: show evpn ethernet-segment {ESI}.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show evpn evi summary command do?
The show evpn evi summary command shows the summary information for EVPN instances. Syntax: show evpn evi summary.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show evpn evi summary command?
The syntax of the show evpn evi summary command is: show evpn evi summary.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show evpn evi <EVI-ID> 188 command do?
The show evpn evi <EVI-ID> 188 command shows the information for the particular EVPN instance. Specifies the EVPN instance ID. <EVI-ID>. Syntax: show evpn evi <EVI-ID>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show evpn evi <EVI-ID> 188 command?
The syntax of the show evpn evi <EVI-ID> 188 command is: show evpn evi <EVI-ID>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show evpn evi detail command do?
The show evpn evi detail command shows the detailed information for all EVPN instances. Syntax: show evpn evi detail.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show evpn evi detail command?
The syntax of the show evpn evi detail command is: show evpn evi detail.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show evpn evi <EVI-ID> detail command do?
The show evpn evi <EVI-ID> detail command shows the detailed information for the particular EVPN instance. Specifies the EVPN instance ID. <EVI-ID>. Syntax: show evpn evi <EVI-ID> detail.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show evpn evi <EVI-ID> detail command?
The syntax of the show evpn evi <EVI-ID> detail command is: show evpn evi <EVI-ID> detail.

The show evpn evi igmp snooping command shows the information about the IGMP group memberships learned through EVPN. A default SMET route is identified by the Source and Group addresses set to 0.0.0.0 and is applicable for both IP versions. Specifies the EVPN instance ID. Range: 1-16777214. <EVI-ID>. Syntax: show evpn evi <EVI-ID> igmp snooping.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show evpn evi igmp snooping command?
The syntax of the show evpn evi igmp snooping command is: show evpn evi <EVI-ID> igmp snooping.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show evpn mac-ip command do?
The show evpn mac-ip command show the information about the EVPN MAC-IP for the EVPN instances. Syntax: show evpn mac-ip.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show evpn mac-ip command?
The syntax of the show evpn mac-ip command is: show evpn mac-ip.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show evpn mac-ip evi command do?
The show evpn mac-ip evi command show the information about the EVPN MAC-IP for the particular EVPN instance. Specifies the EVPN instance ID. <EVI-ID>. Syntax: show evpn mac-ip evi <EVI-ID>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show evpn mac-ip evi command?
The syntax of the show evpn mac-ip evi command is: show evpn mac-ip evi <EVI-ID>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show evpn evi mld snooping command do?

The show evpn evi mld snooping command shows the information about the MLD group memberships learned through EVPN. A default SMET route is identified by the Source and Group addresses set to 0: : 0 and is applicable for both IP versions. Specifies the EVPN instance ID. Range: 1-16777214. Public show evpn evi mld snooping 200. Syntax: show evpn evi <EVI-ID> mld snooping.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show evpn evi mld snooping command?
The syntax of the show evpn evi mld snooping command is: show evpn evi <EVI-ID> mld snooping.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show evpn vtep-neighbor command do?

The show evpn vtep-neighbor command shows the remote VTEPs MAC-IP binding. The state of the peer VTEP denotes whether VXLAN tunnel to the VTEP is Up or Down. Public show evpn vtep-neighbor 202 Display information for all VRFs. Specify a VRF by VRF name (if no <VRF-NAME> is specifie d, the default VRF is implied. Syntax: show evpn vtep-neighbor {all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-Name>}.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show evpn vtep-neighbor command?
The syntax of the show evpn vtep-neighbor command is: show evpn vtep-neighbor {all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-Name>}.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the clear dsm statistics command do?

The clear dsm statistics command clears the statistics of the interfaces attached to the distributed services module. Shows the slot ID of the DSM. <SLOT-ID> global Clears the DSM interface statistics for all sessions. Public DSM commands 757. Syntax: clear dsm <SLOT-ID> statistics [global].
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the clear dsm statistics command?
The syntax of the clear dsm statistics command is: clear dsm <SLOT-ID> statistics [global]
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the diag dsm console command do?

The diag dsm console command provides access to the operator console of the attached distributed services module. From this console, operator commands can be run. Exiting the console returns the user back to CLI. This is intended for diagnostic purposes only. Public diag dsm console 758 Shows the slot ID of the DSM. <SLOT-ID>. Syntax: diag dsm console <SLOT-ID>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the diag dsm console command?
The syntax of the diag dsm console command is: diag dsm console <SLOT-ID>

The syntax of the show dsm command is: show dsm <SLOT-ID>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show dsm erspan command do?
The show dsm erspan command displays the status of the Encapsulated Remote Switched Port Analyzer (ERSPAN) feature for DSM. Public show dsm erspan 765. Syntax: show dsm erspan.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show dsm erspan command?
The syntax of the show dsm erspan command is: show dsm erspan.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the interface vxlan command do?

The interface vxlan command creates VXLAN interface 1 and changes to the config-vxlan-if context. A maximum of one VXLAN interface is supported. By default, the VXLAN is disabled. To enable the VXLAN, use the command no shutdown. The no form of this command removes VXLAN interface 1. This deletes the VXLAN tunnel, and all VNIs and VLAN-to-VNI mappings associated with it. Only one VXLAN interface is supported. (Optional) Specify if the interface tunnel uses IPv4 or IPv6 add ressing. If this parameter is not included, the interface default is IPv6. Changes the inter-VXLAN bridging mode on tunnels where VXLAN bridging across tunnels is disabled by default. Default mode is deny. The no form of this command sets the inter-VXLAN bridging mode to the default mode of deny. Syntax: interface vxlan 1 [mode IPv4|IPv6].
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the interface vxlan command?
The syntax of the interface vxlan command is: interface vxlan 1 [mode IPv4|IPv6]
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ip-fragment-bypass command do?

The ip-fragment-bypass command this command toggles IP fragment bypass functionality for a VLAN. When enabled, IP fragment packets that are normally redirected to the Distributed Services Module, are not redirected. This is configured on a per-VLAN basis. Syntax: ip-fragment-bypass.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the ip-fragment-bypass command?
The syntax of the ip-fragment-bypass command is: ip-fragment-bypass.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show interface vxlan command?
The syntax of the show interface vxlan command is: show interface vxlan <IFNAME>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show interface vxlan vni command do?

The show interface vxlan vni command shows detailed VNI information for the VXLAN interface. Specifies the number for a given VNI. Range: 1 to 16777214. Shows the VTEPS information for a specified VNI. transport-VRFshows the transport VRFstatus for a specified VNI. Syntax: show interface vxlan vni <VNI-ID> [vteps | transport-vrf].
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show interface vxlan vni command?
The syntax of the show interface vxlan vni command is: show interface vxlan vni <VNI-ID> [vteps | transport-vrf]
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show vlan ip-fragment-bypass command do?

The show vlan ip-fragment-bypass command this command displays the status of VLANs with IP fragment bypass enabled. Disables the VXLAN interface and deletes all VXLAN tunnels, segments, and members on the interface. Public shutdown 816 The no form of this command starts the VXLAN interface and creates all VXLAN tunnels and segments. If members are configured, they are added to the VXLAN segment. Configures the source IPv4 address for a VXLAN interface. All VXLAN encapsulated packets use this source IP address in the outer IP header. If you change an existing source IP address, all tunnels with the old source IP address are deleted, and new tunnels are created with the new source IP address. Syntax: show vlan ip-fragment-bypass.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show vlan ip-fragment-bypass command?
The syntax of the show vlan ip-fragment-bypass command is: show vlan ip-fragment-bypass.
