messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the guide say about Security - PKI?

Security - PKI: Enables you to configure the switch to distribute fir mware to other switches. Enables you to run the ping command with variou s options. Enables you to run the traceroute command w ith various options. Enables you to run the showtech command. Ad ministrator rights are required to run this command. Shows the details of the spanning tree configuration, assigned ports, and inconsistent ports. Allows you to enable Spanning Tree with Multiple Spanning Tree or Rapid Per-VLANspanning Tree mode. Shows the details of the devices connected to the sw itch. Shows the details of the TA profile, EST profile, digi tal certificates, and associated applications in the switch. Allows you to add, edit, and delete TA profile; ad d, view, and delete EST profile; add, view, upload, and delete certificates; and edit associated applications.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the diag ospfv3 lsdb dump command do?

The diag ospfv3 lsdb dump command this command dumps the OSPFv3 AF protocol LSA information. Public diag ospfv3 lsdb dump 443 Disables the OSPFv3 AF process. By default OSPFv3 AF process is enabled. This command does not remove the OSPFv3 AF configurations. Defines an administrative distance for OSPFv3 AF. Administrative distance is used as a criteria to select the best route when the same route is learned by multiple routing protocols. The no form of this command sets the OSPFv3 AF administrative distance to the default value of 110. Optionally, administrative distance can be set to default for the specific OSPF route type: intra-area, inter-area, or external type-5 and type-7 routes. Specifies the OSPFv3 AF administrative distance. Range: 1 to 2 55. Default: 110. Specifies the OSPFv3 AF distance for intra-area routes. Syntax: diag ospfv3 lsdb dump.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the diag ospfv3 lsdb dump command?
The syntax of the diag ospfv3 lsdb dump command is: diag ospfv3 lsdb dump.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the router-id command do?

The router-id command sets an ID for the router in an IPv4 address format. <ROUTER-ADDRESS> Required Specifies the router address in IPv4 format (x.x.x.x), where x is a decimal number from 0 to 255. no Unconfigures the router-id for the instance. The Router-id is changed to the dynamically selected router-id. The default ro uter-id 0.0.0.0 is updated to the routing stack that triggers to auto-elect a router-id based on the highest ip-address of l oopback interface or highest ip-address of interfaces. The co nfiguration is supported globally as well as per address-famili es IPv4-unicast, IPv6-unicast. If address-family specific rou ter-id is configured, it takes precedence over global configurat ion. Syntax: router-id <ROUTER-ADDRESS>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the router-id command?
The syntax of the router-id command is: router-id <ROUTER-ADDRESS>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the router ospfv3-af command do?

The router ospfv3-af command creates the OSPFv3 AF process (if not created already) and enters the router OSPFv3 AF instance context. Optionally if specified, you can specify a named VRF, or the default VRF if the <vrf-name> is not specified. Maximum of eight OSPFv3 processes are allowed per VRF. The switch allows configuration of OSPFv2, OSPFv3, OSPFv3 IPv4 AF and OSPFv3 IPv6 AF. But at any point only 8 OSPF instances can run per address-family, 8 instances for IPv4 (OSPFv2 + OSPFv3 IPv4 AF) and 8 instances for IPv6 (OSPFv3 + OSPFv3 IPv6 AF). The no form of this command removes the OSPFv3 AF instance. If a VRF is specified, it removes the OSPF instance from the named VRF, or the default VRF if the <var-name> is not specified. Specifies an OSPFv3 AF process ID. Length: 1 to 65535. Syntax: router ospfv3-af <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the router ospfv3-af command?
The syntax of the router ospfv3-af command is: router ospfv3-af <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show ospfv3-af IPv4 statistics command do?

The show ospfv3-af IPv4 statistics command this command displays the OSPFv3 AF shortest path first (SPF) calculation statistics. Public show ospfv3-af IPv4 statistics 521 Specifies an OSPFv3 AF process ID. Range: 1 to 65535 <PROCESS-ID> all-vrfs Display the OSPFv3 AF statistics information for all the VRFs. vrf <VRF-NAME> Specify the VRF name. Syntax: show ospfv3-af [<process-id>] IPv4 statistics [all-vrfs | vrf <vrf-name>].
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show ospfv3-af IPv4 statistics command?
The syntax of the show ospfv3-af IPv4 statistics command is: show ospfv3-af [<process-id>] IPv4 statistics [all-vrfs | vrf <vrf-name>]
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show ospfv3-af IPv4 command?
The syntax of the show ospfv3-af IPv4 command is: show ospfv3-af [<PROCESS-ID>] IPv4 [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show ospfv3-af IPv4 interface command?
The syntax of the show ospfv3-af IPv4 interface command is: show ospfv3-af [<PROCESS-ID>] IPv4 interface [<INTERFACE-NAME>] | vlan.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show ospfv3-af IPv4 neighbors command do?

The show ospfv3-af IPv4 neighbors command shows information about OSPFv3 AF neighbors. Specifies an OSPFv3 AF process ID to show OSPFv3 AF neigh bor information for the particular OSPFv3 AF process. Range: 1 to 65535. Public show ospfv3-af IPv4 neighbors 545 neighbors <NEIGHBOR-ID> Shows information about a particular neighbor, specified in IP v 4 format (A. B. C. D). interface <INTERFACE-NAME> Shows neighbor information only for the specified interface. vlan <VLAN-ID> Specifies the vlan interface name. lag <LAG-ID> Specifies the lag interface name. loopback <LOOPBACK-ID> Specifies the loopback interface name. tunnel <TUNNEL-ID> Specifies the tunnel interface name. detail summary all-vrfs Shows detailed information for the neighbors. Shows summary information for the neighbors. Shows neighbor information for all VRFs. Syntax: show ospfv3-af [<PROCESS-ID>] IPv4 neighbors [<NEIGHBOR-ID>].
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show ospfv3-af IPv4 neighbors command?
The syntax of the show ospfv3-af IPv4 neighbors command is: show ospfv3-af [<PROCESS-ID>] IPv4 neighbors [<NEIGHBOR-ID>]
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show ospfv3-af IPv4 routes command do?

The show ospfv3-af IPv4 routes command shows the OSPFv3 AF routing table information. <PROCESS-ID> Specifies an OSPFv3 AF process ID that shows information fro m the OSPFv3 AF routing table for the particular OSPFv3 AF p rocess. Range: 1 to 65535. Public show ospfv3-af IPv4 routes 549 <PREFIX/LENGTH> all-vrfs Specifies an IP address in IPv4 format (x.x.x.x), where x is a de cimal number from 0 to 255. Select to show OSPFv3 AF routing table information for all VR Fs. vrf <VRF-NAME> Specifies the name of a VRF. Default: default. Syntax: show ospfv3-af [<PROCESS-ID>] IPv4 routes [<PREFIX/LENGTH>].
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show ospfv3-af IPv4 routes command?
The syntax of the show ospfv3-af IPv4 routes command is: show ospfv3-af [<PROCESS-ID>] IPv4 routes [<PREFIX/LENGTH>]
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show ospfv3-af IPv4 statistics interface command do?

The show ospfv3-af IPv4 statistics interface command shows information about OSPFv3 AF enabled IPv4 interfaces. <PROCESS-ID> <INTERFACE-NAME> Specifies an OSPFv3 AF process ID optionally to show the OSP Fv3 AF enabled interfaces for the particular OSPFv3 AF proces s. Range: 1 to 65535. Selects to show information only for the specified OSPFv3 AF e nabled interface. vlan <VLAN-ID> Specifies the vlan interface name. lag <LAG-ID> Specifies the lag interface name. loopback <LOOPBACK-ID> Specifies the loopback interface name. tunnel <TUNNEL-ID> Specifies the tunnel interface name. Public show ospfv3-af IPv4 statistics interface 552 Include this parameter to display a brief overview of the followi ng OSPFv3 AF configuration information. Interface: OSPFv3 AF interface name. • • Area: OSPFv3 AF area ID. Syntax: show ospfv3-af [<PROCESS-ID>] IPv4 statistics interface [<INTERFACE-NAME>].
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show ospfv3-af IPv4 statistics interface command?
The syntax of the show ospfv3-af IPv4 statistics interface command is: show ospfv3-af [<PROCESS-ID>] IPv4 statistics interface [<INTERFACE-NAME>]
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show ospfv3-af IPv6 command?
The syntax of the show ospfv3-af IPv6 command is: show ospfv3-af [<PROCESS-ID>] IPv6 [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show ospfv3-af IPv6 interface command?
The syntax of the show ospfv3-af IPv6 interface command is: show ospfv3-af [<PROCESS-ID>] IPv6 interface [<INTERFACE-NAME>] | vlan.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show ospfv3-af IPv6 lsdb command do?

The show ospfv3-af IPv6 lsdb command shows the OSPFv3 AF link state database summary for different OSPF LSAs (Link State Advertisement). Use the parameters to get information for a particular LSA. Enter an OSPFv3 AF process ID to display general OSPF inform ation for a particular OSPF process. Range: 1 to 65535. Public show ospfv3-af IPv6 lsdb 567 adv-router {<ROUTER-ID>| self} Select to display link states for a particular advertising router. S pecify either a Router ID of the advertising router or specify sel f to show self-originated link states. area <AREA-ID> Select to display information filtered for the specified area in on e of the following formats. • OSPFv3 AF area identifier in IPv4 format (x.x.x.x), where x is a decimal number from 0 to 255. • OSPFv3 AF area identifier in decimal format. Value: 0 to 42 94967295. Syntax: show ospfv3-af [<process-id>] IPv6 lsdb.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show ospfv3-af IPv6 lsdb command?
The syntax of the show ospfv3-af IPv6 lsdb command is: show ospfv3-af [<process-id>] IPv6 lsdb.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show ospfv3-af IPv6 routes command do?

The show ospfv3-af IPv6 routes command shows the OSPFv3 AF routing table information. <PROCESS-ID> Specifies an OSPFv3 AF process ID that shows information fro m the OSPFv3 AF routing table for the particular OSPFv3 AF p rocess. Range: 1 to 65535. Public show ospfv3-af IPv6 routes 578 Specifies the IPv6 destination prefix showing information about a particular destination prefix. For example, 2010: bd9: : /32. <PREFIX/LENGTH> all-vrfs Select to show OSPFv3 AF routing table information for all VR Fs. vrf <VRF-NAME> Specifies the name of a VRF. Default: default. Syntax: show ospfv3-af [<PROCESS-ID>] IPv6 routes [<PREFIX/LENGTH>].
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show ospfv3-af IPv6 routes command?
The syntax of the show ospfv3-af IPv6 routes command is: show ospfv3-af [<PROCESS-ID>] IPv6 routes [<PREFIX/LENGTH>]
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show ospfv3-af IPv6 statistics command do?

The show ospfv3-af IPv6 statistics command shows OSPFv3 AF statistics. <PROCESS-ID> all-vrfs Specifies an OSPFv3 AF process ID that shows information on t he OSPFv3 AF SPF statistics for the particular OSPFv3 AF proc ess. Range: 1 to 65535. Select to show OSPFv3 AF SPF statistics information for all VR Fs. vrf <VRF-NAME> Specifies the name of a VRF. Default: default. Syntax: show ospfv3-af IPv6 [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show ospfv3-af IPv6 statistics command?
The syntax of the show ospfv3-af IPv6 statistics command is: show ospfv3-af IPv6 [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show ospfv3-af IPv6 virtual-links command do?

The show ospfv3-af IPv6 virtual-links command displays the current state and parameters of the OSPFv3 AF virtual links. <PROCESS-ID> brief all-vrfs Enter an OSPFv3 AF process ID to display information on the O SPFv3 AF virtual links for the particular OSPFv3 AF process. Ra nge: 1 to 65535. Select to display brief overview information for the OSPFv3 AF virtual links. Select to display OSPFv3 AF virtual links information for all VR Fs. vrf <VRF-NAME> Specifies the name of a VRF. Default: default. Public show ospfv3-af IPv6 virtual-links 587. Syntax: show ospfv3-af [<PROCESS-ID>] IPv6 virtual-links [brief].
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show ospfv3-af IPv6 virtual-links command?
The syntax of the show ospfv3-af IPv6 virtual-links command is: show ospfv3-af [<PROCESS-ID>] IPv6 virtual-links [brief]
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show running-config all command do?
The show running-config all command displays the current running configuration, including default values for all protocols such as OSPFv3 AF. Syntax: show running-config all.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show running-config all command?
The syntax of the show running-config all command is: show running-config all.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show tech ospfv3-af command do?

The show tech ospfv3-af command shows the output of the following OSPFv3 AF commands: • show ospfv3-af all vrfs • show ospfv3-af interface all-vrfs • show ospfv3-af neighbors detail all-vrfs • show ospfv3-af neighbors summary all-vrfs • show ospfv3-af route all-vrfs • show ospfv3-af statistics all-vrfs Public show tech ospfv3-af 610 • show ospfv3-af statistics interface all-vrfs • show ospfv3-af border-routers all-vrfs • show ospfv3-af lsdb all-vrfs database-summary • show ospfv3-af virtual-links all-vrfs Captures the command output into a local file. Optional. Syntax: show tech ospfv3-af [local-file].
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show tech ospfv3-af command?
The syntax of the show tech ospfv3-af command is: show tech ospfv3-af [local-file]
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the neighbor activate command do?

The neighbor activate command this command enables the address-family capability and exchange of information specific to an address family with a BGP neighbor. The no form of this command removes the address-family capability and disables the exchange of routes for the specified address-family with the BGP neighbor. Specifies an IP address in IPv4 format (x.x.x.x), where x is a de cimal number from 0 to 255, or IPv6 format (xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: x xxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx), where x is a hexadecimal number f rom 0 to F. Specifies the unnumbered interface name of the nei ghbor. Specifies the unnumbered interface name of the neighbor. <IP-ADDR> <IFNAME> NOTE VPNV4 address-family, peer group, and BFD are not supported for BGP unnumbered. Syntax: neighbor {<IP-ADDR>|<IFNAME>} activate.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the neighbor activate command?
The syntax of the neighbor activate command is: neighbor {<IP-ADDR>|<IFNAME>} activate.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the neighbor add-paths advertise-best command?
The syntax of the neighbor add-paths advertise-best command is: neighbor {<IP-ADDR>|<IFNAME>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} add-paths advertise-best.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the neighbor ao command do?

The neighbor ao command enables TCP Authentication Option (TCP-AO) authentication on a TCP connection between two BGP neighbors. To disable this function, use the no form of this command. Specifies an IP address. <IP-ADDRESS> <IFNAME> <PEER-GROUP-NAME> Specifies the unnumbered interface name of the neighbor. Specifies a peer group. This is not supported on BGP unnumbe red. <keychain-name> Name of the keychain for the neighbor. The keychain allows keys to be configured with different valid li fetimes. This mechanism provides a way for a set of keys to be rotated a nd hence protect against long-lived-key attacks. At any give n time only one key is selected as active-key and keys are vali d for a duration of the defined send-lifetime. Syntax: neighbor {<IP-ADDR>|<IFNAME>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} ao <keychain-name>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the neighbor ao command?
The syntax of the neighbor ao command is: neighbor {<IP-ADDR>|<IFNAME>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} ao <keychain-name>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the neighbor ebgp-multihop command do?

The neighbor ebgp-multihop command enables BGP to establish a session with external peers residing on networks that are not directly connected. By default, BGP can only establish sessions with external BGP peers that are directly connected. If external BGP peer are not connected directly, then ebgp-multihop is enabled to initiate the connection with that external peer. If ebgp-multihop is disabled and external BGP peers are indirectly connected, then BGP peer session is not established. The no form of this command disables the peer ebgp-multihop feature. Specifies an IP address. <IP-ADDR> <PEER-GROUP-NAME> <ttl>. Syntax: neighbor {<IP-ADDR> | <PEER-GROUP-NAME>} ebgp-multihop <ttl>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the neighbor ebgp-multihop command?
The syntax of the neighbor ebgp-multihop command is: neighbor {<IP-ADDR> | <PEER-GROUP-NAME>} ebgp-multihop <ttl>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the neighbor fall-over bfd command do?

The neighbor fall-over bfd command enables BGP to register with BFD to receive fast peering session deactivation messages from BFD. You can either configure BFD support for BGP per neighbor or peer-group. The no form of this command disables BGP for BFD. NOTE Multihop BFD is not supported for BGP. Public neighbor fall-over bfd 732 Specifies the neighbor address in IPv4 format (x.x.x.x), where x is a decimal number from 0 to 255. Specifies the neighbor address in IPv6 format (xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx), where x is a hexadecimal numbe r from 0 to F. This parameter applies only to HPE Aruba Networ king 6300 and 6400 Switch series. Specifies a peer group. <IPv4-ADDR> <IPv6-ADDR> <PEER-GROUP-NAME>. Syntax: neighbor {<IPv4-ADDR>|<IPv6-ADDR>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} fall-over bfd.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the neighbor fall-over bfd command?
The syntax of the neighbor fall-over bfd command is: neighbor {<IPv4-ADDR>|<IPv6-ADDR>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} fall-over bfd.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the neighbor graceful-shutdown command do?

The neighbor graceful-shutdown command configures the wait time before shutting down the BGP neighbor session, and can also configure the local preference value to be advertised before graceful shutdown. The no form of this command sets the wait time to the default value of 180 seconds and the local- preference value to the default of 0. <IP-ADDR> <IFNAME> <PEER-GROUP-NAME> local-preference<LOCAL- PREF> <CONFIG-DELAY> Specifies an IP address in IPv4 format (x.x.x.x), where x is a de cimal number from 0 to 255, or IPv6 format (xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: x xxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx), where x is a hexadecimal number f rom 0 to F. Specifies the unnumbered interface name of the neighbor. Specifies a peer group. This is not supported on BGP unnumbe red. Syntax: neighbor {<IP-ADDR>|<IFNAME>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} graceful-shutdown.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the neighbor graceful-shutdown command?
The syntax of the neighbor graceful-shutdown command is: neighbor {<IP-ADDR>|<IFNAME>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} graceful-shutdown.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the neighbor interface command do?

The neighbor interface command configure a BGP neighbor by specifying the interface name on which the neighbor is connected instead of the neighbor’s IP address. When an unnumbered interface is specified to the neighbor command, a static route is auto-provisioned for the neighbor with the unnumbered interface as a destination. A static route is also auto-provisioned if the neighbor IP address is learned via LLDP. NOTE If OSPF is enabled on an unnumbered interface then the auto-provisioning is not supported as the route to the BGP peer is expected to be learned via OSPF. Peer-group is not supported for BGP peering on the IP unnumbered interface. The no form of this command disables this feature. Specifies an IP address. <IP-ADDR>. Syntax: neighbor {<IP-ADDR>|<IFNAME>} interface <ifname>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the neighbor interface command?
The syntax of the neighbor interface command is: neighbor {<IP-ADDR>|<IFNAME>} interface <ifname>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the neighbor next-hop-unchanged command do?

The neighbor next-hop-unchanged command enables the neighbor to preserve next-hop while advertising routes to eBGP peers, in the L2VPN EVPN address-family. The no form of this command resets the peer next-hop-unchanged status to default. <IP-ADDRESS> Specifies the neighbor IP address in the IPv4 format (x.x.x.x), where x is a decimal number from 0 to 255, or in the IPv6 form at (xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx), where x is a hexadecimal number from 0 to F. NOTE IPv6 MP-BGP peering must not be used for L 2VPN EVPN address family, because VXLAN tu nnel interface does not support IPv6 addresses. Specifies the unnumbered interface name of the neighbor. <IFNAME> Public neighbor next-hop-unchanged 751. Syntax: neighbor {<IP-ADDRESS>|<IFNAME>} next-hop-unchanged.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the neighbor next-hop-unchanged command?
The syntax of the neighbor next-hop-unchanged command is: neighbor {<IP-ADDRESS>|<IFNAME>} next-hop-unchanged.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the neighbor passive command do?

The neighbor passive command configures a specific neighbor, or all neighbors in a peer-group, as passive, which means that they will not initiate the TCP session. The no form of this command enables the neighbor to initiate the TCP session. Specifies an IP address. <IP-ADDRESS> <IFNAME> <PEER-GROUP-NAME> Specifies the unnumbered interface name of the neighbor. Specifies a peer group. Syntax: neighbor {<IP-ADDR>|<IFNAME>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} passive.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the neighbor passive command?
The syntax of the neighbor passive command is: neighbor {<IP-ADDR>|<IFNAME>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} passive.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the neighbor remote-as command do?

The neighbor remote-as command creates a peer, initiates the connection to the peer, and adds an entry to the BGP neighbor table. Specifies a neighbor with an autonomous system (AS) number that identifies the neighbor as internal to the local autonomous system. Otherwise, the neighbor is considered as external. By default, neighbors that are defined using this command, exchange only unicast address prefixes. The no form of this command disables the peer session and deletes the peer information. Specifies an IP address. <IP-ADDR> <IFNAME> <PEER-GROUP-NAME> remote-as <AS-NUMBER> Specifies the unnumbered interface name of the neighbor. Specifies a peer group. This is not supported on BGP unnumbe red. Syntax: neighbor {<IP-ADDR>|<IFNAME>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} remote-as <AS-NUMBER>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the neighbor remote-as command?
The syntax of the neighbor remote-as command is: neighbor {<IP-ADDR>|<IFNAME>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} remote-as <AS-NUMBER>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the neighbor remove-private-AS command do?

The neighbor remove-private-AS command this command forces the BGP neighbor to drop the private AS numbers. When the outbound update contains a sequence of private AS numbers, this sequence is dropped. If the command is configured for peer-group, then all the neighbors that are part of peer-group will remove the private-AS before sending the BGP update message. The no form of this command allows the private-AS number to be carried in BGP update message. Specifies an IP address. <IP-ADDRESS> <IFNAME> <PEER-GROUP-NAME> Specifies the unnumbered interface name of the neighbor. Specifies a peer group. This is not supported on BGP unnumbe red. Syntax: neighbor {<IP-ADDR>|<IFNAME>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} remove-private-AS.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the neighbor remove-private-AS command?
The syntax of the neighbor remove-private-AS command is: neighbor {<IP-ADDR>|<IFNAME>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} remove-private-AS.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the neighbor replace-rt-asn command?
The syntax of the neighbor replace-rt-asn command is: neighbor {<IP-ADDR>|<IPv6-ADDR>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} replace-rt-asn.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the neighbor route-reflector-client command do?

The neighbor route-reflector-client command this command configures the router as a BGP route reflector and the specified peer as its client. The no form of this command disables this function. <IP-ADDRESS> Specifies the neighbor IP address in the IPv4 format (x.x.x.x), where x is a decimal number from 0 to 255, or in the IPv6 form at (xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx), where x is a hexadecimal number from 0 to F. NOTE Pv6 MP-BGP peering must not be used for L2 VPN EVPN address family, because VXLAN tun nel interface does not support IPv6 addresses. Specifies the unnumbered interface name of the neighbor. <IFNAME> Public neighbor route-reflector-client 772 Specifies a peer group. This is not supported on BGP unnumbe red. <PEER-GROUP-NAME>. Syntax: neighbor {<IP-ADDR>|<IFNAME>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} route-reflector-client.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the neighbor route-reflector-client command?
The syntax of the neighbor route-reflector-client command is: neighbor {<IP-ADDR>|<IFNAME>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} route-reflector-client.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the neighbor shutdown command do?

The neighbor shutdown command this command disables the peer session, terminates any active session for the specified neighbor or peer group, and removes all associated routing information. This action can cause the sudden termination of many peering sessions. The no form of this command enables the peer session for the specified neighbor. Specifies an IP address. <IP-ADDRESS> <IFNAME> <PEER-GROUP-NAME>. Syntax: neighbor {<IP-ADDR>|<IFNAME>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} shutdown.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the neighbor shutdown command?
The syntax of the neighbor shutdown command is: neighbor {<IP-ADDR>|<IFNAME>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} shutdown.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the neighbor slow-peer command?
The syntax of the neighbor slow-peer command is: neighbor {<IP-ADDR>|<IPv6-ADDR>|<IFNAME>} slow-peer.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the neighbor timers command do?

The neighbor timers command this command sets the timers for a specific BGP neighbor or peer group. When the timer is applied to peer-group then all the neighbors that are part of peer-group will inherit the value configured. The no form of this command clears the timers for a specific BGP neighbor or peer group. Specifies an IP address. <IP-ADDRESS> <IFNAME> <PEER-GROUP-NAME> <KEEPALIVE> <HOLDTIME>. Syntax: neighbor {<IP-ADDR>|<IFNAME>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} timers <KEEPALIVE>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the neighbor timers command?
The syntax of the neighbor timers command is: neighbor {<IP-ADDR>|<IFNAME>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} timers <KEEPALIVE>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the neighbor timers connect-retry command do?

The neighbor timers connect-retry command this command sets the connect-retry timer for a specific BGP neighbor or peer group. The no form of this command clears the connect-retry timers for a specific BGP neighbor or peer group. Specifies an IP address. <IP-ADDR> <IFNAME> <PEER-GROUP-NAME> <RETRYTIME>. Syntax: neighbor {<IP-ADDR>|<IFNAME>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} timers connect-retry.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the neighbor timers connect-retry command?
The syntax of the neighbor timers connect-retry command is: neighbor {<IP-ADDR>|<IFNAME>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} timers connect-retry.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the neighbor weight command do?

The neighbor weight command this command assigns a weight to a neighbor connection. When the weight is applied to a peer-group then all the neighbors that are part of the peer-group will inherit the value configured. The no form of this command removes a weight assignment. Specifies an IP address. <IP-ADDR> <IFNAME> <PEER-GROUP-NAME> <WEIGHT-VALUE> This command specifies the networks to be advertised by the Border Gateway Protocol (BGP) routing processes. The no form of this command removes an entry from the routing table. Specifies the IPv4 network with mask. For example: 1.1.1.1/24 Public network 792 Specifies the IPv6 network with mask. For example: 2001: 0db8: 85a3: : 8a2e: 0370: 7334/24 route-map <ROUTE-MAP-NAME> Optional parameter. Syntax: neighbor {<IP-ADDR>|<IFNAME>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} weight <WEIGHT-VALUE>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the neighbor weight command?
The syntax of the neighbor weight command is: neighbor {<IP-ADDR>|<IFNAME>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} weight <WEIGHT-VALUE>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the router bgp command do?

The router bgp command this command configures the BGP instance on the router, configures the AS (Autonomous System) the router belongs to, and enters into the BGP router configuration mode. Only a single BGP AS number can be assigned for the entire system. The no form of the command deletes the BGP instance from the router. Public router bgp 796 Specifies a 4-byte AS number in the range 1-4294967295 i n integer format or from 0.1-65535.65535 in dotted format. AS-NUMBER Sets the extended community number attribute for a route matching the route map. Extended communities are supported only on Route Targets. This command is applicable to OSPF, static, and connected routes which will be redistributed to the BGP protocol. Syntax: router bgp <AS-NUMBER>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the router bgp command?
The syntax of the router bgp command is: router bgp <AS-NUMBER>

section: show bgp l2vpn evpn vni route-type

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show bgp l2vpn evpn vni route-type command do?

The show bgp l2vpn evpn vni route-type command shows the BGP L2VPN information for the particular EVPN VNI and routes type. Specifies the VNI. <ROUTE-TYPE-Value> Specifies the routes filtered by NLRI route type. HPE Aruba Networking 8325 and 10000 Switch series can disp lay information for route types 1-6. HPE Aruba Networking 6300, 6400, 8100, 8320, 8360, 9300, 9300S, 10040 Switch series display information for route types 2-6. Public show bgp l2vpn evpn vni route-type 848. Syntax: show bgp l2vpn evpn vni <VNI-Value> route-type <ROUTE-TYPE-Value>.

command: show bgp l2vpn evpn vni route-type
syntax: show bgp l2vpn evpn vni <VNI-Value> route-type <ROUTE-TYPE-Value>

section: show bgp l2vpn evpn vni route-type

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show bgp l2vpn evpn vni route-type command?
The syntax of the show bgp l2vpn evpn vni route-type command is: show bgp l2vpn evpn vni <VNI-Value> route-type <ROUTE-TYPE-Value>

command: show bgp l2vpn evpn vni route-type
syntax: show bgp l2vpn evpn vni <VNI-Value> route-type <ROUTE-TYPE-Value>

section: show bgp l2vpn evpn vtep route-type

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show bgp l2vpn evpn vtep route-type command do?

The show bgp l2vpn evpn vtep route-type command shows the BGP L2VPN information for the particular EVPN VTEP IP address and routes type. Specifies the VTEP IP address. <ROUTE-TYPE-Value> Specifies the routes filtered by NLRI route type. Public show bgp l2vpn evpn vtep route-type 852 HPE Aruba Networking 8325 and 10000 Switch series can disp lay information for route types 1-5. HPE Aruba Networking 6300, 6400, 8100, 8320, 8360, 9300, 9300S, 10040 switches display information for route types 2, 3 and 5. Syntax: show bgp l2vpn evpn vtep <IP-address> route-type <ROUTE-TYPE-Value>.

command: show bgp l2vpn evpn vtep route-type
syntax: show bgp l2vpn evpn vtep <IP-address> route-type <ROUTE-TYPE-Value>

section: show bgp l2vpn evpn vtep route-type

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show bgp l2vpn evpn vtep route-type command?
The syntax of the show bgp l2vpn evpn vtep route-type command is: show bgp l2vpn evpn vtep <IP-address> route-type <ROUTE-TYPE-Value>

command: show bgp l2vpn evpn vtep route-type
syntax: show bgp l2vpn evpn vtep <IP-address> route-type <ROUTE-TYPE-Value>

section: show bgp l2vpn evpn vtep vni

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show bgp l2vpn evpn vtep vni command do?

The show bgp l2vpn evpn vtep vni command shows the BGP L2VPN information for the particular EVPN VTEP IP address and VNI. Specifies the VTEP IP address. <IP-address> Public show bgp l2vpn evpn vtep vni 854 Specifies the VNI. Syntax: show bgp l2vpn evpn vtep <IP-address> vni <VNI-Value>.

command: show bgp l2vpn evpn vtep vni
syntax: show bgp l2vpn evpn vtep <IP-address> vni <VNI-Value>

section: show bgp l2vpn evpn vtep vni

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show bgp l2vpn evpn vtep vni command?
The syntax of the show bgp l2vpn evpn vtep vni command is: show bgp l2vpn evpn vtep <IP-address> vni <VNI-Value>

command: show bgp l2vpn evpn vtep vni
syntax: show bgp l2vpn evpn vtep <IP-address> vni <VNI-Value>

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the router vrrp {enable disable} command do?

The router vrrp {enable disable} command enables or disables VRRP protocol globally. You must globally enable the VRRP feature for VRRP virtual router. no router vrrp enable disables VRRP protocol globally but does not remove all VRRP configurations. no router vrrp disable enables VRRP protocol globally. Syntax: router vrrp {enable | disable}.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the router vrrp {enable disable} command?
The syntax of the router vrrp {enable disable} command is: router vrrp {enable | disable}.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the no router vrrp command do?

The no router vrrp command removes VRRP configuration and VRRP global protocol. If auto-confirm is enabled or VRRP is not configured on any interface, this command will not ask for user confirmation. Syntax: no router vrrp.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the no router vrrp command?
The syntax of the no router vrrp command is: no router vrrp.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show track command do?

The show track command shows all or specific track object information. Displays brief information about all or specific track objects Public show track 967 Displays information about a specified track object (1-128). Syntax: show track [brief | <OBJECT-ID>].
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show track command?
The syntax of the show track command is: show track [brief | <OBJECT-ID>]
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show track brief command do?
The show track brief command shows brief information for all track objects. Syntax: show track brief.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show track brief command?
The syntax of the show track brief command is: show track brief.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ip route interface command do?

The ip route interface command creates a route leak between the SRC-VRF and DST-VRF. Using the static method, the route must first be added to the destination VRF. The route is added to the local interface of the source VRF with a next-hop interface. The existing IP route command can then take either the next-hop IP or the next-hop interface. • INTERFACE refers to the outgoing interface in an m/s/p format • Users must provide both the next-hop IP and the interface information to leak network routes. • The next-hop IP information is not required to leak connected routes. The [no] form of command deletes the static VRF leaked route. Required: IPv4 IP-Address route destination. Syntax: ip route <IPv4 ADDR/MEMBER> interface <IPv4 ADDR>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the ip route interface command?
The syntax of the ip route interface command is: ip route <IPv4 ADDR/MEMBER> interface <IPv4 ADDR>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the router bgp vrf command do?

The router bgp vrf command this command configures VRF for the BGP instance. The no form of this command removes the configuration. Specifies a 4-byte AS number in the range 1-4294967295 i n integer format or from 0.1-65535.65535 in dotted format. Public router bgp vrf 1018 String VRF name for the VRF. Syntax: router bgp <AS-NUMBER> [vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the router bgp vrf command?
The syntax of the router bgp vrf command is: router bgp <AS-NUMBER> [vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show bgp info vrf command do?

The show bgp info vrf command displays BGP route-targets information for specified VRF. Display BGP RT information. Required string VRF name for the vrf. Shows the output from the VSX peer switch. If the switches do not have the VSX configuration or the ISL is down, the output f rom the VSX peer switch is not displayed. This parameter is avai lable on switches that support VSX. Syntax: show bgp info vrf <vrf-name>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show bgp info vrf command?
The syntax of the show bgp info vrf command is: show bgp info vrf <vrf-name>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the router rip command?
The syntax of the router rip command is: router rip <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show capacities rip command do?
The show capacities rip command displays maximum number of RIP interfaces, routes and process. Syntax: show capacities rip.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show capacities rip command?
The syntax of the show capacities rip command is: show capacities rip.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show capacities-status rip command do?
The show capacities-status rip command displays number of RIP interfaces, routes and process configured in the system. Syntax: show capacities-status rip.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show capacities-status rip command?
The syntax of the show capacities-status rip command is: show capacities-status rip.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the router ripng command?
The syntax of the router ripng command is: router ripng <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show capacities ripng command do?
The show capacities ripng command displays the maximum number of RIPng interfaces, routes and process. Syntax: show capacities ripng.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show capacities ripng command?
The syntax of the show capacities ripng command is: show capacities ripng.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show capacities-status ripng command do?
The show capacities-status ripng command displays number of RIPng interfaces, routes and process configured in the system. Syntax: show capacities-status ripng.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show capacities-status ripng command?
The syntax of the show capacities-status ripng command is: show capacities-status ripng.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, how do you configure IRDP?

To configure IRDP, follow the documented procedure: 1. Enable IRDP on an interface with the command ip irdp 2. Set the maximum hold time with the command ip irdp holdtime 3. Set the maximum router advertisement interval with the command ip irdp maxadvertinterv 4. Set the minimum router advertisement interval with the command ip irdp minadvertinterv 5. Set the IRDP preference level with the command ip irdp preference 6. Review IRDP configuration settings with the command show ip irdp.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, how do you configure IPv6 RA?

To configure IPv6 RA, follow the documented procedure: 1. Enable transmission of IPv6 router advertisements with the command no IPv6 nd suppress-r 2. Optionally, configure IPv6 unicast address prefixes with the command IPv6 nd prefix 3. Optionally, configure support for DNS name resolution with the commands IPv6 nd ra dns ser.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv6 address link-local command do?

The IPv6 address link-local command enables IPv6 on the current interface. If no address is specified, an IPv6 link-local address is auto-generated for the interface. If an address is specified, auto-configuration is disabled and the specified address/mask is assigned to the interface. To disable IPv6 link-local on the interface, remove IPv6 address link-local, IPv6 address <global-IPv6- address>, and IPv6 address autoconfig from the interface. NOTE This feature is not available on the management VRF. <IPv6-ADDR> Specifies the IP address in IPv6 format (xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx), where x is a hexadecimal number from 0 to F. You can use two colons (: :) to represent consecutive zero s (but only once), remove leading zeros, and collapse a hextet o f four zeros to a single 0. Syntax: IPv6 address link-local [<IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK>].
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the IPv6 address link-local command?
The syntax of the IPv6 address link-local command is: IPv6 address link-local [<IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK>]
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv6 nd cache-limit command do?

The IPv6 nd cache-limit command configures the limit on the number of neighbor entries in the ND cache. The no form of this command sets the cache limit to the default value. Specifies the neighbor cache entries limit. Range: 1-131072. Default: 131072. <CACHELIMIT>. Syntax: IPv6 nd cache-limit <CACHELIMIT>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the IPv6 nd cache-limit command?
The syntax of the IPv6 nd cache-limit command is: IPv6 nd cache-limit <CACHELIMIT>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv6 nd dad attempts command do?

The IPv6 nd dad attempts command configures the number of neighbor solicitations to be sent when performing duplicate address detection (DAD) for a unicast address configured on an interface. If the active gateway is configured with the same IP as an SVI IP, then IPv6 DAD cannot be configured. The no form of this command sets the number of attempts to the default value. dad attempts <NUM-ATTEMPTS> Specifies the number of neighbor solicitations to send. Range: 0 -15. Default: 1. Syntax: IPv6 nd dad attempts <NUM-ATTEMPTS>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the IPv6 nd dad attempts command?
The syntax of the IPv6 nd dad attempts command is: IPv6 nd dad attempts <NUM-ATTEMPTS>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv6 nd hop-limit command do?

The IPv6 nd hop-limit command configures the hop limit to be sent in RAs. The no form of this command resets the hop limit to 0. This reset eliminates the hop limit from the RAs that originate on the interface, so the host determines the hop limit. hop-limit <HOPLIMIT> Specifies the hop limit. Range: 0-255. Default: 64. Syntax: IPv6 nd hop-limit <HOPLIMIT>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv6 nd mtu command do?

The IPv6 nd mtu command configures the MTU size to be sent in the RA messages. Public IPv6 nd mtu 44 The no form of this command sets hop limit to the default value. Specifies the MTU size. Range: 1280-65535 bytes. Default: 1 500 bytes. <MTU-VALUE>. Syntax: IPv6 nd mtu <MTU-VALUE>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the IPv6 nd mtu command?
The syntax of the IPv6 nd mtu command is: IPv6 nd mtu <MTU-VALUE>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv6 nd ns-interval command do?

The IPv6 nd ns-interval command configures the ND time in milliseconds between DAD neighbor solicitations sent for an unresolved destination. Increase the ns-interval time if the network is slow or if there are persistent retry failures. If the active gateway is configured with the same IP as an SVI IP, then IPv6 DAD cannot be configured Public IPv6 nd ns-interval 45 The no form of this command sets the ns-interval to the default value. Specifies the neighbor solicitation interval. Range: 1000-3600 000 milliseconds. Default: 1000 milliseconds. <TIME>. Syntax: IPv6 nd ns-interval <TIME>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the IPv6 nd ns-interval command?
The syntax of the IPv6 nd ns-interval command is: IPv6 nd ns-interval <TIME>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv6 nd prefix command do?

The IPv6 nd prefix command specifies prefixes for the routing switch to include in RAs transmitted on the interface. IPv6 hosts use the prefixes in RAs to autoconfigure themselves with global unicast addresses. The autoconfigured address of a host is composed of the advertised prefix and the interface identifier in the current link-local address of the host. By default, advertise, autoconfig, and onlink are set. The no form of this command removes the configuration on the interface. Specifies the IPv6 prefix to advertise in RA. Format: X: X: : X: X/M <IPv6-ADDR>/<PREFIX-LEN> default Specifies apply configuration to all on-link prefixes that are n ot individually set by the IPv6 ra prefix <IPv6-ADDR>/<PRE FIX-LEN> command. Syntax: IPv6 nd prefix <IPv6-ADDR>/<PREFIX-LEN>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the IPv6 nd prefix command?
The syntax of the IPv6 nd prefix command is: IPv6 nd prefix <IPv6-ADDR>/<PREFIX-LEN>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv6 nd ra dns server command do?

The IPv6 nd ra dns server command configures the IPv6 address of a preferred Recursive DNS Server (RDNSS) to be included in Router Advertisements (RAs) transmitted on the interface. The no form of this command removes the configured DNS server from the RAs transmitted on the interface. <IPv6-ADDR> Specifies the RDNSS address in IPv6 format (xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: x xxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx), where x is a hexadecimal number f rom 0 to F. You can use two colons (: :) to represent consecutive zeros (but only once), remove leading zeros, and collapse a hex tet of four zeros to a single 0. For example, this address 2222: 0 000: 3333: 0000: 0000: 0000: 4444: 0055 becomes 2222: 0: 333 3: : 4444: 55. lifetime <TIME> Specifies IPv6 DNS server lifetime in seconds. Range: 4-4294 967295 seconds. Default: 1800 seconds. Syntax: IPv6 nd ra dns server <IPv6-ADDR> [lifetime <TIME>].
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the IPv6 nd ra dns server command?
The syntax of the IPv6 nd ra dns server command is: IPv6 nd ra dns server <IPv6-ADDR> [lifetime <TIME>]
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv6 nd ra lifetime command do?

The IPv6 nd ra lifetime command configures the lifetime, in seconds, for the routing switch to be used as a default router by hosts on the current interface. The no form of this command sets lifetime to the default of 1800 seconds. <TIME> Specifies lifetime in seconds of a default router. A setting of 0 fo r default router lifetime in an RA indicates that the routing switc h is not a default router on the interface. Range: 0-9000 seco nds. Default: 1800 seconds. Public IPv6 nd ra lifetime 51. Syntax: IPv6 nd ra lifetime <TIME>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the IPv6 nd ra lifetime command?
The syntax of the IPv6 nd ra lifetime command is: IPv6 nd ra lifetime <TIME>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv6 nd ra managed-config-flag command do?

The IPv6 nd ra managed-config-flag command controls the M fLAGsetting in RAs the router transmits on the current interface. Enable the M flag to indicate that hosts can obtain IP address through DHCPv6. The M flag is disabled by default. The no form of this command turns off (disables) the M flag. Public IPv6 nd ra managed-config-flag 52 0 0 1 1 0 1 0 1 Indicates that no information is available via DHCPv6. Indicates that other configuratio n information is available via DH CPv6. Examples of such informat ion are DNS-related informatio n or information on other servers within the network. Indicates that addresses are avail able via Dynamic Host Configura tion Protocol (DHCPv6). If the M flag is set, the O flag is redundant and can be ignored be cause DHCPv6 will return all avai lable configuration information. Syntax: IPv6 nd ra managed-config-flag.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the IPv6 nd ra managed-config-flag command?
The syntax of the IPv6 nd ra managed-config-flag command is: IPv6 nd ra managed-config-flag.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv6 nd ra max-interval command do?

The IPv6 nd ra max-interval command configures the maximum interval between transmissions of IPv6 RAs on the interface. The interval between RA transmissions on an interface is a random value that changes every time an RA is sent. The interval is calculated to be a value between the current max-interval and min-interval settings. The no form of this command returns the setting to its default, provided the default value is less than the default lifetime value. Specifies the maximum advertisement time in seconds. Range: 4-1800. Default: 600 seconds. <TIME>. Syntax: IPv6 nd ra max-interval <TIME>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the IPv6 nd ra max-interval command?
The syntax of the IPv6 nd ra max-interval command is: IPv6 nd ra max-interval <TIME>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv6 nd ra min-interval command do?

The IPv6 nd ra min-interval command configures the minimum interval between transmissions of IPv6 RAs on the interface. The interval between RA transmissions on an interface is a random value that changes every time an RA is sent. The interval is calculated to be a value between the current max-interval and min-interval settings. The no form of this command returns the setting to its default, provided the default value is less than the current max-interval setting. Specifies a minimum advertisement time in seconds. Range: 3 -1350. Default: 200 seconds. <TIME> Public IPv6 nd ra min-interval 55. Syntax: IPv6 nd ra min-interval <TIME>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the IPv6 nd ra min-interval command?
The syntax of the IPv6 nd ra min-interval command is: IPv6 nd ra min-interval <TIME>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv6 nd ra other-config-flag command do?

The IPv6 nd ra other-config-flag command controls the O-bit in RAs the router transmits on the current interface; but is ignored unless the M-bit is disabled in RAs. Configure to set the O-bit in RA messages for host to obtain network parameters through DHCPv6. The other-config-flag is disabled by default. For more information on configuring the M-bit, see IPv6 nd ra managed-config-flag. Public IPv6 nd ra other-config-flag 56 The no form of this command turns off (disables) the setting for this command in RAs. Syntax: IPv6 nd ra other-config-flag.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the IPv6 nd ra other-config-flag command?
The syntax of the IPv6 nd ra other-config-flag command is: IPv6 nd ra other-config-flag.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv6 nd ra reachable-time command do?

The IPv6 nd ra reachable-time command sets the amount of time that the interface considers a device to be reachable after receiving a reachability confirmation from the device. The no form of this command sets the reachable time to the default value of 0. (no limit). Public IPv6 nd ra reachable-time 57 Specifies the reachable time in milliseconds. Range: 1000-36 00000. Default: 0 (no limit). <TIME>. Syntax: IPv6 nd ra reachable-time <TIME>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the IPv6 nd ra reachable-time command?
The syntax of the IPv6 nd ra reachable-time command is: IPv6 nd ra reachable-time <TIME>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv6 nd ra retrans-timer command do?

The IPv6 nd ra retrans-timer command configures the period (retransmit timer) between ND solicitations sent by a host for an unresolved destination, or between DAD neighbor solicitation requests. By default, hosts on the interface use their own locally configured NS-interval settings instead of using the value received in the RAs. Increase this timer when neighbor solicitation retries or failures are occur, or in a "slow" (WAN) network. Public IPv6 nd ra retrans-timer 58 The no form of this command sets the value to the default of 0. Specifies the retransmit timer value in milliseconds. Range: 0 - 4294967295 milliseconds. Default: 0 (Use locally configured N S-interval). <TIME>. Syntax: IPv6 nd ra retrans-timer <TIME>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the IPv6 nd ra retrans-timer command?
The syntax of the IPv6 nd ra retrans-timer command is: IPv6 nd ra retrans-timer <TIME>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv6 nd router-preference command do?

The IPv6 nd router-preference command specifies the value that is set in the Default Router Preference (DRP) field of Router Advertisements (RAs) that the switch sends from an interface. An interface with a DRP value of high will be preferred by other devices on the network over interfaces with an RA value of medium or low. The no form of this command set the value to the default of medium. Sets DRP to high. Sets DRP to medium. Default. Sets DRP to low. switch(config)# interface 1/1/1 switch(config-if)# IPv6 nd router-preference high Public IPv6 nd router-preference 61. Syntax: IPv6 nd router-preference {high | medium | low}.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the guide say about Interface counters polling?

Interface counters polling: • Agent IP configuration for IPv4 and IPv6 • Header size configuration • Max datagram size configuration • Ingress sampling for all forwarded traffic (L2, L3) • Egress sampling for all forwarded traffic (L2, L3) • Enable/Disable sFlow per interface • Support for three remote collectors • A collector can be defined on the default and non-default VRF • Sampling on Ethernet and LAG interfaces • High availability support (sampling continues to work after switch-over) • Source IP support (setting source IP for sFlow datagrams sent to a remote collector) Limitations • Sampling rate cannot be set per interface (global only) • sFlow is not configurable through SNMP. • sFlow egress global counters will increase for broadcast, unknown-unicast, & multicast (BUM) traffic. However, the interface counters will not increase because it is flooded traffic. The output port in the sFlow sampled packet for BUM traffic will be 0x80000000. • When the sampling mode is configured as both, the ingress and egress packets may not be sampled in equal proportions.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the clear sflow statistics command do?

The clear sflow statistics command this command clears the sFlow sample statistics counter to 0 either globally or for a specific interface. Specifies all interfaces on the switch. interface <INTERFACE-NAME> Specifies the name of an interface on the switch. Syntax: clear sflow statistics {global | interface <INTERFACE-NAME>}.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the clear sflow statistics command?
The syntax of the clear sflow statistics command is: clear sflow statistics {global | interface <INTERFACE-NAME>}.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the sflow command do?

The sflow command enables the sFlow agent. • In the config context, this command enables the sFlow agent globally on all interfaces. • In an config-if context, this command enables the sFlow agent on a specific interface. sFlow cannot be enabled on a member of a LAG, only on the LAG. The sFlow agent is disabled by default. The no form of this command disables the sFlow agent and deletes all sFlow configuration settings, either globally, or for a specific interface. Syntax: sflow.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the sflow command?
The syntax of the sflow command is: sflow.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the sflow disable command do?
The sflow disable command disables the sFlow agent, but retains any existing sFlow configuration settings. The settings become active if the sFlow agent is re-enabled. Syntax: sflow disable.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the sflow disable command?
The syntax of the sflow disable command is: sflow disable.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the sflow polling <INTERVAL> command do?

The sflow polling <INTERVAL> command defines the global polling interval for sFlow in seconds. The no form of this command sets the polling interval to the default value of 30 seconds. Specifies the polling interval in seconds. Range: 10 to 3600. De fault: 30. <INTERVAL>. Syntax: sflow polling <INTERVAL>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the sflow polling <INTERVAL> command?
The syntax of the sflow polling <INTERVAL> command is: sflow polling <INTERVAL>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the no ip dhcp preferred-vlan vlan <VLAN-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command do?

The no ip dhcp preferred-vlan vlan <VLAN-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command specifies the preferred VLAN for DHCP client options, including DNS, NTP, and Default Gateway. The preferred VLAN is the VLAN used to select DHCP client options within a specified VRF when multiple Public ip dhcp preferred-vlan 112 VLANs have DHCP client enabled in the same VRF. If no VRF is specified, the configuration applies to the default VRF. In cases where the preferred VLAN is not explicitly configured, the lowest VLAN with DHCP client enabled will be automatically selected. The no form of the command removes the preferred VLAN configuration and restores the default behavior. VLAN ID to set as preferred VLAN. VLAN identifier range: 1 to 4094. Specifies the VRF in which the preferred VLANshould be confi gured.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the dhcp-relay hop-count-increment command do?

The dhcp-relay hop-count-increment command enables the DHCP relay hop count increment feature, which causes the DHCP relay agent to increment the hop count in all relayed DHCP packets. Hop count is enabled by default. The no form of this command disables the hop count increment feature. Syntax: dhcp-relay hop-count-increment.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the dhcp-relay hop-count-increment command?
The syntax of the dhcp-relay hop-count-increment command is: dhcp-relay hop-count-increment.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the dhcp-relay l2vpn-clients command do?

The dhcp-relay l2vpn-clients command enables forwarding of packets from L2 VPN clients. Forwarding is enabled by default. Best practices is to disable this configuration on all the VXLAN tunnel endpoints (VTEPs), to avoid forwarding duplicate DHCP requests to the server. The no form of this command disables forwarding of packets from L2 VPN clients. Syntax: dhcp-relay l2vpn-clients.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the dhcp-relay l2vpn-clients command?
The syntax of the dhcp-relay l2vpn-clients command is: dhcp-relay l2vpn-clients.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the dhcp-relay vsx active-active command do?

The dhcp-relay vsx active-active command enables DHCP relay active-active mode in VSX. When enabled, DHCP relay is active on both VSX Primary and Secondary peers. DHCP client requests are processed on the same VSX peer that receives them. A VSX peer that receives DHCP client request packets from its peer silently ignores them. By default, this feature is disabled, and DHCP request packets are forwarded to the configured helper addresses only by the VSX primary. The no form of this command disables DHCP relay active-active mode, and DHCP request packets are forwarded to configured helper addresses only by the VSX primary. Syntax: dhcp-relay vsx active-active.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the dhcp-relay vsx active-active command?
The syntax of the dhcp-relay vsx active-active command is: dhcp-relay vsx active-active.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the dhcp-smart-relay command do?

The dhcp-smart-relay command enables DHCP Smart Relay on the device and on all the interfaces where IP helper addresses are configured. Disabled by default at the device level. Not supported on the management interface. The no form of this command disables DHCP Smart Relay. NOTE Prior to enabling DHCP Smart Relay, enable IP helper address configuration and configure secondary IP addresses on the interface. Syntax: dhcp-smart-relay.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the dhcp-smart-relay command?
The syntax of the dhcp-smart-relay command is: dhcp-smart-relay.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, how do you configure the DHCPv6 relay agent?

To configure the DHCPv6 relay agent, follow the documented procedure: 1. Enable the DHCPv6 relay agent with the command dhcpv6-relay 2. Configure one or more IP helper addresses with the command IPv6 helper-address. This.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the dhcpv6-relay vsx active-active command do?

The dhcpv6-relay vsx active-active command enables DHCPv6 relay active-active mode in VSX. When enabled, DHCPv6 relay is active on both VSX Primary and Secondary peers. DHCPv6 client requests are processed on the same VSX peer that receives them. A VSX peer that receives DHCPv6 client request packets from its peer silently ignores them. By default, this feature is disabled, and DHCPv6 request packets are forwarded to the configured helper addresses only by the VSX primary. The no form of this command disables DHCPv6 relay active-active mode, and DHCPv6 request packets are forwarded to configured helper addresses only by the VSX primary. Syntax: dhcpv6-relay vsx active-active.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the dhcpv6-relay vsx active-active command?
The syntax of the dhcpv6-relay vsx active-active command is: dhcpv6-relay vsx active-active.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the diag-dump dhcpv6-relay basic command do?
The diag-dump dhcpv6-relay basic command dumps DHCPv6 relay configurations for all interfaces. Syntax: diag-dump dhcpv6-relay basic.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the diag-dump dhcpv6-relay basic command?
The syntax of the diag-dump dhcpv6-relay basic command is: diag-dump dhcpv6-relay basic.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, how do you troubleshoot?

To troubleshoot, follow the documented procedure: 1. Ping to check IP reachability between switch and the server(s) 2. Confirm the ping test is made in the appropriate VRF and with the correct gateway IP. By default, DHCP.

section: Configuring a DHCPv4 server on a VRF

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, how do you configure a DHCPv4 server on a VRF?

To configure a DHCPv4 server on a VRF, follow the documented procedure: 1. Assign the DHCPv4 server to a VRF with the command dhcp-server vrf. This switches to the DHCPv4 2. Define an address pool for the VRF with the command pool. This switches to the DHCPv4 server pool.

task: Configuring a DHCPv4 server on a VRF

section: Configuring the DHCPv6 server on a VRF

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, how do you configure the DHCPv6 server on a VRF?

To configure the DHCPv6 server on a VRF, follow the documented procedure: 1. Define an address pool for the VRF with the command pool. This switches to the DHCPv6 server pool 2. Enable the DHCPv6 server on the VRF with the command enable 3. Configure support for persistent external storage of DHCP settings with the command dhcpv6-server 4. View DHCPv6 server configuration settings with the command show dhcpv6-server all-vrfs.

task: Configuring the DHCPv6 server on a VRF

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the bootp <REMOTE-URL> command do?

The bootp <REMOTE-URL> command sets the BOOTP options that are returned by the DHCPv4 server for the current pool. BOOTP provides a way to distribute an IP address and boot image file to client stations. The DHCPv4 server returns the IP address and the location of the boot image file, which must be stored on an external TFTP server. The no form of this command disables support for BOOTP. <REMOTE-URL> tftp://{<IP> | <HOST>}/<FILE> Specifies the name and location of a BOOTP file on a TFTP server in the format: Public bootp 181 • <IP>: Specifies the IP address of the TFTP server hosti ng the file in IPv4 format (x.x.x.x), where x is a decimal nu mber from 0 to 255. You can remove leading zeros. For exa mple, the address 192.169.005.100 becomes 192.168.5.100. Syntax: bootp <REMOTE-URL>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the bootp <REMOTE-URL> command?
The syntax of the bootp <REMOTE-URL> command is: bootp <REMOTE-URL>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the dhcp-server vrf <VRF-NAME> command do?

The dhcp-server vrf <VRF-NAME> command configures the DHCPv4 server to support a VRF and changes to the config-dhcp-server context for that VRF. The no form of this command removes DHCPv4 server support on a VRF. Name of a VRF. Syntax: dhcp-server vrf <VRF-NAME>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the dhcp-server vrf <VRF-NAME> command?
The syntax of the dhcp-server vrf <VRF-NAME> command is: dhcp-server vrf <VRF-NAME>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the disable command do?
The disable command disables the DHCPv4 server on the current VRF. The DHCPv4 server is disabled by default when configured on a VRF. Syntax: disable.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the disable command?
The syntax of the disable command is: disable.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the dns-server <IPv4-ADDR-LIST> command do?

The dns-server <IPv4-ADDR-LIST> command defines up to four DNS servers for the current DHCPv4 server pool. The no form of this command removes the specified DNS servers from the pool. Public dns-server 190 Specifies the IP addresses of the DNS servers in IPv4 format (x. x.x.x), where x is a decimal number from 0 to 255. Separate add resses with a space. <IPv4-ADDR-LIST>. Syntax: dns-server <IPv4-ADDR-LIST>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the dns-server <IPv4-ADDR-LIST> command?
The syntax of the dns-server <IPv4-ADDR-LIST> command is: dns-server <IPv4-ADDR-LIST>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the domain-name <DOMAIN-NAME> command do?

The domain-name <DOMAIN-NAME> command defines a domain name for the current DHCPv4 server pool. The no form of this command removes the specified domain name from the pool. Specifies a domain name. Range: 1 to 255 printable ASCII chara cters. <DOMAIN-NAME>. Syntax: domain-name <DOMAIN-NAME>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the domain-name <DOMAIN-NAME> command?
The syntax of the domain-name <DOMAIN-NAME> command is: domain-name <DOMAIN-NAME>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the lease {<TIME> | infinite} command do?

The lease {<TIME> | infinite} command sets the length of the DHCPv4 lease time for the current pool. The lease time determines how long an IP address is valid before a DHCPv4 client must request that it be renewed. The no form of this command returns the DHCPv4 lease time to its default value 1 hour. Public lease 194 Sets the DHCPv4 lease time. Format: DD: HH: MM. Default: 01: 0 0: 00. Sets the DHCPv4 lease time to infinite. This means that addres ses do not need to be renewed. <TIME> infinite. Syntax: lease {<TIME> | infinite}.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the lease {<TIME> | infinite} command?
The syntax of the lease {<TIME> | infinite} command is: lease {<TIME> | infinite}.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the netbios-name-server <IPv4-ADDR-LIST> command do?

The netbios-name-server <IPv4-ADDR-LIST> command defines up to four NetBIOS WINS servers for the current DHCPv4 server pool. WINS is used by Microsoft DHCP clients to match host names with IP addresses. The no form of this command removes the specified WINS servers from the pool. Specifies the IP addresses of NetBIOS (WINS) servers in IPv4 fo rmat (x.x.x.x), where x is a decimal number from 0 to 255. Sep arate addresses with a space. A maximum of four IP addresses can be defined. <IPv4-ADDR-LIST>. Syntax: netbios-name-server <IPv4-ADDR-LIST>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the netbios-name-server <IPv4-ADDR-LIST> command?
The syntax of the netbios-name-server <IPv4-ADDR-LIST> command is: netbios-name-server <IPv4-ADDR-LIST>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the netbios-node-type <TYPE> command do?

The netbios-node-type <TYPE> command defines the NetBIOS node type for the current DHCPv4 server pool. The no form of this command removes the NetBIOS node type for the current pool. Public netbios-node-type 197 Specifies the NetBIOS node type: broadcast, hybrid, mixed, or peer-to-peer. <TYPE>. Syntax: netbios-node-type <TYPE>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the netbios-node-type <TYPE> command?
The syntax of the netbios-node-type <TYPE> command is: netbios-node-type <TYPE>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the pool <POOL-NAME> command do?

The pool <POOL-NAME> command creates a DHCPv4 server pool for the current VRF and switches to the config-dhcp-server-pool context for it. Multiple pools, each with a distinct range, can be assigned to a VRF. A maximum of 64 pools (IPv4 and IPv6), 64 address ranges, and 8182 clients are supported on the switch across all VRFs. The no form of this command deletes the specified DHCPv4 server pool. Specifies the DHCPv4 pool name. A maximum of 64 pools (IP v 4 and IPv6) are supported across VRFs on the switch. Range: 1 to 32 printable ASCII characters. First character must be a letter or number. <POOL-NAME>. Syntax: pool <POOL-NAME>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the pool <POOL-NAME> command?
The syntax of the pool <POOL-NAME> command is: pool <POOL-NAME>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the range <LOW-IPv4-ADDR> <HIGH-IPv4-ADDR> [prefix-len <MASK>] command do?

The range <LOW-IPv4-ADDR> <HIGH-IPv4-ADDR> [prefix-len <MASK>] command defines the range of IP addresses supported by the current DHCPv4 server pool. A maximum of 64 ranges are supported per switch across all VRFs. The no form of this command deletes the address range for the current pool. <LOW-IPv4-ADDR> Specifies the lowest IP address in the pool in IPv4 format (x.x.x.x), where x is a decimal number from 0 to 255. Specifies the highest IP address in the pool in IPv4 format (x.x. x.x), where x is a decimal number from 0 to 255. Public range 202 Specifies the number of bits in the address mask in CIDR format (x), where x is a decimal number from 0 to 32. NOTE When active gateway is configured on the inter face serviced by the pool, you must specify a pr efix length that matches the mask on the IP add ress assigned to the interface. Syntax: range <LOW-IPv4-ADDR> <HIGH-IPv4-ADDR> [prefix-len <MASK>].
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the range <LOW-IPv4-ADDR> <HIGH-IPv4-ADDR> [prefix-len <MASK>] command?
The syntax of the range <LOW-IPv4-ADDR> <HIGH-IPv4-ADDR> [prefix-len <MASK>] command is: range <LOW-IPv4-ADDR> <HIGH-IPv4-ADDR> [prefix-len <MASK>]
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the static-bind {ip <IPv4-ADDR>}|{ mac <MAC-ADDR>} [hostname <HOST>] command do?

The static-bind {ip <IPv4-ADDR>}|{ mac <MAC-ADDR>} [hostname <HOST>] command creates a static binding that associates an IP address in the current pool with a specific MAC address. This causes the DHCPv4 server to only assign the specified IP address to a client station with the specified MAC address. The no form of this command removes the specified binding. <IPv4-ADDR> Specifies an IP address in IPv4 format (x.x.x.x), where x is a de cimal number from 0 to 255. The IP address must be within the address range defined for the current pool. Public static-bind 207 Specifies a client station MAC address (xx: xx: xx: xx: xx: xx), where x is a hexadecimal number from 0 to F. Specifies the host name of the client station. Range: 1 to 255 pr intable ASCII characters Defines a static address for the server pool primary-pool on VRF primary. Syntax: static-bind {ip <IPv4-ADDR>}|{ mac <MAC-ADDR>} [hostname <HOST>].
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the static-bind {ip <IPv4-ADDR>}|{ mac <MAC-ADDR>} [hostname <HOST>] command?
The syntax of the static-bind {ip <IPv4-ADDR>}|{ mac <MAC-ADDR>} [hostname <HOST>] command is: static-bind {ip <IPv4-ADDR>}|{ mac <MAC-ADDR>} [hostname <HOST>]
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the clear dhcpv6-server leases command do?

The clear dhcpv6-server leases command clears DHCPv6 server lease information. The DHCPv6 server must be disabled before clearing lease information. Clears leases for all VRFs. <IPv6-ADDR> vrf <VRF-NAME> Clears the lease for a specific client on a specific VRF. Specify t he client address in IPv6 format (xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: x xxx: xxxx: xxxx), where x is a hexadecimal number from 0 to F. You can use two colons (: :) to represent consecutive zeros (but only once), remove leading zeros, and collapse a hextet of four zeros to a single 0. For example, this address 2222: 0000: 3 333: 0000: 0000: 0000: 4444: 0055 becomes 2222: 0: 3333: : 4444: 55. vrf <VRF-NAME> Clears leases for a specific VRF. Syntax: clear dhcpv6-server leases [all-vrfs | <IPv6-ADDR> vrf <VRF-NAME>] | vrf.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the clear dhcpv6-server leases command?
The syntax of the clear dhcpv6-server leases command is: clear dhcpv6-server leases [all-vrfs | <IPv6-ADDR> vrf <VRF-NAME>] | vrf.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the dhcpv6-server vrf VRF-NAME command do?

The dhcpv6-server vrf VRF-NAME command configures the DHCPv6 server to support a VRF and changes to the config-dhcpv6-server context for that VRF. Public dhcpv6-server vrf 214 The no form of this command removes DHCPv6 server support on a VRF. Name of a VRF. Syntax: dhcpv6-server vrf VRF-NAME.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the dhcpv6-server vrf VRF-NAME command?
The syntax of the dhcpv6-server vrf VRF-NAME command is: dhcpv6-server vrf VRF-NAME.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the dns-server <IPVv6-ADDR-LIST> command do?

The dns-server <IPVv6-ADDR-LIST> command defines up to four DNS servers for the current DHCPv6 server pool. The no form of this command removes the specified DNS servers from the pool. Specifies the IP addresses of the DNS servers in IPv6 format (x xxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx), where x is a hexa decimal number from 0 to F. Separate addresses with a space. A maximum of four IP addresses can be defined. <IPVv6-ADDR-LIST>. Syntax: dns-server <IPVv6-ADDR-LIST>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the dns-server <IPVv6-ADDR-LIST> command?
The syntax of the dns-server <IPVv6-ADDR-LIST> command is: dns-server <IPVv6-ADDR-LIST>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the range <LOW-IPv6-ADDR> <HIGH-IPv6-ADDR> [prefix-len <MASK>] command do?

The range <LOW-IPv6-ADDR> <HIGH-IPv6-ADDR> [prefix-len <MASK>] command defines the range of IP addresses supported by the current DHCPv6 server pool. A maximum of 64 ranges are supported per switch across all VRFs. The no form of this command deletes the address range for the current pool. Specifies the lowest IP address in the pool in IPv6 format (xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx), where x is a hexadeci mal number from 0 to F. Specifies the highest IP address in the pool in IPv6 format (xxx x: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx), where x is a hexade cimal number from 0 to F. Specifies the number of bits in the address mask in CIDR format (x), where x is a decimal number from 64 to128. <LOW-IPv6-ADDR> <HIGH-IPv6-ADDR> prefix-len <MASK>. Syntax: range <LOW-IPv6-ADDR> <HIGH-IPv6-ADDR> [prefix-len <MASK>].
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the range <LOW-IPv6-ADDR> <HIGH-IPv6-ADDR> [prefix-len <MASK>] command?
The syntax of the range <LOW-IPv6-ADDR> <HIGH-IPv6-ADDR> [prefix-len <MASK>] command is: range <LOW-IPv6-ADDR> <HIGH-IPv6-ADDR> [prefix-len <MASK>]
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the static-bind IPv6 <IPVv6-ADDR> client-id <ID> [hostname <HOST>] command do?

The static-bind IPv6 <IPVv6-ADDR> client-id <ID> [hostname <HOST>] command creates a static binding that associates an IP address in the current pool with a client identifier or DUID. This causes the DHCPv6 server to only assign the specified IP address to a client station with the specified client identifier or DUID. The no form of this command removes the specified static binding from the pool. Public static-bind 228 <IPv6-ADDR> Specifies the IP address to assign in IPv6 format (xxxx: xxxx: xx xx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx), where x is a hexadecimal num ber from 0 to F. For example, this address 2222: 0000: 333 3: 0000: 0000: 0000: 4444: 0055 becomes 2222: 0: 3333: : 4444: 55. client-id <ID> Specifies the client identifier or DUID. hostname <HOST> Specifies the host name of the client station. Range: 1 to 255 pr intable ASCII characters. Syntax: static-bind IPv6 <IPVv6-ADDR> client-id <ID> [hostname <HOST>].
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the static-bind IPv6 <IPVv6-ADDR> client-id <ID> [hostname <HOST>] command?
The syntax of the static-bind IPv6 <IPVv6-ADDR> client-id <ID> [hostname <HOST>] command is: static-bind IPv6 <IPVv6-ADDR> client-id <ID> [hostname <HOST>]

section: 4. Which VLAN is assigned a DHCP address?

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the guide say about 4. Which VLAN is assigned a DHCP address? ?

4. Which VLAN is assigned a DHCP address? : The factory default VLAN to which the DHCP address is assigned is VLAN1. Users can configure the VLAN for DHCP allocation on platforms supporting multiple VLAN configurations. Only a single VLAN can support DHCP assignments. 5. Can DHCP assignments be disabled in an HPE Aruba Networking AOS-CX switch? Use the no ip dhcp command on the required VLAN interface or management VRF port you can disable the DHCP assignments in an HPE Aruba Networking AOS-CX switch. Public FAQ 231 A static configuration option is available on both ports. 6. Can IP DHCP switch assignment be carried out on a Routed only Port (RoP)? No, RoP is not supported. Only VLAN and OOBM ports can be allocated an address with DHCP. 7. Can an HPE Aruba Networking AOS-CX switch support DHCP IPv6 Allocation? Yes, an HPE Aruba Networking AOS-CX switch can support DHCP IPv6 allocation. However, for Zero Touch Provisioning (ZTP), IPv4 is required. 8.

topic: 4. Which VLAN is assigned a DHCP address?

section: Configuring DHCPv4 and v6 snooping over VXLAN over... 237

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, how do you configure DHCPv4 and v6 snooping over VXLAN over... 237?

To configure DHCPv4 and v6 snooping over VXLAN over... 237, follow the documented procedure: 1. Configure VXLAN overlay setup to establish the VxLAN tunnel. For more information, see AOS-CX 2. Configure DHCPv4 and v6 snooping in the global and VLAN contexts with the commands dhcpv4-s 3. Configure the server connected port as trusted with the commands dhcpv4-snooping trust 4. Validate the DHCPv4 and v6 snooping configuration with the commands show dhcpv4-snoopin. Commands shown in the procedure include: vlan 110.
