Using the show dhcpv4-snooping command, you can see some of the following DHCPv4 information:
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the clear dhcp-snooping statistics command do?
The clear dhcp-snooping statistics command clears all DHCP snooping statistics. Syntax: clear dhcp-snooping statistics.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the clear dhcp-snooping statistics command?
The syntax of the clear dhcp-snooping statistics command is: clear dhcp-snooping statistics.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the dhcp-snooping command?
The syntax of the dhcp-snooping command is: dhcp-snooping.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the dhcp-snooping allow-overwrite-binding command do?

The dhcp-snooping allow-overwrite-binding command allows binding to be overwritten for the same IP address. When enabled, and a DHCP server offers a host an IP address that is already bound to an existing host in the binding table, the existing binding is overwritten for the new host if the new host is successfully able to acquire the same IP address. This overwriting is disabled by default, causing the DHCP server offers to be dropped. The no form of the command disables DHCP snooping overwrite binding. Syntax: dhcp-snooping allow-overwrite-binding.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the dhcp-snooping allow-overwrite-binding command?
The syntax of the dhcp-snooping allow-overwrite-binding command is: dhcp-snooping allow-overwrite-binding.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the dhcp-snooping authorized-server <IP-ADDR> [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command do?

The dhcp-snooping authorized-server <IP-ADDR> [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command adds an authorized (trusted) DHCP server to a list of authorized servers for use by DHCP snooping. This command can be issued multiple times, adding a maximum of 20 authorized servers per VRF. By default, with an empty list of authorized servers, all DHCP servers are considered to be trusted for DHCP snooping purposes. NOTE The mgmt VRF cannot be used with this command. The no form of this command deletes the specified DHCP server from the authorized list. Specifies the IP address of the trusted DHCP server. <IP-ADDR> vrf <VRF-NAME> Specifies the VRF name. Public dhcp-snooping authorized-server 249. Syntax: dhcp-snooping authorized-server <IP-ADDR> [vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the dhcp-snooping authorized-server <IP-ADDR> [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command?
The syntax of the dhcp-snooping authorized-server <IP-ADDR> [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command is: dhcp-snooping authorized-server <IP-ADDR> [vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the dhcp-snooping event-log client command do?

The dhcp-snooping event-log client command this command enables or disables dhcp-snooping client level event logs that help with client telemetry on a remote management station such as HPE Aruba Networking Central. By default, client level event logs are disabled. The no form of this command disables client-level event logs for DHCP snooping after they are enabled. View these logged DHCP snooping events by issuing the command show events -c dhcp-snooping. NOTE For additional information on DHCP-related event logging, please refer to the Event Log Message Reference Guide. Syntax: dhcp-snooping event-log client.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the dhcp-snooping event-log client command?
The syntax of the dhcp-snooping event-log client command is: dhcp-snooping event-log client.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the dhcp-snooping external-storage volume <VOL-NAME> file <FILE-NAME> command do?

The dhcp-snooping external-storage volume <VOL-NAME> file <FILE-NAME> command configures external storage to be used for backing up IP bindings (used by DHCP snooping) to a file. When configured, the switch stores all the IP bindings in an external storage file so that they are retained after the switch restarts. When the switch restarts, it reads the IP bindings from the configured external storage file to populate its local cache. NOTE When both external storage and flash storage are configured to store DHCP snooping IP bindings, the external storage takes priority, and is used exclusively until it becomes unconfigured, at which time flash storage (if configured) is used. Later, if external storage is configured again, flash storage stops and external storage resumes. The no form of this command disables the saving of IP bindings in an external storage file. Syntax: dhcp-snooping external-storage volume <VOL-NAME> file <FILE-NAME>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the dhcp-snooping external-storage volume <VOL-NAME> file <FILE-NAME> command?
The syntax of the dhcp-snooping external-storage volume <VOL-NAME> file <FILE-NAME> command is: dhcp-snooping external-storage volume <VOL-NAME> file <FILE-NAME>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the dhcp-snooping flash-storage [delay <DELAY>] command do?

The dhcp-snooping flash-storage [delay <DELAY>] command configures switch flash storage to be used for backing up client IP bindings (used by DHCP snooping). When flash storage is configured (and external storage is not already configured for this purpose), the switch stores the IP bindings in switch flash storage. When the switch restarts, it reads the IP bindings from the switch flash storage to populate its local cache. Writing the IP bindings to flash storage only occurs after the configured delay and if there has been a change in client IP bindings. Writing is skipped when client IP bindings have not changed since the previous write. Omitting delay <DELAY> sets the default delay of 900 seconds. Syntax: dhcp-snooping flash-storage [delay <DELAY>].
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the dhcp-snooping flash-storage [delay <DELAY>] command?
The syntax of the dhcp-snooping flash-storage [delay <DELAY>] command is: dhcp-snooping flash-storage [delay <DELAY>]
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the dhcp-snooping max-bindings <MAX-BINDINGS> command do?

The dhcp-snooping max-bindings <MAX-BINDINGS> command sets the maximum number of DHCP bindings allowed on the selected interface. For all interfaces on which this command is not run, the default max binding is the maximum value of the range. The no form of the command reverts max bindings for the selected interface to its default. Specifies the maximum number of DHCP bindings. Range: 1 to 2000. Range 1 to 2048 <MAX-BINDINGS>. Syntax: dhcp-snooping max-bindings <MAX-BINDINGS>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the dhcp-snooping max-bindings <MAX-BINDINGS> command?
The syntax of the dhcp-snooping max-bindings <MAX-BINDINGS> command is: dhcp-snooping max-bindings <MAX-BINDINGS>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the dhcp-snooping static-attributes command do?

The dhcp-snooping static-attributes command enables storage of static attributes provided to the DHCP client by DHCP server during DHCP packet exchange. Disabled by default. When enabled, the following attributes are stored in OVSDB along with the client IP binding entry: 1. Name server IP addresses: DNS server IP s provided by the DHCP server to the client. Maximum: 3 per client. 2. Default gateway IP address: Router IP addresses provided by DHCP server to the client. Maximum: 3 per client. 3. Server IP address: IP address of the DHCP server that leased the IP to the client. Public dhcp-snooping static-attributes 259 The no form of the command disables storing of client static attributes. After disabling, existing client static attributes will be flushed. Syntax: dhcp-snooping static-attributes.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the dhcp-snooping static-attributes command?
The syntax of the dhcp-snooping static-attributes command is: dhcp-snooping static-attributes.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the dhcp-snooping trust command do?

The dhcp-snooping trust command enables DHCP snooping trust on the selected port. Only server packets received on trusted ports are forwarded. All the ports are untrusted by default. The no form of the command disables DHCP snooping trust on the selected port. Syntax: dhcp-snooping trust.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the dhcp-snooping trust command?
The syntax of the dhcp-snooping trust command is: dhcp-snooping trust.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the dhcp-snooping tunnel vxlan trust command do?

The dhcp-snooping tunnel vxlan trust command enables dhcp-snooping trust on all VxLAN tunnels. The no form of the command to marks all VxLAN tunnels as untrusted. By default, all VxLAN tunnel interfaces are trusted. When trust is disabled on VxLAN tunnel interfaces: • DHCP broadcast packets are not forwarded on VxLAN tunnels. • DHCP server packets received on VxLAN tunnel interfaces are discarded. Syntax: dhcp-snooping tunnel vxlan trust.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the dhcp-snooping tunnel vxlan trust command?
The syntax of the dhcp-snooping tunnel vxlan trust command is: dhcp-snooping tunnel vxlan trust.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the dhcp-snooping verify mac command do?

The dhcp-snooping verify mac command this command enables verification of the hardware address field in DHCP client packets. When enabled, the DHCP client hardware address field and the source MAC address must be the same for packets received on untrusted ports or else the packet is dropped. This DHCP snooping MAC verification is enabled by default. The no form of the command disables DHCP snooping MAC verification. Syntax: dhcp-snooping verify mac.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the dhcp-snooping verify mac command?
The syntax of the dhcp-snooping verify mac command is: dhcp-snooping verify mac.

The syntax of the clear dhcpv6-snooping statistics command is: clear dhcpv6-snooping statistics.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the dhcpv6-snooping command?
The syntax of the dhcpv6-snooping command is: dhcpv6-snooping.

The dhcpv6-snooping guard-policy <POLICY-NAME> command configures a DHCPv6 snooping guard policy with the given name and enters the guard policy configuration context. The no form of the command disables the specified guard policy. Specifies the name of the DHCPv6 snooping guard policy. Maxi mum length: 64. <POLICY-NAME> Public dhcpv6-snooping guard-policy 276. The dhcpv6-snooping authorized-server <IPv6-ADDR> [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command adds an authorized (trusted) DHCPv6 server to a list of authorized servers for use by DHCPv6 snooping. This command can be issued multiple times, adding a maximum of 20 authorized servers per VRF. By Public dhcpv6-snooping authorized-server 279 default, with an empty list of authorized servers, all DHCPv6 servers are considered to be trusted for DHCPv6 snooping purposes. NOTE The mgmt VRF cannot be used with this command. NOTE Configure the link local IPv6 address instead of global IPv6 address of the DHCPv6 server as the authorized-server. For example: switch(config)# dhcpv6-snooping authorized-server fe80: : 2ca4: fa40: d4cd: bc2f The no form of this command deletes the specified DHCPv6 server from the authorized list. Specifies the IPv6 address of the trusted DHCPv6 server. Syntax: dhcpv6-snooping authorized-server <IPv6-ADDR> [vrf <VRF-NAME>].

The syntax of the dhcpv6-snooping authorized-server <IPv6-ADDR> [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command is: dhcpv6-snooping authorized-server <IPv6-ADDR> [vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the dhcpv6-snooping event-log client command do?

The dhcpv6-snooping event-log client command this command enables or disables DHCPv6 snooping client level event logs that help with client telemetry on a remote management station such as HPE Aruba Networking Central. By default, client level event logs are disabled. The no form of this command disables client-level event logs for DHCPv6 snooping after they are enabled. View these logged DHCPv6 snooping events by issuing the command show events -c dhcpv6-snooping. NOTE For additional information on DHCP-related event logging, please refer to the Event Log Message Reference Guide. Public dhcpv6-snooping event-log client 281. The syntax of the dhcpv6-snooping event-log client command is: dhcpv6-snooping event-log client.

Later, if external storage is configured again, flash storage stops and external storage resumes. Syntax: dhcpv6-snooping external-storage volume <VOL-NAME> file <FILE-NAME>.

Writing the IP bindings to flash storage only occurs after the configured delay and if there has been a change in client IP bindings. Writing is skipped when client IP bindings have not changed since the previous write. Public dhcpv6-snooping flash-storage 284 Omitting delay <DELAY> sets the default delay of 900 seconds. Syntax: dhcpv6-snooping flash-storage [delay <DELAY>].

For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the match client prefix-list <PREFIX-LIST-NAME> command do?

The match client prefix-list <PREFIX-LIST-NAME> command configures a prefix-list for the DHCPv6 snooping guard policy enabling the policy to allow the assigned IPv6 addresses within a specific prefix range. The no form of the command removes a prefix list from the DHCPv6 snooping guard policy. Specifies the name of the IPv6 prefix list. <PREFIX-LIST-NAME>. Syntax: match client prefix-list <PREFIX-LIST-NAME>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the match client prefix-list <PREFIX-LIST-NAME> command?
The syntax of the match client prefix-list <PREFIX-LIST-NAME> command is: match client prefix-list <PREFIX-LIST-NAME>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the match server access-list <ACL-NAME> command do?

The match server access-list <ACL-NAME> command configures an access list to a DHCPv6 snooping guard policy, enabling the DHCPv6 snooping guard policy to allow or deny the specific DHCP server to assign an IPv6 address. If no filters are applied, DHCP server traffic from any source IP address is allowed in the trusted port. The no form of the command removes the specified access list from the DHCPv6 snooping guard policy. Specifies the name of the IPv6 access list to be matched. Public match server access-list 291. Syntax: match server access-list <ACL-NAME>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the match server access-list <ACL-NAME> command?
The syntax of the match server access-list <ACL-NAME> command is: match server access-list <ACL-NAME>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the preference [minimum | maximum ] <VALUE> command do?

The preference [minimum | maximum ] <VALUE> command enables a DHCPv6 snooping guard policy to allow or deny the DHCPv6 servers in the specified server preference range. If not configured the minimum preference is set to 0 and maximum preference is set to 255. The no form of the command removes the server preference limits on the specified DHCPv6 snooping guard policy. minimum <VALUE> maximum <VALUE>. Syntax: preference [minimum | maximum ] <VALUE>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the preference [minimum | maximum ] <VALUE> command?
The syntax of the preference [minimum | maximum ] <VALUE> command is: preference [minimum | maximum ] <VALUE>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show dhcpv6-snooping guard-policy [vlan <VLAN-ID>] [vsx-peer] command do?

The show dhcpv6-snooping guard-policy [vlan <VLAN-ID>] [vsx-peer] command shows the DHCPv6 snooping guard policy configuration and statistics for the specified VLAN. Public show dhcpv6-snooping guard-policy vlan 297 Specifies the VLAN ID for which the DHCPv6 guard counter in formation is displayed.

section: 5. Server and client are connected to same the VLAN

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the guide say about 5. Server and client are connected to same the VLAN?

5. Server and client are connected to same the VLAN: a. Check if the server is reachable on the same VLAN as the client. b. If the server is on a different L3 network, configure DHCP-Relay to forward the packets between the client and server. 6. Authorized server configurations-When configured, the server assigning IP address should be one of the IP addresses from the authorized server list. In DHCP snooping IPv6, if DHCP Relay is between a test device and a server, the authorised server configuration should include a link to the local IP address of the relay interface. This is because, while forwarding the server's response to the client, it will stamp a link to the local IP address as the source IP of the packet.

topic: 5. Server and client are connected to same the VLAN

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the guide say about IP-Binding mismatch in VSX topologies?

IP-Binding mismatch in VSX topologies: 1. Both primary and secondary VSX peers should have the same clock value. If not, there is a possibility of inconsistent ip-bindings that could result in ip-binding sync in a loop. There is no straight-forward way to check the inconsistencies but by looking at the number of bindings and debug log analysis 2. Ensure the ISL state is up and operational. DHCP client IP traffic is dropped at data path 1. This can happen when IP-Source-Lockdown is used along with DHCP snooping. Ensure IP-Bindings are learned for clients that are experiencing traffic failure issues 2. Check the hardware programming state of the IP-Binding entry by IP-Source-Lockdown. Use show i pv4 source-lockdown bindings or show IPv6 source-lockdown bindings commands. Public.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the http-proxy {<FQDN | IPv4-ADDR> | IPv6-ADDR[: PORT]} [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command do?

The http-proxy {<FQDN | IPv4-ADDR> | IPv6-ADDR[: PORT]} [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command specifies HTTP proxy location and VRF. When HTTP proxy location and VRF are configured on the switch, it overrides any existing HTTP proxy location and VRF as this has the highest priority over the values obtained from other sources. Following locations can be used for the HTTP proxy location: • A fully qualified domain name (FQDN). • An IPv4 address with colon separated port number • An IPv6 address with colon separated port number NOTE When configuring an IPv6 address with a port number, the address must be specified inside square brackets. An example - [2001: 0db8: 85a3: 0000: 0000: 8a2e: 0370: 7334]: 8080. Public DHCP Options commands 307 If the command is entered without the VRF parameter, then the VRF used will be 'default' VRF. Syntax: http-proxy {<FQDN | IPv4-ADDR> | IPv6-ADDR[: PORT]} [vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the http-proxy {<FQDN | IPv4-ADDR> | IPv6-ADDR[: PORT]} [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command?
The syntax of the http-proxy {<FQDN | IPv4-ADDR> | IPv6-ADDR[: PORT]} [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command is: http-proxy {<FQDN | IPv4-ADDR> | IPv6-ADDR[: PORT]} [vrf <VRF-NAME>]

section: Configuring ND snooping over VxLAN with IPv4 and IPv6 underlay

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, how do you configure ND snooping over VxLAN with IPv4 and IPv6 underlay?

To configure ND snooping over VxLAN with IPv4 and IPv6 underlay, follow the documented procedure: 1. Configure VXLAN overlay setup to establish the VxLAN tunnel. For more information, see AOS-CX 2. Configure or enable Nd-snooping at the global and VLAN contexts with the commands Nd-snoopin 3. Configure the server connected port as trusted with the command Nd-snooping trust.

task: Configuring ND snooping over VxLAN with IPv4 and IPv6 underlay

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the clear nd-snooping statistics command do?
The clear nd-snooping statistics command clears all ND snooping statistics. Syntax: clear nd-snooping statistics.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the clear nd-snooping statistics command?
The syntax of the clear nd-snooping statistics command is: clear nd-snooping statistics.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the diag-dump nd-snooping basic command do?
The diag-dump nd-snooping basic command this command displays information about the ND-Snooping configuration and runtime context. Syntax: diag-dump nd-snooping basic.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the diag-dump nd-snooping basic command?
The syntax of the diag-dump nd-snooping basic command is: diag-dump nd-snooping basic.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the nd-snooping command?
The syntax of the nd-snooping command is: nd-snooping.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the nd-snooping mac-check command do?

The nd-snooping mac-check command this command enables verification of the hardware address field in ND snooping packets. When enabled, the ICMPv6 target link layer address field and the source MAC address must be the same for packets received on untrusted ports or else the packets are dropped. This ND snooping MAC verification is enabled by default. The no form of the command disables ND snooping MAC verification. Syntax: nd-snooping mac-check.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the nd-snooping mac-check command?
The syntax of the nd-snooping mac-check command is: nd-snooping mac-check.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the nd-snooping prefix-list <IPv6-ADDR> command do?

The nd-snooping prefix-list <IPv6-ADDR> command configures the ND snooping prefix list for the selected VLAN and the specified IPv6 address prefix. ND snooping must be enabled both globally and on this VLAN before this prefix list configuration takes effect. The no form of this command removes the prefix list configuration for the selected VLAN and IPv6 address. Public nd-snooping prefix-list 323 Specifies the IPv6 address. <IPv6-ADDR>. Syntax: nd-snooping prefix-list <IPv6-ADDR>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the nd-snooping prefix-list <IPv6-ADDR> command?
The syntax of the nd-snooping prefix-list <IPv6-ADDR> command is: nd-snooping prefix-list <IPv6-ADDR>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the nd-snooping max-bindings <MAX-BINDINGS> command do?

The nd-snooping max-bindings <MAX-BINDINGS> command sets the maximum number of ND bindings allowed on the selected interface. For all interfaces on which this command is not run, the default max bindings applies. The no form of the command reverts max bindings for the selected interface to its default. Specifies the maximum number of ND bindings. You can use the show capacities command to see the maximum available for yo ur switch model. <MAX-BINDINGS>. Syntax: nd-snooping max-bindings <MAX-BINDINGS>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the nd-snooping max-bindings <MAX-BINDINGS> command?
The syntax of the nd-snooping max-bindings <MAX-BINDINGS> command is: nd-snooping max-bindings <MAX-BINDINGS>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the nd-snooping ra-drop command do?

The nd-snooping ra-drop command this command enables Routing Advertisement (RA) drop on the selected VLAN. When enabled, ingress RA packets on the selected VLAN are blocked on both trusted and untrusted ports. When disabled, RA packets are forwarded on the selected VLAN with ND snooping trusted port validation. RA drop is disabled by default. NOTE ND snooping must be enabled in both the config context and the config-vlan context before this command can be used. The no form of the command disables ND snooping RA drop on the selected VLAN. Syntax: nd-snooping ra-drop.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the nd-snooping ra-drop command?
The syntax of the nd-snooping ra-drop command is: nd-snooping ra-drop.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the nd-snooping trust command do?

The nd-snooping trust command enables ND snooping trust on the selected interface (port) allowing RA and RR packets to be received. RA and RR packets received on untrusted ports are discarded, all ports are untrusted by default. The no form of the command disables ND snooping trust on the selected port. Syntax: nd-snooping trust.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the nd-snooping trust command?
The syntax of the nd-snooping trust command is: nd-snooping trust.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show nd-snooping [vlan <VLAN-ID>] [vsx-peer] command do?
The show nd-snooping [vlan <VLAN-ID>] [vsx-peer] command shows either all ND snooping configuration or the configuration for the specified VLAN. Public show nd-snooping 331.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv6 nd-snooping ra-guard policy command do?

The IPv6 nd-snooping ra-guard policy command creates the Router Advertisement (RA) guard policy with the given name and enters the RA guard policy configuration context. The no form of the command removes the specified RA guard policy from the switch. NOTE ND snooping must be enabled in both the global context and the config-vlan context before this command can be used. Specifies the name of the RA guard policy. Maximum length: 64. <POLICY-NAME> Public IPv6 nd-snooping ra-guard policy 341. Syntax: IPv6 nd-snooping ra-guard policy <POLICY-NAME>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the IPv6 nd-snooping ra-guard policy command?
The syntax of the IPv6 nd-snooping ra-guard policy command is: IPv6 nd-snooping ra-guard policy <POLICY-NAME>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the nd-snooping ra-guard attach-policy <POLICY-NAME> command do?

The nd-snooping ra-guard attach-policy <POLICY-NAME> command applies the created RA guard policy to a specific L2 port or VLAN. The no form of the command detaches the specified RA guard policy from the L2 port or VLAN. Specifies the name of the RA guard policy. <POLICY-NAME>. Syntax: nd-snooping ra-guard attach-policy <POLICY-NAME>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the nd-snooping ra-guard attach-policy <POLICY-NAME> command?
The syntax of the nd-snooping ra-guard attach-policy <POLICY-NAME> command is: nd-snooping ra-guard attach-policy <POLICY-NAME>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the other-config-flag [on | off] command do?

The other-config-flag [on | off] command enables the verification of the advertised other configuration flag. Verifies that the advertised Other Stateful Configuration flag is On or Off based on the configured value. The no form of the command disables other configuration flag verification. NOTE ND snooping must be enabled in both the global context and the config-vlan context before this command can be used. on. Syntax: other-config-flag [on | off].
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the other-config-flag [on | off] command?
The syntax of the other-config-flag [on | off] command is: other-config-flag [on | off]
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show IPv6 destination-guard statistics {vlan <VLAN-ID>} command do?
The show IPv6 destination-guard statistics {vlan <VLAN-ID>} command shows IPv6 destination guard statistics for the specified VLAN. Public show IPv6 destination-guard statistics vlan 358.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the clear IPv6 destination-guard statistics vlan <VLAN-ID> command do?
The clear IPv6 destination-guard statistics vlan <VLAN-ID> command clears IPv6 destination guard statistics from the specified VLAN.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the destination ip <IPv4-ADDR> command do?

The destination ip <IPv4-ADDR> command sets the destination IP address for an IP tunnel. Specify the address of the interface on the remote device to which the tunnel will be established. The no form of this command deletes the destination IP address from an IP tunnel. <IPv4-ADDR> Specifies the destination IP address in IPv4 format (x.x.x.x), where x is a decimal number from 0 to 255. Syntax: destination ip <IPv4-ADDR>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the destination ip <IPv4-ADDR> command?
The syntax of the destination ip <IPv4-ADDR> command is: destination ip <IPv4-ADDR>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the destination IPv6 <IPVv6-ADDR> command?
The syntax of the destination IPv6 <IPVv6-ADDR> command is: destination IPv6 <IPVv6-ADDR>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv6 address command do?

The IPv6 address command sets the local IP address of an IPv6 to IPv4 tunnel or of an IPv6 to IPv6 tunnel. This address identifies the tunnel interface for routing. It must be on the same subnet as the tunnel address assigned on the remote device. The no form of this command deletes the local IP address assigned to an IPv6 to IPv4 tunnel. Specifies the tunnel IP address in IPv6 format (xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx), where x is a hexadecimal numbe r from 0 to F. Specifies the number of bits in the address mask in CIDR format (x), where x is a decimal number from 0 to 32. <IPv6-ADDR> <MASK>. Syntax: IPv6 address <IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the IPv6 address command?
The syntax of the IPv6 address command is: IPv6 address <IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ip mtu command do?

The ip mtu command sets the MTU (maximum transmission unit) for an IP interface. The default value is 1500 bytes. The no form of this command sets the MTU to the default value of 1500 bytes. Specifies the MTU in bytes. Range: 1, 280 bytes to 9, 192 bytes. <VALUE>. Syntax: ip mtu <VALUE>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the ip mtu command?
The syntax of the ip mtu command is: ip mtu <VALUE>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the source ip <IPv4-ADDR> command do?

The source ip <IPv4-ADDR> command sets the source IP address for an IP tunnel. Specify the IP address of a layer 3 interface on the switch. Tunnels can have the same source IP address and different destination IP addresses. The no form of this command deletes the source IP address for an IP tunnel. Specifies the source IP address in IPv4 format (x.x.x.x), where x is a decimal number from 0 to 255. <IPv4-ADDR>. Syntax: source ip <IPv4-ADDR>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the source ip <IPv4-ADDR> command?
The syntax of the source ip <IPv4-ADDR> command is: source ip <IPv4-ADDR>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the source IPv6 <IPv6-ADDR> command do?

The source IPv6 <IPv6-ADDR> command sets the source IPv6 address to be used for the encapsulation. The no form of this command deletes the source IPv6 address for an IP tunnel. Specifies the tunnel IP address in IPv6 format (xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx), where x is a hexadecimal numbe r from 0 to F. This is optional in the no form of the command. <IPv6-ADDR>. Syntax: source IPv6 <IPv6-ADDR>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the source IPv6 <IPv6-ADDR> command?
The syntax of the source IPv6 <IPv6-ADDR> command is: source IPv6 <IPv6-ADDR>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the transport vrf <VRF-NAME> command do?

The transport vrf <VRF-NAME> command indicates the transport VRF of the tunnel. This VRF is the same as the VRF associated with the underlay interface over which the tunnel sends or receives encapsulated packets. If not configured, VRF Aware feature is not enabled. The no form of this command removes the transport VRF from the tunnel. Specifies the transport VRF of the tunnel. <VRF-NAME>. Syntax: transport vrf <VRF-NAME>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the transport vrf <VRF-NAME> command?
The syntax of the transport vrf <VRF-NAME> command is: transport vrf <VRF-NAME>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ttl <COUNT> command do?

The ttl <COUNT> command sets the TTL (time-to-live), also known as the hop count, for tunneled packets. If not configured, the default value of 64 is used for the tunnel. (The hop count of the original packets is not changed.) A maximum of four different TTL values can be used at the same time by all tunnels on the switch. For example, if tunnel-1 has TTL 10, tunnel-2 has TTL 20, tunnel-3 has TTL 30, and tunnel-4 has TTL 40, then tunnel-5 cannot have a unique TTL value, it must reuse one of the values assigned to the other tunnels (10, 20, 30, 40). Public ttl 401 The no form of this command sets TTL to the default value of 64. Specifies the hop count. Range: 1 to 255. Default: 64. <COUNT>. Syntax: ttl <COUNT>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the ttl <COUNT> command?
The syntax of the ttl <COUNT> command is: ttl <COUNT>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the vrf attach command do?

The vrf attach command assigns an IP tunnel to a VRF. By default, all tunnels are automatically assigned to the default VRF when they are created. The no form of this command assigns a tunnel to the default VRF (default). Specifies the VRF name to which to assign the tunnel. <VRF-NAME>. Syntax: vrf attach <VRF-NAME>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the vrf attach command?
The syntax of the vrf attach command is: vrf attach <VRF-NAME>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show capacities-status tunneling command do?

The show capacities-status tunneling command showing system capacities status and their values for IPsec IPv4, GRE IPv4, IPv6 in IPv4, and IPv6 in IPv6 features. Syntax: show capacities-status tunneling.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show capacities-status tunneling command?
The syntax of the show capacities-status tunneling command is: show capacities-status tunneling.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show capacities-status command do?
The show capacities-status command showing system capacities status and their values at the system level. Syntax: show capacities-status.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show capacities-status command?
The syntax of the show capacities-status command is: show capacities-status.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show interface tunnel brief command do?

The show interface tunnel brief command shows the brief tunnel information such as tunnel interface names and link state. Specifies the number of an IP tunnel. Range: 1 to 127. <TUNNEL-NUMBER>. Syntax: show interface tunnel [<TUNNEL-NUMBER>] brief.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show interface tunnel brief command?
The syntax of the show interface tunnel brief command is: show interface tunnel [<TUNNEL-NUMBER>] brief.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the no IPv4 source-binding <VLAN-ID> command do?

The no IPv4 source-binding <VLAN-ID> command adds static IPv4 client source binding information to the switch IP binding database. Although DHCPv4 snooping is often used to dynamically populate the binding database, this command is available for manually adding entries to the switch IP binding database. NOTE Statically configured IP binding information supersedes any dynamically collected binding information for the same client. The no form of this command removes the specified binding that was statically configured with the IPv4 source-binding command. The no form has no effect on bindings that were dynamically configured with DHCPv4 snooping. Specifies the ID of an existing VLAN on which the client is conn ected. Range: 1 to 4094.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv4 source-lockdown command do?

The IPv4 source-lockdown command enables IPv4 source lockdown for all VLANs on the selected interface (port). The no form of this command disables IPv4 source lockdown for the selected interface (port). CAUTION This configuration will disable flow tracking statistics collection. Syntax: IPv4 source-lockdown.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the IPv4 source-lockdown command?
The syntax of the IPv4 source-lockdown command is: IPv4 source-lockdown.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show IPv4 source-binding command do?

The show IPv4 source-binding command shows all IPv4 static source binding information irrespective of source lockdown configuration.. Shows the output from the VSX peer switch. If the switches do not have the VSX configuration or the ISL is down, the output f rom the VSX peer switch is not displayed. This parameter is avai lable on switches that support VSX. Syntax: show IPv4 source-binding [vsx-peer].
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show IPv4 source-binding command?
The syntax of the show IPv4 source-binding command is: show IPv4 source-binding [vsx-peer]
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the guide say about VLAN Id: 12?

VLAN Id: 12: MAC address: 00: 50: 56: 96: d8: 3d IP Address: 120.168.76.182 Time Remaining: static Lockdown Status: Yes Public show IPv4 source-lockdown 431 Hardware Status: Yes Hardware Error Reason: -- Showing the detailed binding record and related information for interface 1/1/2: switch# show IPv4 source-lockdown binding interface 1/1/2.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the guide say about VLAN Id: 2000?

VLAN Id: 2000: MAC address: 00: 50: 56: 96: e4: cf IP Address: 192.168.142.113 Time Remaining: static Lockdown Status: Yes Hardware Status: Yes Hardware Error Reason: -- Public show IPv4 source-lockdown 432 Showing the detailed binding record and related information for interface 1/1/2 (identified in this example command by the VLAN): switch# show IPv4 source-lockdown binding vlan 100.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the no IPv6 source-binding <VLAN-ID> command do?

The no IPv6 source-binding <VLAN-ID> command adds static IPv6 client source binding information to the switch IPv6 binding database. Although DHCPv6 snooping is often used to dynamically populate the binding database, this command is available for manually adding entries to the switch IPv6 binding database. NOTE Statically configured IPv6 binding information supersedes any dynamically collected binding information for the same client. The no form of this command removes the specified binding that was statically configured with the IPv6 source-binding command. The no form has no effect on bindings that were dynamically configured with DHCPv6 snooping. Specifies the ID of an existing VLAN on which the client is conn ected. Range: 1 to 4094.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv6 source-lockdown command do?

The IPv6 source-lockdown command enables IPv6 source lockdown for all VLANs on the selected interface (port). The no form of this command disables IPv6 source lockdown for the selected interface (port). CAUTION This configuration will disable flow tracking statistics collection. Syntax: IPv6 source-lockdown.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the IPv6 source-lockdown command?
The syntax of the IPv6 source-lockdown command is: IPv6 source-lockdown.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv6 source-lockdown hardware retry <VLAN-ID> command do?

The IPv6 source-lockdown hardware retry <VLAN-ID> command retries the IPv6 source lockdown hardware programming for a client identified by VLAN and IPv6 address. Specifies the ID of an existing VLAN on which the client is conn ected. Range: 1 to 4094.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show IPv6 source-binding command do?

The show IPv6 source-binding command shows all IPv6 static source binding information irrespective of source lockdown configuration. Shows the output from the VSX peer switch. If the switches do not have the VSX configuration or the ISL is down, the output f rom the VSX peer switch is not displayed. This parameter is avai lable on switches that support VSX. Syntax: show IPv6 source-binding [vsx-peer].
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show IPv6 source-binding command?
The syntax of the show IPv6 source-binding command is: show IPv6 source-binding [vsx-peer]
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, how do you configure the DNS client?

To configure the DNS client, follow the documented procedure: 1. Configure one or more DNS name servers with the command ip dns server 2. To resolve DNS requests by appending a domain name to the requests, either configure a single domain 3. To use static name resolution for certain hosts, associate an IP address to a host with the command ip 4. Review your DNS configuration settings with the command show ip dns. Commands shown in the procedure include: VRF Name: default; VRF Name: mainvrf.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ip dns domain-name command do?

The ip dns domain-name command configures a domain name that is appended to the DNS request. The domain can be either IPv4 or IPv6. By default, requests are forwarded on the default VRF. If a domain list is defined with the command ip dns domain-list, the domain name defined with this command is ignored. The no form of this command removes the domain name. Public ip dns domain-name 454 | Parameter | | Description | | | | Specifies the domain name to append to DNS requests. Length: 1 to 256 characters. <DOMAIN-NAME> | vrf <VRF-NAME> | | Specifies a VRF name. Default: default. Syntax: ip dns domain-name <DOMAIN-NAME> [ vrf <VRF-NAME> ].
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the ip dns domain-name command?
The syntax of the ip dns domain-name command is: ip dns domain-name <DOMAIN-NAME> [ vrf <VRF-NAME> ]
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ip dns host command do?

The ip dns host command associates a static IP address with a hostname. The DNS client returns this IP address instead of querying a DNS server for an IP address for the hostname. Up to six hosts can be defined. If no VRF is defined, the default VRF is used. The no form of this command removes a static IP address associated with a hostname. host <HOST-NAME> Specifies the name of a host. Length: 1 to 256 characters. <IP-ADDR> Specifies an IP address in IPv4 format (x.x.x.x), where x is a de cimal number from 0 to 255, or IPv6 format (xxxx: xxxx: x xxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx: xxxx), where x is a hexa decimal number from 0 to F. vrf <VRF-NAME> Specifies a VRF name. Default: default. Syntax: ip dns host <HOST-NAME>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the ip dns host command?
The syntax of the ip dns host command is: ip dns host <HOST-NAME>

section: Step 2: Configuring L3 gateway in switch A

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the guide say about Step 2: Configuring L3 gateway in switch A?

Step 2: Configuring L3 gateway in switch A: L3-GW(config)# vlan 100 L3-GW(config-vlan-100)# exit L3-GW(config)# interface 1/1/25 L3-GW(config-if)# vlan trunk allowed 100 L3-GW(config-if)# no shutdown L3-GW(config-if)# exit L3-GW(config)# interface vlan 100 L3-GW(config-if-vlan)# ip address 192.168.1.1/24 Now the clients will be able to ping the gateway IP; but will not be able to communicate with each other due to the isolation enforced with private VLAN. As expected, the ping from client 1 to client 2 will fail in the above example, although the clients are in the same subnet.

topic: Step 2: Configuring L3 gateway in switch A

section: Step 3: Configuring local proxy ARP in switch A

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the guide say about Step 3: Configuring local proxy ARP in switch A?

Step 3: Configuring local proxy ARP in switch A: If local proxy ARP is enabled in the L3 gateway, the L3 gateway responds to ARP requests for addresses in the same subnet. In other words, the L3 gateway will be doing proxy for ARP resolution. L3-GW(config)# no ip icmp redirect L3-GW(config)# interface vlan 100 L3-GW(config-if-vlan)# ip local-proxy-arp With this, the L3 communication will be enabled between client 1 and client 2. Now the ping from client 1 to client 2 will be successful in the above example. The ARP table in client 1 will be populated with the MAC of L3 gateway; not the MAC of client 2. Similar ARP table entry for client 2 as well. In other words, the L3 gateway is doing proxy for ARP resolution for the local network where clients are isolated at layer 2. Dynamic ARP inspection Public ARP 471 Dynamic arp inspection is provided as a mechanism for making ARP more secure. NOTE Dynamic ARP inspection is available on the 8325/8325H/8325P and 10000 Switch Series.

topic: Step 3: Configuring local proxy ARP in switch A

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, how do you configure proxy ARP?

To configure proxy ARP, follow the documented procedure: 1. Switch to configuration context with the command config 2. Switch to an interface with the command interface, or to an interface VLAN with the command i 3. Enable local proxy ARP with the command ip proxy-arp.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, how do you configure local proxy ARP?

To configure local proxy ARP, follow the documented procedure: 1. Switch to configuration context with the command config 2. Switch to an interface with the command interface, or to an interface VLAN with the command i 3. Enable local proxy ARP with the command ip local-proxy-arp.

section: Configuring dynamic ARP inspection over VXLAN overlay

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, how do you configure dynamic ARP inspection over VXLAN overlay?

To configure dynamic ARP inspection over VXLAN overlay, follow the documented procedure: 1. Configure VXLAN overlay setup to establish the VxLAN tunnel. For more information, see AOS-CX 2. Configure ARP inspection in the VLAN context with the command arp inspection 3. Configure the port as trusted or untrusted with the command arp inspection trust 4. Validate the ARP inspection configuration with the command show arp inspection.

task: Configuring dynamic ARP inspection over VXLAN overlay

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the arp inspection trust command do?

The arp inspection trust command configures the interface as a trusted. All interfaces are untrusted by default. Public arp inspection trust 477 The no form of this command returns the interface to the default state (untrusted). Syntax: arp inspection trust.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the arp inspection trust command?
The syntax of the arp inspection trust command is: arp inspection trust.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the arp ip <IP_ADDR> mac <MAC_ADDR> command do?

The arp ip <IP_ADDR> mac <MAC_ADDR> command specifies a permanent static neighbor entry in the ARP table (for IPv4 neighbors). The no form of this command deletes a permanent static neighbor entry from the ARP table. Public arp ip 478 Specifies the IP address of the neighbor or the virtual IP addres s of the cluster in IP format (x.x.x.x), where x is a decimal num ber from 0 to 255.. Range: 4096 to 131072. Default: 131072. Specifies the MAC address of the neighbor or the multicast M AC address in IANA format (xx: xx: xx: xx: xx: xx), where x is a h exadecimal number from 0 to F. Range: 4096 to 131072. Defaul t: 131072. Syntax: arp ip <IP_ADDR> mac <MAC_ADDR>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the arp ip <IP_ADDR> mac <MAC_ADDR> command?
The syntax of the arp ip <IP_ADDR> mac <MAC_ADDR> command is: arp ip <IP_ADDR> mac <MAC_ADDR>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the arp process-grat-arp command do?

The arp process-grat-arp command enables the processing of gratuitous ARP packets on the individual port or group of L3 ports together. By default, the gratuitous ARP processing is enabled. When gratuitous ARP (GARP) processing is enabled, a switch that is advertising any changes in its MAC through the GARP will reflect in the neighbor table of the switch. However, the switch will not be able to learn the neighbor through the GARP. This configuration is applicable only on L3 interfaces such as ROPs, subinterfaces, and SVIs. The no form of this command disables the processing of gratuitous ARP packets. Syntax: arp process-grat-arp.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the arp process-grat-arp command?
The syntax of the arp process-grat-arp command is: arp process-grat-arp.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the debug arp-security <LOG-CATEGORY> [severity <LEVEL>] command do?

The debug arp-security <LOG-CATEGORY> [severity <LEVEL>] command enables ARP security debug logs. If <SEVERITY> is omitted, all severities are logged. The no form of this command disables ARP security debug logs. <LOG-CATEGORY> severity <LEVEL> Selects the ARP security debug log category. Available categori es are: • all: Selects all ARP security debug log categories. • config: Selects the ARP security config debug log category. inspection: Selects the ARP security inspection debug log • category. • packet: Selects the ARP security packet debug log categor y. Specifies how to filter the ARP security debug logging by settin g the minimum severity level for which debug logging will be pe rformed. The selected severity level and all severities above (m ore severe) will be included in the logging. Syntax: debug arp-security <LOG-CATEGORY> [severity <LEVEL>].
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the debug arp-security <LOG-CATEGORY> [severity <LEVEL>] command?
The syntax of the debug arp-security <LOG-CATEGORY> [severity <LEVEL>] command is: debug arp-security <LOG-CATEGORY> [severity <LEVEL>]
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ip local-proxy-arp exclude command do?

The ip local-proxy-arp exclude command enables exemption of a specific IP address from local proxy ARP for the specified interface. This allows specified IP addresses to bypass local proxy ARP processing on the interface. The maximum IP addresses can be exempted from Local Proxy ARP per interface are 256. The maximum IP addresses can be exempted from Local proxy ARP across all interfaces are 256. The no version of this command disables exemption of a specific IP address from local proxy ARP for the specified interface, while local proxy ARP remains configured on the interface. This allows specified IP addresses to be processed by local proxy ARP on the interface. IP address to exclude from local proxy ARP. Syntax: ip local-proxy-arp exclude {<ip-address>}.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the ip local-proxy-arp exclude command?
The syntax of the ip local-proxy-arp exclude command is: ip local-proxy-arp exclude {<ip-address>}.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the IPv6 local-proxy-nd exclude command do?

The IPv6 local-proxy-nd exclude command enables IP exemption for local proxy ND for the specified interface and IPv6 address. When configured, the switch will not respond to neighbor solicitation (NS) messages for the specified IPv6 address. The maximum IPv6 addresses can be exempted from Local Proxy ND per interface are 256. The maximum IPv6 addresses can be exempted from Local proxy ND across all interfaces are 256. The no version of this command disables IP exemption for local proxy ND for the specified interface and IPv6 address, while local proxy ND remains configured on the interface. After executing this command, the switch will respond to neighbor solicitation (NS) messages for the specified IPv6 address. IPv6 address to exclude from local proxy ND. Syntax: IPv6 local-proxy-nd exclude {<IPv6-address>}.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the IPv6 local-proxy-nd exclude command?
The syntax of the IPv6 local-proxy-nd exclude command is: IPv6 local-proxy-nd exclude {<IPv6-address>}.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show arp inspection interface [<IFNAME>] [vlan <VLAN-ID>] [vsx-peer] command do?
The show arp inspection interface [<IFNAME>] [vlan <VLAN-ID>] [vsx-peer] command shows the current configuration of dynamic ARP inspection on an interface.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show arp inspection statistics vlan [<VLAN-ID>] [vsx-peer] command do?

The show arp inspection statistics vlan [<VLAN-ID>] [vsx-peer] command shows statistics about forwarded and dropped ARP packets. When <VLAN-ID> is not specified, information is shown for all configured VLANs.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show arp inspection vlan [<VLAN-ID>] [vsx-peer] command do?

The show arp inspection vlan [<VLAN-ID>] [vsx-peer] command shows the current configuration of dynamic ARP inspection on a VLAN. When <VLAN-ID> is not specified, information is shown for all configured VLANs.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show arp state command do?

The show arp state command shows ARP (Address Resolution Protocol) cache entries that are in the specified state. Public show arp state 503 Shows the ARP cache entries for all VRF (Virtual Router Forwa rding) instances. Shows the ARP cache entries that are in failed state. The n eighbor might have been deleted. incomplete Shows the ARP cache entries that are in incomplete state. permanent reachable An incomplete state means that address resolution is in progres s and the link-layer address of the neighbor has not yet bee n determined. A solicitation request was sent, and the switch is waiting for a solicitation reply or a timeout. Shows the ARP cache entries that are in permanent state. ARP entries that are in a permanent state can be removed by a dministrative action only. Syntax: show arp state {all | failed | incomplete | permanent | reachable | stale}.

syntax: show arp state {all | failed | incomplete | permanent | reachable | stale}

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show arp state command?
The syntax of the show arp state command is: show arp state {all | failed | incomplete | permanent | reachable | stale}.

syntax: show arp state {all | failed | incomplete | permanent | reachable | stale}

The syntax of the show arp summary command is: show arp summary [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>] [vsx-peer]

The show spanning-tree inconsistent-ports command shows ports blocked by STP protection functions such as Root guard, Loop guard, BPDU guard, and RPVST guard in addition to MSTI information. Specifies the MSTP instance ID. Range: 1 to 4094. <INSTANCE-ID>. Syntax: show spanning-tree inconsistent-ports [instance <INSTANCE-ID>].

The show spanning-tree mst command shows MSTP configuration and status information for each instance. Shows the output from the VSX peer switch. If the switches do not have the VSX configuration or the ISL is down, the output f rom the VSX peer switch is not displayed. This parameter is avai lable on switches that support VSX. Showing MSTP configuration and status information: switch# show spanning-tree mst. Syntax: show spanning-tree mst [vsx-peer].
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show spanning-tree mst command?
The syntax of the show spanning-tree mst command is: show spanning-tree mst [vsx-peer]

The show spanning-tree mst <INSTANCE-ID> detail 233 command displays MSTP configurations for the given instance ID with corresponding port details. Specifies the MSTP instance number. Range: 0 to 4094. <INSTANCE-ID> vsx-peer Shows the output from the VSX peer switch. If the switches do not have the VSX configuration or the ISL is down, the output f rom the VSX peer switch is not displayed. This parameter is avai lable on switches that support VSX. Syntax: show spanning-tree mst <INSTANCE-ID> detail [vsx-peer].
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show spanning-tree mst <INSTANCE-ID> detail 233 command?
The syntax of the show spanning-tree mst <INSTANCE-ID> detail 233 command is: show spanning-tree mst <INSTANCE-ID> detail [vsx-peer]

The show spanning-tree mst interface command shows MSTP configurations for the given instance ID with corresponding port details. Specifies the MSTP instance number. Range: 0 to 4094. <INSTANCE-ID> <IFNAME> vsx-peer Specifies an interface. Shows the output from the VSX peer switch. If the switches do not have the VSX configuration or the ISL is down, the output f rom the VSX peer switch is not displayed. This parameter is avai lable on switches that support VSX. Syntax: show spanning-tree mst <INSTANCE-ID> interface <IFNAME>.

The syntax of the show spanning-tree mst interface command is: show spanning-tree mst <INSTANCE-ID> interface <IFNAME>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show spanning-tree summary port command do?
The show spanning-tree summary port command shows spanning tree port summary information. Syntax: show spanning-tree summary port.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show spanning-tree summary port command?
The syntax of the show spanning-tree summary port command is: show spanning-tree summary port.

The show spanning-tree summary root command shows spanning tree root summary information. Enables the spanning tree protocol on the switch. The no form of this command disables the spanning tree protocol on the switch. Public spanning-tree 239. Syntax: show spanning-tree summary root.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show spanning-tree summary root command?
The syntax of the show spanning-tree summary root command is: show spanning-tree summary root.

The show capacities rpvst command shows the capacities of RPVST VLANs configurable on a system and RPVST VPORTs supported in a system. Syntax: show capacities rpvst.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show capacities rpvst command?
The syntax of the show capacities rpvst command is: show capacities rpvst.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show capacities-status rpvst command do?
The show capacities-status rpvst command shows the number of RPVST VLANs and RPVST VPORTs currently configured. Syntax: show capacities-status rpvst.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show capacities-status rpvst command?
The syntax of the show capacities-status rpvst command is: show capacities-status rpvst.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show spanning-tree vlan command do?

The show spanning-tree vlan command displays the spanning tree mode and information on the RPVST instance of the specified VLAN. Specifies the number of a VLAN. <VLAN-ID> Public show spanning-tree vlan 317 Shows the output from the VSX peer switch. If the switches do not have the VSX configuration or the ISL is down, the output f rom the VSX peer switch is not displayed. This parameter is avai lable on switches that support VSX. Syntax: show spanning-tree vlan <VLAN-ID>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show spanning-tree vlan command?
The syntax of the show spanning-tree vlan command is: show spanning-tree vlan <VLAN-ID>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show spanning-tree vlan detail command do?

The show spanning-tree vlan detail command displays the spanning tree mode and information on the RPVST instance of the specified VLAN and optionally displays details on the RPVST instance for the VLAN. Specifies the number of a VLAN. <VLAN-ID> vsx-peer Shows the output from the VSX peer switch. If the switches do not have the VSX configuration or the ISL is down, the output f rom the VSX peer switch is not displayed. This parameter is avai lable on switches that support VSX. Public show spanning-tree vlan detail 319 Enables and configures the auto re-enable timeout in seconds for all interfaces with BPDU guard enabled. When an interface is disabled after receiving an unauthorized BPDU it will automatically be re-enabled after the timeout expires. The default is for the interface to stay disabled until manually re-enabled. Syntax: show spanning-tree vlan <VLAN-ID> detail [vsx-peer].

The syntax of the show spanning-tree vlan detail command is: show spanning-tree vlan <VLAN-ID> detail [vsx-peer]

The clear udld statistics command clears UDLD statistics for all interfaces or a specific interface. Syntax: clear udld statistics [interface <INTERFACE-NAME>].

The show capacities private-vlan command shows the maximum number of primary and secondary VLANs per domain and secondary ports per LC that can be configured. Syntax: show capacities private-vlan.

The syntax of the show capacities private-vlan command is: show capacities private-vlan.

The show capacities-status private-vlan command shows the number of primary VLANs currently configured and the maximum capacity of primary VLANs on the switch. Public show capacities-status private-vlan 381. Syntax: show capacities-status private-vlan.

For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show private-vlan association command do?

The show private-vlan association command shows primary and secondary VLAN associations for all private VLANs or a specified private VLAN. Specifies a list of VLANs. Range: 2-4094. Public show private-vlan association 384. Syntax: show private-vlan association <VLAN-ID>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show private-vlan association command?
The syntax of the show private-vlan association command is: show private-vlan association <VLAN-ID>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show private-vlan inconsistency command do?

The show private-vlan inconsistency command shows the list of interfaces and VLANs which are disabled or ignored by private VLAN due to private VLAN configuration and operational inconsistencies. Possible interface inconsistencies: • Hardware resource allocation failure • Interface is a member of multiple secondary VLANs in the same domain • Interface is a member of both primary and secondary VLAN • Interface of private vlan port-type promiscuous is not allowed to join secondary VLAN • Protocol VLANs and private VLANs are mutually exclusive features • Interface of private vlan port-type secondary is not allowed to join the primary VLAN • Interface has reached the private-vlan port limit of 24 ports • Interface is a member of a secondary VLAN which has an SVI configured on it • Interface trunk-allowed-all configuration is not allo. Syntax: show private-vlan inconsistency.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show private-vlan inconsistency command?
The syntax of the show private-vlan inconsistency command is: show private-vlan inconsistency.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the interface lag command do?

The interface lag command creates a Link Aggregation Group (LAG) interface represented by an ID. The no form of this command deletes a LAG interface represented by an ID. Specifies a LAG interface ID. <ID>. Syntax: interface lag <ID>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the interface lag command?
The syntax of the interface lag command is: interface lag <ID>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the interface persona lag command do?

The interface persona lag command creates a persona LAG, a template for LAG (Link Aggregation Group) interfaces. A persona LAG defines configuration options that can be applied to multiple LAGs for consistency and simplified management. Only LAG interfaces can reference persona LAG templates. The persona LAG name cannot use reserved interface names or formats, such as vlan, lag, or loopback. Configurations defined in a persona LAG are valid only for LAG interfaces. Public interface persona lag 52 The no form of this command deletes the persona LAG interface. Specifies the name of the persona LAG. <NAME>. Syntax: interface persona lag <NAME>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the interface persona lag command?
The syntax of the interface persona lag command is: interface persona lag <NAME>

The show lacp interfaces command displays an LACP configuration of the physical interfaces, including VSX s. If an interface name is passed as argument, it only displays an LACP configuration of a specified interface. Optional: Specifies an interface name. <IFNAME> vsx-peer Shows the output from the VSX peer switch. If the switches do not have the VSX configuration or the ISL is down, the output f rom the VSX peer switch is not displayed. This parameter is avai lable on switches that support VSX. Public show lacp interfaces 76. Syntax: show lacp interfaces [<IFNAME>] [vsx-peer].

The syntax of the show lacp interfaces command is: show lacp interfaces [<IFNAME>] [vsx-peer]

The vlan trunk native command assigns a native VLAN ID to a LAG interface. The no form of this command removes a native VLAN from a LAG interface and assigns VLAN ID 1 as its native VLAN. <VLAN-ID> Maximum number of VLANs supported: 512 (4100i) Specifies the number of the VLAN ID to assign. The VLAN ID m ust exist. Syntax: vlan trunk native <VLAN-ID>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the vlan trunk native command?
The syntax of the vlan trunk native command is: vlan trunk native <VLAN-ID>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the diag-dump smartlink basic command do?

The diag-dump smartlink basic command dumps the Smartlink configuration, state and statistics. Configures primary port for a Smartlink group. The no form of this command removes primary port from a Smartlink group. Specifies interface for primary port. <INTERFACE-NAME> Configures secondary port for a Smartlink group. The no form of this command removes secondary port from a Smartlink group. Specifies interface for secondary port. <INTERFACE-NAME> Configures control VLAN in a Smartlink group. The no form of this command removes control VLAN from a Smartlink group. Specifies VLAN ID for a Smartlink group. Public control-vlan 101 Specifies VLANs protected by a Smartlink group. The no form of this command removes VLANs protected by a Smartlink group. Specifies list of VLAN IDs. Range is 1 to 4094. Syntax: diag-dump smartlink basic.

The syntax of the diag-dump smartlink basic command is: diag-dump smartlink basic.

The show capacities smartlink command shows Smartlink capacities or Smartlink capacities and status. Syntax: show capacities smartlink | show capacities-status smartlink.

The syntax of the show-tech ufd command is: show-tech ufd.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what monitoring behavior is documented for egress Physical Interface?

egress Physical Interface: ethernet Type drop Reason4 (ID 1231, 14823 A ruba) drop Start Milliseconds4 (ID 1232, 14823 Aruba) drop End Milliseconds4 (ID 1233, 14823 Aruba) cng Queue Threshold (ID 490, 14 823 Aruba) cng Queue Observed Time Stamp (ID 491, 14823 Aruba) cng Global View Id (ID 495, 14823 Aruba) cng Global Buffer Usage (ID 496, 1 4823 Aruba) cng SourceIP (ID 497, 14823 Aru ba) cng DestinationIP (ID 498, 1482 3 Aruba) cng Queue Dropped Octet Count (I D 499, 14823 Aruba) cng Queue Rx Octet Count (ID 500, 14823 Aruba) cng Queue Buffer Usage (ID 501, 14823 Aruba) cng Queue Id (ID 502, 14823 Aru ba) cng Queue Dropped Packet Count (ID 503, 14823 Aruba) Public IP Flow Information Export 17 Private Information Elements cng Queue Rx Packet Count (ID 50 4, 14823 Aruba) NOTE Private information elements 495-504 are exclulsively reported by a flow congestion monitor. Data generated by a flow congestion monitor is exported simultaneously in two types of IPFIX data records. Each record type contains a subset of information elements, listed in the tables below.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what monitoring behavior is documented for Unknown VLAN?

Unknown VLAN: Martian Address MTU Failure Vlan Mbr Chk Fail L3 Header Error Unroutable Unicast Unicast TTL expired The packet has an unknown VLAN 5420, 6200, 6300, 6400, 8100, 8 325, 8325P, 8325H, 8360, 10000 Switch series Packet has invalid source or destin ation IP address, for example, 0.0. 0.0 or 127.0.0.0. 8325, 8325P, 8325H, 10000 switch series The packet size is larger than the IP Maximum Transmission Unit (M TU) configured in the interface. 5420, 6200, 6300, 6400, 8100, 8 325, 8325P, 8325H, 8360 Switch series.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, how do you configure IP Flow Information Export on 10000 Sw... 31?
To configure IP Flow Information Export on 10000 Sw... 31, follow the documented procedure: 1. Creating a flow record 2. Creating a flow monitor 3. Assigning the flow record to the flow monitor.
