messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the diag-dump ipfix basic command do?
The diag-dump ipfix basic command displays diagnostic information for IPFIX. Syntax: diag-dump ipfix basic.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the diag-dump ipfix basic command?
The syntax of the diag-dump ipfix basic command is: diag-dump ipfix basic.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the flow congestion-monitor <MONITOR-NAME> command do?

The flow congestion-monitor <MONITOR-NAME> command creates or modifies a flow congestion monitor. The no form of this command deletes a flow congestion monitor. Public flow congestion-monitor 44 Specifies the name of the flow congestion monitor. The name ca n be comprised of up to 64 alphanumeric, underscore, hyphen a nd period characters. NOTE A maximum of one flow congestion monitor can be created. Syntax: flow congestion-monitor <MONITOR-NAME>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the flow congestion-monitor <MONITOR-NAME> command?
The syntax of the flow congestion-monitor <MONITOR-NAME> command is: flow congestion-monitor <MONITOR-NAME>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the flow monitor <name> command do?

The flow monitor <name> command on HPE Aruba Networking 5420, 6200, 6300, 6400, 8325, 8325H, 8325P, 8100, 8360, 9300, 9300S, and 10040 Switch series, a flow monitor is the part of the IP Flow Information Export (IPFIX) feature that Public flow monitor 50 performs network monitoring for the selected interface. A flow monitor configuration consists of a flow record, a flow cache, and one or more associated flow exporters. A flow monitor compiles data from the network traffic on the interface and stores it in the flow cache in a format defined by the flow record. The flow exporters associated with the monitor then export data from the flow cache to the flow exporter destination. Syntax: flow monitor <name>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the flow monitor <name> command?
The syntax of the flow monitor <name> command is: flow monitor <name>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the flow record <record-name> command do?

The flow record <record-name> command creates or modifies a flow record and switches to the config-flow-record context for the flow record. Define data to be included in a flow record by configuring flow record match and collect fields. A flow record defines match (key) fields and collection (non-key) fields. Customers configure flow records with match (key) fields and collect (non-key) fields. Match fields are the set of fields that define a flow, such as IP address or UDP port. Collect fields are the set of fields that identify information to collect for a flow, such as packet and byte counters. Traffic with matching attributes (for example, traffic coming from the same interface, sent to the same destination with the same protocol) are classified as a single flow. Syntax: flow record <record-name>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the flow record <record-name> command?
The syntax of the flow record <record-name> command is: flow record <record-name>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ingress interface <IFRANGE> command do?

The ingress interface <IFRANGE> command adds or removes monitored ingress interfaces when in the config-flow-congestion-monitor context. The no form of this command stops monitoring one or more ingress interfaces. One or more comma - or hyphen-separated interfaces. A flow congestion monitor only exportsFlows destined for monitored queues received on a monitored ingress (source) interface. If a flow does not use an ingress interface specified in the congestion monitor, or if the flow egresses on a queue that is not monitored, then the flow will not be exported. NOTE A maximum of 52 ingress interfaces may be added to a flow congestion monitor. Public ingress-interface 59. Syntax: ingress interface <IFRANGE>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the ingress interface <IFRANGE> command?
The syntax of the ingress interface <IFRANGE> command is: ingress interface <IFRANGE>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ip-all flow congestion-monitor command do?

The ip-all flow congestion-monitor command enablesFlow monitoring on an interface when in the config-if orconfig-lag-if contexts. Flow congestion monitors are uniquely applied in the "out" direction to monitor outbound (egress) traffic on queues on an interface. A congestion monitor may be applied to one or more interface queues. The no form disables the flow monitoring on an interface. NOTE Only physical interfaces and LAG interfaces can be monitored. A flow monitor cannot be applied to an interface that is part of a LAG. The monitor must be applied on the LAG itself. If an interface is part of a LAG while attempting to apply a flow monitor, an error message will be displayed. The name of the flow monitor. out queue < QUEUES >. Syntax: ip-all flow congestion-monitor <MONITOR-NAME> out queue <QUEUES>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the ip-all flow congestion-monitor command?
The syntax of the ip-all flow congestion-monitor command is: ip-all flow congestion-monitor <MONITOR-NAME> out queue <QUEUES>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ip-all flow monitor command do?

The ip-all flow monitor command enablesFlow monitoring for all flows passing through Distributed Services Modules (DSMs) in the DSM configuration context. The flow reports arriving from DSM on the monitor's associated collector will be processed by the monitor and the appended fields specified in the monitor's collector will be added. Finally, it will be exported by the monitor's exporter. The [no] form of command disables the flow monitoring. Public ip-all flow monitor 62. Syntax: [no] ip-all flow monitor <name>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the ip-all flow monitor command?
The syntax of the ip-all flow monitor command is: [no] ip-all flow monitor <name>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ip IPv6 flow monitor (interface) command do?

The ip IPv6 flow monitor (interface) command enable flow monitoring on inbound and outbound interfaces by assigning a flow monitor to that interface. Only physical interfaces and LAG interfaces can be monitored. A flow monitor cannot be applied to an interface that is part of a LAG. If an unsupported application is attempted, an error message will be displayed. If the flow monitor is associated with a flow record that contains application fields as collect fields, then Application Recognition should be enabled on the same interface. The [no] form of command disables the flow monitoring. NOTE An IPv6 flow monitor is not supported on 8325, 8325H, 8325P, and 9300S Switch Series. Syntax: [no] ip|IPv6 flow monitor (interface).
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the ip IPv6 flow monitor (interface) command?
The syntax of the ip IPv6 flow monitor (interface) command is: [no] ip|IPv6 flow monitor (interface)
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show flow collector command do?

The show flow collector command displaysFlow collector configuration and status. If no collector name is specified, the output of this command displays information for all flow collectors. The output of this command can indicate the following status types: • Accepted • Rejected (Internal error: failed to process collector) • Rejected (The configured listen address is missing or invalid) • Rejected (The configured listen address not associated with a local interface) Name of the flow collector. The following example displays the configuration of a flow collector named collector-1. Syntax: show flow collector <name>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show flow collector command?
The syntax of the show flow collector command is: show flow collector <name>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show flow congestion-monitor command do?

The show flow congestion-monitor command displaysFlow congestion monitor configuration and status. When no congestion monitor name is provided, all flow congestion monitors are displayed. Public show flow congestion-monitor 66 <MONITOR-NAME> vsx-peer Identifies the name of a flow congestion monitor. If present, the command will display the flow collector configura tion for the VSX. Monitors tx on some interfaces Status: Accepted Flow Exporter: exporter-1 Monitored Ingress Interfaces: 1/1/5, 1/1/8-1/1/10 Monitored Queue Count: 4 (limit 160) Monitored Egress Interfaces and Queues: 1/1/4 queue 2, 4, 5 1/1/7 queue 7 Displaying information with no flow congestion monitors configured: switch# show flow congestion-monitor No flow congestion monitors configured. Syntax: show flow congestion-monitor [<MONITOR-NAME>] [vsx-peer].
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show flow congestion-monitor command?
The syntax of the show flow congestion-monitor command is: show flow congestion-monitor [<MONITOR-NAME>] [vsx-peer]
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show flow exporter command do?

The show flow exporter command displaysFlow exporter statistics, configuration and status. When no exporter name is specified, the output of this command displays information for all flow exporters. DisplaysFlow exporter configuration and status. The output of this command can indicate the following status types: • Accepted • Rejected (Internal error: exporter does not exist) • Rejected (Internal error: destination type does not exist) • Rejected (Destination type is hostname or IP address, but no destination is specified) • Rejected (Destination type is hostname or IP address, but the specified hostname or IP address is invalid) • Rejected (Destination type is Traffic Insight, but no destination is specified) • Rejected (Destination type is Traffic Insight, but the specified Traffic Insight instance does not exist) •. Syntax: show flow exporter [<name>].
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show flow exporter command?
The syntax of the show flow exporter command is: show flow exporter [<name>]
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show flow monitor command do?

The show flow monitor command displaysFlow monitor configuration and status. When no monitor name is specified, the output of this command displays information for all flow monitors. Name of the flow monitor. Includes the statistics parameter to display additional flow and cache statistics. information-elements Displays the configured information elements for the flow moni tor. Used for IPv4 traffic analysis Status: Accepted Flow Record: record-1 Public show flow monitor 75 Flow Exporter(s): exporter-1, exporter-2. Syntax: show flow monitor.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show flow monitor command?
The syntax of the show flow monitor command is: show flow monitor.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the congestion-event profile <NAME> command do?

The congestion-event profile <NAME> command creates a new congestion event profile and switches to the config-cng-prof context for the profile. The no form deletes the profile and removes it from all applied interfaces. NOTE If the specified profile already exists, this command switches to the config-cng- prof context for the named profile. Name of the congestion event profile, up to 64 characters. Public congestion-event profile 91. Syntax: congestion-event profile <NAME>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the congestion-event profile <NAME> command?
The syntax of the congestion-event profile <NAME> command is: congestion-event profile <NAME>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the crypto pki application gnmi certificate command do?

The crypto pki application gnmi certificate command configures a certificate for the gNMI server. local-cert is used by default. For more details, refer to the [Public Key Infrastructure guide] (./Functionality_Guide_PKI.md) Specifies the certificate name. <CERT-NAME>. Syntax: crypto pki application gnmi certificate <CERT-NAME>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the crypto pki application gnmi certificate command?
The syntax of the crypto pki application gnmi certificate command is: crypto pki application gnmi certificate <CERT-NAME>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the gnmi vrf <VRF NAME> command do?

The gnmi vrf <VRF NAME> command enables the gNMI server on a given VRF. gNMI can be enabled on multiple VRFs simultaneously. NOTE There is a maximum of 30 gNMI subscriptions per request and a maximum of 9 concurrent streams per switch. These are system-wide limits shared among all authenticated users regardless of connection method (local or remote). The no form of this command removes the configuration. Disabling the gNMI server on a VRF immediately closes any active streams. Specifies the VRF name. <VRF NAME>. Syntax: gnmi vrf <VRF NAME>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the gnmi vrf <VRF NAME> command?
The syntax of the gnmi vrf <VRF NAME> command is: gnmi vrf <VRF NAME>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show gnmi command do?
The show gnmi command displays the current gNMI configuration. Syntax: show gnmi.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show gnmi command?
The syntax of the show gnmi command is: show gnmi.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the diag-dump command do?
The diag-dump command provides detailed service status information for gNMI and nginx-configurator daemon. Syntax: diag-dump gnmi basic.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the diag-dump command?
The syntax of the diag-dump command is: diag-dump gnmi basic.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the debug nginxconfigurator <all|gnmi> all severity <level> command do?
The debug nginxconfigurator <all|gnmi> all severity <level> command enables nginx configurator and YANG resolver debug logs. Syntax: debug nginxconfigurator <all|gnmi> all severity <level>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the debug nginxconfigurator <all|gnmi> all severity <level> command?
The syntax of the debug nginxconfigurator <all|gnmi> all severity <level> command is: debug nginxconfigurator <all|gnmi> all severity <level>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show tech command do?
The show tech command provides configuration, logs, and status information relevant to the gNMI HTTP/2 server. Syntax: show tech gnmi.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show tech command?
The syntax of the show tech command is: show tech gnmi.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what monitoring behavior is documented for IFA support on VSX?

IFA support on VSX: • All VSX modes are supported: MCLAG, Active Gateway, and Active Forwarding. • VSX Sync configuration is supported. • All IFA roles are supported: Initiator, Transit, and Terminator. • If both VSX members are configured as terminator: ◦ ◦ IFA traffic is terminated on the first VSX member where it is received. It is recommended to review the metrics on both VSX members. • If an IFA flow needs to be initiated on the VSX, both VSX members must be configured as Initiator. If traffic goes through the ISL, the node must also be configured as Transit to capture its metrics. Otherwise, some packages will not be captured and flagged as IFA. • Since each VSX member samplesFlow packets independently. ◦ Initialization of IFA Flows depends on which VSX member receives the flow. ◦ Sampling counts will start based on local IFA class entries settings, and the actual traffic traversing the VSX member. • Both VSX members should be configured as Transit in order to include the device IFA metrics. This is required for traffic that could pass through the ISL.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ifa-sampling-rate <VALUE> command do?

The ifa-sampling-rate <VALUE> command configures the IFA sampling rate in multiples of a thousand packets to initiate cloned packets on filtered flows that match any configured initiator class, among all active IFA initiator monitors. For example, if the rate is set to 4, 1 out of every 4000 packets will be sampled. The no form of this command resets the sampling rate to its default value. Specifies the IFA sampling rate. The default value is 4. Range: 4 to 4200000. <VALUE>. Syntax: ifa-sampling-rate <VALUE>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the ifa-sampling-rate <VALUE> command?
The syntax of the ifa-sampling-rate <VALUE> command is: ifa-sampling-rate <VALUE>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what monitoring behavior is documented for Configuration version migration?

Configuration version migration: A configuration version migration occurred which re quired a reboot. Console error Fabric fault The console failed to start. A fabric fault occurred. All line modules faulted A zero line card condition occurred. Redundancy switchover requested A user requested a redundancy switchover.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what monitoring behavior is documented for Redundant Management election timeout?

Redundant Management election timeout: Critical service fault (error) A failure to elect a standby management module in t he allotted time. A daemon critical to switch operation has stopped fu nctioning. An extra error string may be present to d escribe the error in detail. VSF autojoin renumber Reset triggered by VSF autojoin. VSF member renumbered A user requested a renumber of a VSF member. VSF switchover requested A user requested a VSF switchover.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what monitoring behavior is documented for VSX software update?

VSX software update: Reset triggered by a VSX software update. Chassis critical temperature Chassis operating temperature exceeded. Chassis low critical temperature Chassis temperature below the minimum operating threshold. Chassis insufficient fans Insufficient fans to cool the chassis. Chassis unsupported PSUs/fans Unsupported or misconfigured PSUs or system fans.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the directory <DIRECTORY-NAME> command do?

The directory <DIRECTORY-NAME> command selects an existing directory on the external storage volume. The no form of this command clears a directory of an external storage volume. Specifies the external storage directory for mapping the volume. <DIRECTORY-NAME>. Syntax: directory <DIRECTORY-NAME>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the directory <DIRECTORY-NAME> command?
The syntax of the directory <DIRECTORY-NAME> command is: directory <DIRECTORY-NAME>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the external-storage <VOLUME-NAME> command do?

The external-storage <VOLUME-NAME> command creates or updates an external storage volume. The no form of this command deletes an external storage volume. Public external-storage 207. Syntax: external-storage <VOLUME-NAME>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the external-storage <VOLUME-NAME> command?
The syntax of the external-storage <VOLUME-NAME> command is: external-storage <VOLUME-NAME>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the password [{plaintext | ciphertext} <PASSWORD>] command do?

The password [{plaintext | ciphertext} <PASSWORD>] command sets the password for network attached storage server login. The no form of this command clears the password for network attached storage server login. {ciphertext | plaintext} Selects the password format. Specifies the password. <PASSWORD> NOTE When the password is not provided on the com mand line, plaintext password prompting occurs upon pressing Enter. The entered password cha racters are masked with asterisks. Syntax: password [{plaintext | ciphertext} <PASSWORD>].
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the password [{plaintext | ciphertext} <PASSWORD>] command?
The syntax of the password [{plaintext | ciphertext} <PASSWORD>] command is: password [{plaintext | ciphertext} <PASSWORD>]
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the username <USER-NAME> command do?

The username <USER-NAME> command sets the username for logging in to a network attached storage server. The no form of this command clears a username. Specifies the username. <USER-NAME>. Syntax: username <USER-NAME>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the username <USER-NAME> command?
The syntax of the username <USER-NAME> command is: username <USER-NAME>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ip-sla command do?

The ip-sla command creates an IP Service Level Agreement (SLA) profile and switches to the config-ip-sla context. The no form of this command deletes an IP-SLA profile. By default, all profile use the default VRF (default). Public ip-sla 226 Specifies an IP-SLA profile name. Length: 1 to 64 characters. <IP-SLA-NAME>. Syntax: ip-sla <IP-SLA-NAME>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the ip-sla command?
The syntax of the ip-sla command is: ip-sla <IP-SLA-NAME>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the ip-sla responder command do?

The ip-sla responder command selects the IP-SLA responder. The responder can be configured for udp-echo, tcp-connect, udp-jitter-voip type. It requires the SLA name, SLA type, and port number as arguments. The no form of this command removes the IP-SLA responder. Specifies the IP-SLA responder name. Length: 1 to 64 chara cters. <SLA-NAME> udp-echo tcp-connect Enables responder for udp-echo probes. Selects TCP connect as the IP-SLA test mechanism. udp-jitter-voip Selects VOIP jitter as the IP-SLA test mechanism. Specifies the port number to listen for IP-SLA probes. Range: 1 to 65535. <PORT-NUM> source {<SOURCE-IPv4- ADDR>|<SOURCE-IPv6-ADDR>| <IFNAME>} Selects the source IPv4 or IPv6 address for SLA probes or the s ource interface to use for sending IP-SLA probes. vrf <VRF-NAME> Specifies the name of the VRF to use. Syntax: ip-sla responder <SLA-NAME> (udp-echo | tcp-connect | udp-jitter-voip).
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the ip-sla responder command?
The syntax of the ip-sla responder command is: ip-sla responder <SLA-NAME> (udp-echo | tcp-connect | udp-jitter-voip)
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show ip-sla all command do?
The show ip-sla all command shows all ip-sla source configuration and status. Syntax: show ip-sla all.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show ip-sla all command?
The syntax of the show ip-sla all command is: show ip-sla all.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the start-test command do?
The start-test command starts the IP-SLA probes. Syntax: start-test.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the start-test command?
The syntax of the start-test command is: start-test.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the stop-test command do?
The stop-test command stops the IP-SLA probes. Public stop-test 237. Syntax: stop-test.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the stop-test command?
The syntax of the stop-test command is: stop-test.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the tcp-connect {<DEST-IPv4-ADDR>|<DEST-IPv6-ADDR>|<HOSTNAME>} <DEST-PORT-NUM> command do?

The tcp-connect {<DEST-IPv4-ADDR>|<DEST-IPv6-ADDR>|<HOSTNAME>} <DEST-PORT-NUM> command configures TCP connect as the IP-SLA test mechanism. Requires destination address/hostname and destination port for the IP-SLA of tcp-connect IP-SLA type. The no form of this command removes the the TCP connection. {<DEST-IPv4-ADDR>|<DEST- IPv6-ADDR>|<HOSTNAME>} Selects the destination, either an IPv4 address, an IPv6 address, or hostname, for the IP-SLA. Destination port for the IP-SLA. Range: 1 to 65535. <DEST-PORT-NUM> history-interval <HISTORY- INTERVAL> Configures the history interval for the IP-SLA. Set the history i nterval to minimum of two times the probe-interval for the SL A. Range: 60 to 7200. name-server {<IPv4-ADDR- DNS-SERVER>|<IPv6-ADDR-DNS- SERVER>} probe-interval <PROBE- INTERVAL> Specifies the DNS server for destination hostname resolution. Probe interval in seconds. Syntax: tcp-connect {<DEST-IPv4-ADDR>|<DEST-IPv6-ADDR>|<HOSTNAME>} <DEST-PORT-NUM>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the tcp-connect {<DEST-IPv4-ADDR>|<DEST-IPv6-ADDR>|<HOSTNAME>} <DEST-PORT-NUM> command?
The syntax of the tcp-connect {<DEST-IPv4-ADDR>|<DEST-IPv6-ADDR>|<HOSTNAME>} <DEST-PORT-NUM> command is: tcp-connect {<DEST-IPv4-ADDR>|<DEST-IPv6-ADDR>|<HOSTNAME>} <DEST-PORT-NUM>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the udp-echo {<DEST-IPv4-ADDR>|<DEST-IPv6-ADDR>|<HOSTNAME>} <DEST-PORT-NUM> command?
The syntax of the udp-echo {<DEST-IPv4-ADDR>|<DEST-IPv6-ADDR>|<HOSTNAME>} <DEST-PORT-NUM> command is: udp-echo {<DEST-IPv4-ADDR>|<DEST-IPv6-ADDR>|<HOSTNAME>} <DEST-PORT-NUM>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the clear mirror command do?

The clear mirror command clears the mirror statistics for all configured mirror sessions or a specified session Specifies all configured sessions. Specifies a numeric identifier for the session. Range: 1 to 4. Syntax: clear mirror [all | <SESSION-ID>].
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the clear mirror command?
The syntax of the clear mirror command is: clear mirror [all | <SESSION-ID>]
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the copy tcpdump-pcap command do?

The copy tcpdump-pcap command saves packet capture files to external storage. Specifies the packet capture file to save. <FILE-NAME> <REMOTE-URL> Specifies the external storage to which the packet capture file w ill be saved. Public copy tcpdump-pcap 253. Syntax: copy tcpdump-pcap <FILE-NAME> <REMOTE-URL>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the copy tcpdump-pcap command?
The syntax of the copy tcpdump-pcap command is: copy tcpdump-pcap <FILE-NAME> <REMOTE-URL>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the copy tshark-pcap command do?

The copy tshark-pcap command copies the tshark capture data to a file on a TFTP or SFTP server. <REMOTE-URL> Specifies the capture file on a remote TFTP or SFTP server. The URL format is: {tftp:// | sftp://<USER>@} {<IP>|<HOST>} [: <PORT>] [; blocksi ze=<SIZE>]/<FILE> vrf <VRF-NAME> Specifies the name of a VRF. Default: default. Syntax: copy tshark-pcap <REMOTE-URL> [vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the copy tshark-pcap command?
The syntax of the copy tshark-pcap command is: copy tshark-pcap <REMOTE-URL> [vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the destination cpu command do?

The destination cpu command the command causes the mirror session to transmit mirrored packets to the switch CPU. This destination may be configured for multiple sessions, however only one such configured session may be active at a given time. The diagnostic utility Tshark may be used to view and capture packets transmitted to the CPU through this route. Ctrl+C must be entered to terminate a Tshark capture session. More details can be found in the Supportability Guide. The no form of this command will immediately stops mirroring traffic to the CPU, but will not remove any sources from the mirror configuration. Syntax: destination cpu.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the destination cpu command?
The syntax of the destination cpu command is: destination cpu.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the destination interface {<INTERFACE-ID>|<LAG-NAME>} command?
The syntax of the destination interface {<INTERFACE-ID>|<LAG-NAME>} command is: destination interface {<INTERFACE-ID>|<LAG-NAME>}.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the diagnostic command do?

The diagnostic command captures packets from a mirror-to-cpu session, and save the most recent 32MB to pcap file which can then be copied and analyzed. When capturing a mirror-to-cpu session to a file, packets will not be dumped to the console. NOTE The diagnostic command must be entered prior to the diag utiliti es tshark command. Use the delete-file form of this command to delete the most recent capture file. Since file and delete-file are optional, the behavior of the base command diag utilities tshark does not save anything to a file, and instead dumps the tshark session to the console until CTRL + c is entered. Saves captured packets to a temporary file. delete-file Deletes the most recent captured file. Syntax: diagnostic.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the diagnostic command?
The syntax of the diagnostic command is: diagnostic.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the NOTE command do?

The NOTE command enables mirror endpoint from its default disabled state. To verify the mirror endpoint was successfully activated, run the show mirror endpoint NAME command and verify that the Admin Status and Operational Status has changed from disabled to enabled. If the status value remains disabled, consult the system logs to determine the reason for activation failure. To disable the mirror endpoint, first disable the remote mirror session on the switch that's originating the data. Next, use the shutdown command to disable the mirror endpoint. Syntax: NOTE.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the NOTE command?
The syntax of the NOTE command is: NOTE.

Public packet-capture enable 287 Enables packet capture for the specified session. A session name must be specified first before issuing the enable command or its no form. If the session hasn't been configured as a packet-capture session prior to running these commands, the error message Packet Capture session does not exist is displayed. Syntax: packet-capture <SESSION_NAME> [enable].

The lldp trap enable command enables sending SNMP traps for LLDP related events from a particular interface. The no form of this command disables the LLDP trap generation. NOTE LLDP trap generation is disabled by default at the global level and must be enabled before any LLDP traps are sent. The snmpv3 context <NAME> vrf <VRF-NAME> [community <STRING>] command creates an SNMP v3 context on the specified VRF. The no form of this command removes the specified SNMP context. Specifies the name of the context. Range: 1 to 32 printable ASC II characters, excluding space and question mark (?). Public snmpv3 context 345 vrf <VRF-NAME> Specifies the VRF associated with the context. Default: default. community <STRING> Specifies the SNMP community string associated with the con text. The snmpv3 engine-id <ENGINE-ID> command configures the SNMP v3 SNMP engine-id allowing an administrator to configure a unique SNMP engine-id for the switch. This engine-id is used by the NMS management tool to identify and distinguish multiple switches on the same network. The no form of this command restores the default engine-id, created by the switch using a combination of the enterprise OID value and the switch's MAC address. SNMP v3 SNMP engine-id in colon separated hexadecimal n otation. <ENGINE-ID>. Syntax: snmpv3 engine-id <ENGINE-ID>.

The syntax of the snmpv3 engine-id <ENGINE-ID> command is: snmpv3 engine-id <ENGINE-ID>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the snmpv3 security-level {auth | auth-privacy} command do?

The snmpv3 security-level {auth | auth-privacy} command configures the SNMP v3 security level. The security level determines which SMNPv3 users defined by the command snmpv3 user are able to connect. The no form of this command changes the security level as follows: • no snmpv3 security-level auth: Sets the security level to auth-privacy. • no snmpv3 security-level auth-privacy: Sets the security level to no authentication or privacy, allowing any SNMP user to connect. SNMP v3 users that support authentication, or authentication a nd privacy are allowed. Only SNMP v3 users with both authentication and privacy are al lowed. This is the highest level of SNMP v3 security. Default. Syntax: snmpv3 security-level {auth | auth-privacy}.

The snmp-server agent-port command sets the UDP port number that the SNMP master agent uses to communicate. UDP port 161 is the default port. The no form of this command sets the SNMP master agent port to the default value. Specifies the UDP port number that the SNMP master agent wil l use. Range: 1 to 65535. Default: 161. <PORT>. Syntax: snmp-server agent-port <PORT>.

The syntax of the snmp-server agent-port command is: snmp-server agent-port <PORT>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the snmp-server community command do?

The snmp-server community command adds an SNMP v1/SNMP v2c community string. A community string is like a password that controls read/ write access to the SNMP agent. A network management program must supply this name when attempting Public snmp-server community 351 to get SNMP information from the switch. A maximum of 10 community strings are supported. Once you create your own community string, the default community string (public) is deleted. The no form of this command removes the specified SNMP v1/SNMP v2c community string. When no community string exists, a default community string with the value public is automatically defined. Specifies the SNMP v1/SNMP v2c community string. Range: 1 to 32 printable ASCII characters, excluding space and question ma rk. Syntax: snmp-server community <STRING>.

The syntax of the snmp-server community command is: snmp-server community <STRING>

The queue-monitor command this command enables the queue monitoring feature, allowing the switch to collect queue statistics. then display this information as a queue statistics history. These aggregate statistics can be used to monitor network health, troubleshoot network issues, and identify normal network behavior patterns and Public Regulatory Information 388 performance anomalies. This feature is disabled by default, but can be enabled on a maximum of 52 interfaces per switch. Syntax: queue-monitor.

The clear ip mroute command clears the multicast routing information for a specified VRF or all VRFs. If you do not include VRF information in these commands, the clear ip mroute commands clear the default VRF. Clears multicast routing for all VRFs. Clears the multicast routing information for the group address i n the specified VRF or all VRFs. If the command does not include VRF information, it clears routi ng information for the group address in the default VRF. Clears multicast routing information for the group and source a ddresses in the specified VRF or all VRFs. If the command does not include VRF information, it clears routi ng information for the group and source addresses in the defau lt VRF. Clears the multicast routing information for the group prefix in t he specified VRF or all VRFs. Syntax: clear ip mroute.

The version must be a text string that starts with a letter or number and can contain alphanumeric characters and the special characters. (dot), - (hyphen), and _ (underscore). Version must not be empty. Author Specifies the author of the script. Must be a text string. Optional items AOSCXPlatform List Specifies the exact list of AOS-CX platforms (model numbers) on which the NAE Script is permitted to run. The platform list is enclosed in square brackets with each platform enclosed in single or double quotes and separated from other platforms with a comma. messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the Max Algorithm(continuous_learning_window="<duration>") command do?

The Max Algorithm(continuous_learning_window="<duration>") command the Max Algorithm function smooths raw data within the initial learning time or the continuous learning window by calculating an average over time to find the greatest normal data during the past learning window. Syntax: Max Algorithm(continuous_learning_window="<duration>").
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the Max Algorithm(continuous_learning_window="<duration>") command?
The syntax of the Max Algorithm(continuous_learning_window="<duration>") command is: Max Algorithm(continuous_learning_window="<duration>")
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the guide say about algorithm for dynamic Threshold calculation?

algorithm for dynamic Threshold calculation: self.alg = Max Algorithm(continuous_learning_window="2h") How the Max Algorithm function works The Max Algorithm function uses smoothed data over time to find the maximum value and calculate a baseline of "typical" data. By setting a high threshold based on this calculation, the number of false positives can be reduced. The agent generates alerts for extreme anomalies only. The Max Algorithm function is best for data that has no limit on its maximum value, such as incoming network traffic. Smoothing data The Max Algorithm function uses the "simple moving average" algorithm to smooth data. In general, data smoothing is the process of detecting and removing "noise" data, allowing important patterns to remain. In its formula, the simple moving average algorithm reduces the influence of datapoints that are not similar to the others because it includes multiple datapoints in the calculation. In addition, at each subsequent measurement point, the new data point is added and the oldest data point is removed, so short-term anomalies are eventually removed from the calculation.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the Inside a callback action: command do?

The Inside a callback action: command the CLI action executes the specified command from the switch CLI. Generates a multiple line report with customized content. The report content can be viewed in the Web UI from the alert details for the agent. The SHELL action executes the specified command in the Bash shell of the underlying Linux operating system of the switch. The Action Syslog action enters the specified message in the event log. Users can access event log messages from the CLI, REST API, or Web UI of the switch. Syntax: Inside a callback action: .

The syntax of the Inside a callback action: command is: Inside a callback action:

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the guide say about CPU value when the Condition met?

CPU value when the Condition met: cpu = event["/v1/system/subsystems/system/base? attributes=resource_utilization.cpu"] Action Syslog("CPU Utilization at %d" % cpu) ActionCLI("top cpu") def set_normal(self, event): self.remove_alert_level() Alert level functions The Aruba Network Analytics Engine provides several predefined functions related to alerts. The alert level functions get, set, or remove the alert level of the agent: get_alert_level() Gets the current alert level of the agent. Example: status = self.get_alert_level() set_alert_level(<Alert_Level>) Sets the alert level of the agent. Example: self.set_alert_level(Alert Level. CRITICAL) The <Alert_Level> must be one of the following values: Alert Level. CRITICAL The agent has detected a critical issue. Alert Level. MAJOR The agent has detected a major issue. Alert Level. MINOR The agent has detected a minor issue. Public Alert level functions 167 remove_alert_level() Removes the alert level of the agent.

The https-server max-user-sessions command sets the maximum amount of concurrent open sessions for any given user through the HTTPS server. The amount of concurrent open sessions may have an impact on system performance, so it is recommended to set this value to the minimum necessary. Specifies the maximum number of user sessions allowed. Defaul t: 6. Maximum value: 8. <SESSION-AMT>. Syntax: https-server max-user-sessions <SESSION-AMT>.

For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the https-server rest access-mode command do?

The https-server rest access-mode command changes the REST API access mode. The default mode is read-write. This command does not affect Central connections, which have permission to alter configurations regardless of the access mode set on the switch. Selects the read/write mode. Allows POST, PUT, PATCH, and DE LETE methods to be called on all configurable elements in the s witch database. Selects the read-only mode. Write access to most switch resou rces through the REST API is disabled. Setting the mode to read-write on the REST API allows POST, PUT, PATCH, and DELETE methods to be called on all configurable elements in the switch database. By default, REST APIs in the device are in the read-write mode. Some switch resources allow POST, PUT, PATCH, and DELETE regardless of REST API mode. Syntax: https-server rest access-mode {read-only | read-write}.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the https-server rest access-mode command?
The syntax of the https-server rest access-mode command is: https-server rest access-mode {read-only | read-write}.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the https-server rest firmware-site-distribution command do?

The https-server rest firmware-site-distribution command this command enables or disables the Firmware Site Distribution server listening on port 9443. Public https-server rest firmware-site-distribution 228 The firmware site distribution allows you to use a switch to distribute a firmware image file to other switches in the same network. This prevents the switches from connecting to the cloud or an external network to download a firmware image file. On enabling the firmware site distribution, it exposes a REST endpoint that allows the switches to download a switch primary or secondary firmware image. 1 NOTE As per the limitation, up to two switches can download the firmware image simultaneously. This endpoint is to be used along with REST /firmware endpoint to handle the firmware download and installation process. Syntax: https-server rest firmware-site-distribution.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the https-server rest firmware-site-distribution command?
The syntax of the https-server rest firmware-site-distribution command is: https-server rest firmware-site-distribution.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the https-server session close all command do?

The https-server session close all command invalidates and closes all HTTPS sessions. All existing WebUI sessions (including sessions used for Central connections) will be logged out. REST and WebUI users will have to reauthenticate. and all real-time notification feature Web Socket connections are closed and must be resubscribed. Syntax: https-server session close all.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the https-server session close all command?
The syntax of the https-server session close all command is: https-server session close all.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the https-server session-timeout command do?

The https-server session-timeout command configures the timeout, in minutes, for any given HTTPS server session. A value of 0 disables the timeout. This command does not affect sessions used for Central connections. Specifies the maximum idle time, in minutes for an HTTPS sess ion. Default: 20. Maximum: 480 (8 hours). 0 disables the timeou t, but the maxium is still enforced. <MINUTES>. Syntax: https-server session-timeout <MINUTES>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the https-server session-timeout command?
The syntax of the https-server session-timeout command is: https-server session-timeout <MINUTES>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the https-server vrf command do?

The https-server vrf command configures and starts the HTTPS server on the specified VRF, allowing access to REST and the WebUI from ports assigned to that VRF. This command does not affect access to HPE Aruba Networking Central instances, as this feature has its own dedicated connection channel. The no form of the command stops any HTTPS servers running on the specified VRF and removes the HTTPS server configuration. Specifies the VRF name. Required. Length: Up to 32 alpha num eric characters. By using this command, you enable access to both the Web UI and to the REST API on the specified VRF. You can enable access on multiple VRFs. By default, HPE Aruba Networking 8100, 8320, 8325, 8360, 8400, 9300/9300S, 10000, 10040 Switch Series have an HTTPS server enabled on the mgmt VRF. Syntax: https-server vrf <VRF-NAME>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the https-server vrf command?
The syntax of the https-server vrf command is: https-server vrf <VRF-NAME>

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the clear queue-monitor interface command do?

The clear queue-monitor interface command clear the data collected on an interface since enabling queue monitoring. If no interfaces are specified, perform a clear of data collected on all interfaces with queue monitoring enabled. | Parameter | | Description | | | | <IFNAME> | | Name of the interface. | | | Format: <MEMBER>/<SLOT>/<PORT> | <IFRANGE> | | Range of interfaces. | | | Format: <MEMBER>/<SLOT>/<PORT>-<MEMBER>/<SLOT> /<PORT> queue-monitor This command enables the queue monitoring feature, allowing the switch to coll ect queue statistics at 10-second time intervals. These aggregate statistics ca n be used to monitor network health, troubleshoot network issues, and identify normal network behavior patterns and performance anomalies. Syntax: clear queue-monitor interface [<IFNAME>|<IFRANGE>].

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the qos dscp command do?

The qos dscp command configures a differentiated services code point (DSCP) remark for an Ethernet or LAG interface. IPv4 and IPv6 packets that ingress on the interface are remarked at egress using the configured DSCP value. The remark only occurs when QoS trust mode on the interface is set to none. If a DSCP remark is configured and then trust mode is subsequently set to cos or dscp, then the DSCP remark is ignored. The following commands will show the remark status as not applied (incompatible QoS global or port trust configuration): • show running-configuration • show interface <INTERFACE-NAME> Public qos dscp 103 • show interface <INTERFACE-NAME> qos The no form of this command removes a CoS remark on an interface. Specifies an IP differentiated services code point value. Range: 0 to 63. <CODE-POINT>. Syntax: qos dscp <CODE-POINT>.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the qos pool command do?

The qos pool command creates or modifies a packet buffer pool for lossless traffic by configuring lossless pool size, headroom buffer associated with the pool, and the priorities that are mapped to the pool. The lossless pool size is a percentage of the total available buffer memory on the device. The headroom pool memory is allocated from the lossless pool and is used for storing packets that arrive on a port after pause has been asserted. The no form of this command removes the QoS pool and returns the memory back to the default lossy pool. Public qos pool 105 Specifies the index of the lossless pool. factory-default Specifies the default lossless pool or headroom buffer sizes. Specifies the lossless pool size in percent. Default: 40.6. Range: 10 to 90. Specifies the headroom buffer size in kilobytes. Syntax: qos pool <INDEX> lossless size {factory-default|<PERCENT>} percent headroom.

syntax: qos pool <INDEX> lossless size {factory-default|<PERCENT>} percent headroom

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the qos schedule-profile command do?

The qos schedule-profile command creates a QoS schedule profile and switches to the config-schedule context for the profile. If the specified schedule profile exists, this command switches to the config-schedule context for the profile. The schedule profile determines the order in which queues are selected to transmit a packet, and the amount of service defined for each queue. Specifies the name of the QoS queue profile to create or config ure. Range: 1 to 64 alphanumeric characters, including period (.), underscore (_), and hyphen (-). <NAME>. Syntax: qos schedule-profile <NAME>.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the qos shape command do?

The qos shape command limits the egress bandwidth on an interface to a value that is lower than its line rate. An optional burst value may also be specified. Errors will be generated in the following events: • A user configures a port-shaping value that is greater than 100 percent • A user configures a kbps port-shaping value that is less than the supported minimum kbps value. The supported minimum kbps shaping value can be retrieved using the show capacities command. The no form of this command removes shaping from an interface. Specifies the maximum traffic rate in kbps. Range: 1 to 100000 000. 1 to 400000000 for the HPE Aruba Networking 9300/93 Public qos shape 111 00S Switch Series. Alternatively, the bandwidth can also be conf igured as a percentage of link bandwidth. The supported range is 1-100. Syntax: qos shape <RATE> [kbps|percent] [burst <SIZE>].

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the qos threshold-profile command do?

The qos threshold-profile command creates a QoS threshold profile and switches to the config-threshold context for the profile. If the specified threshold profile exists, this command switches to the config-threshold context for the existing profile. The threshold profile determines the action to take when a threshold is exceeded for each queue. A threshold profile is composed of up to 8 queues, numbered from 0 to 7. Each queue defines the action to take when buffer utilization exceeds a specific threshold. Configure queues with the command queue. The no form of this command removes the specified QoS threshold profile. Only profiles that are not currently applied to an interface can be removed. Specifies the name of the QoS threshold profile to create or co nfigure. Syntax: qos threshold-profile <NAME>.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the qos trust command do?

The qos trust command in the config context: • This command sets the trust mode that is globally applied to all interfaces that do not have a trust mode configured. • The no form of this command restores all interfaces that do not currently have a trust mode configured to the default setting. Public qos trust 114 In the config-if context: • This command sets the trust mode override for a specific interface. • The no form of this command clears a trust mode override. The interface then uses the global setting. This is the only way to remove a trust mode override. • When configuring qos trust none, a local-priority and color may be optionally specified. In such case, all traffic entering the device on this interface will be assigned the specified value. none Ignores all packet headers.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show forwarding-mode command do?

The show forwarding-mode command display information about the forwarding mode setting configured and applied to the switch interfaces. If cut-through forwarding mode has been configured but is not yet applied to the hardware, the output of the show forwarding-mode command will indicate the current state of hardware. NOTE Cut-through forwarding mode is not supported on ports with linked speeds less than 10G or ports with Link Level Flow Control (LLFC) Rx enabled. Shows the output from the VSX peer switch. If the switches do not have the VSX configuration or the ISL is down, the output f rom the VSX peer switch is not displayed. This parameter is onl y available on switches that support VSX. Syntax: show forwarding-mode [vsx-peer].

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show interface queue-monitor command do?

The show interface queue-monitor command display queue statistics history in the CLI as either a list or a histogram, or as a table, showing the previous five minutes of queue statistics history. Public show interface queue-monitor 143 interface [<IFNAME>] Specifies the name of an Ethernet port or LAG on the switch. If the optional <IFNAME> parameter is omitted, the output of this command displays all queue statistic data for all interfaces. Format: <MEMBER>/<SLOT>/<PORT> or lag <ID> histogram|list|table Specifies the output format for queue monitor output. • histogram - Queue statistics history is presented in histog • ram format for the last 8 hours. list - Queue statistics history is presented in the list form at for the last 8 hours • table - Queue statistics history is presented in tabular for mat for the last 5 minutes. Syntax: show interface {<IFNAME>} queue-monitor.

If no interface is specified, the command will display the queue monitoring status for all interfaces configured. {<IFNAME> | <IFRANGE>} vsx-peer Use the queue-monitor status parameters to show whether t he feature is enabled or disabled, and information about memor y consumption and monitored interfaces. Include the optional <I FNAME> parameter to filter the output of this command to sho w data for just the specified interface, or include both the <IFN AME > parameter and the additional <IFRANGE> parameter als o to view data for a range of interfaces. If no interface is specifi ed, the command will display the queue monitor status for all in terfaces that have queue monitoring configured. LAG interfaces • LAG interfaces are of type lag. • You can use the DELETE method to delete a LAG interface. For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the guide say about Behavior?

Behavior: It can be used in a collection, a specific resource, and in a request including wildcards. If the request for any resource includes an attribute that does not exist, a bad request (HTTP 400) will be returned and the request will fail. There is no limit on the number of attributes that can be specified in the request. Use case It allows for more efficient data retrieval and can reduce the amount of data transferred over the network. It reduces CPU and memory consumption, especially in cases with resources that contain many attributes and high amount of data, which also improves the response time to the user. Examples.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the guide say about Valid VLAN collection attribute list?

Valid VLAN collection attribute list: GET https://<IP>/rest/<version>/system/vlans/1? attributes=name, type, admin { "admin": "up", "name": "DEFAULT_VLAN_1", "type": "default" } Public Attributes parameter 64 Count parameter Syntax The count parameter indicates the request wants to obtain the total number of entries related to a resource. A successful response contains a JSON format with the count key and a number as the value. It works with requests including wildcard and other query parameters. Omitting it in the request is equivalent to specifying count=false. Including it in the request as count= is equivalent to specifying count=true. A request with count to a specific resource is valid and will return 1. Use Cases The user gets the number of entries instead of the values of entries. Requests to dynamic or protected resources are not limited. Limitations It is not supported for notifications. Examples Single resource A request with a count to a specific resource is valid and will return 1.

For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the guide say about CLI Management Interface?

CLI Management Interface: Introduction Firmware Site Distribution (FWSD) is an efficient feature designed to allow the client to upgrade multiple devices in a network simultaneously. The orchestrator coordinates the entire upgrade process, providing better control and monitoring. The feature can handle a large number of devices, making it suitable for networks of various sizes. Devices in the network can retrieve firmware images from seed devices, minimizing the load on external sources. This feature can be used by a CLI command or a REST request. It's possible to execute the firmware site distribution via CLI and the REST management interface request at the same time. The switch can handle up to 2 concurrent requests if the requests share the same SSL session. Although it is possible, it is recommended to use only one to avoid any inconvenience. FWSD is particularly suitable for environments with a large number of devices which are locally co-located. FWSD operates as follows: 1. The orchestrator determines which devices require upgrades. 2. The orchestrator sends upgrade commands to the pending devices. 3.

To access a peer REST API from your connected switch, insert /vsx-peer in the URI path after the server URL and before the REST API and version identifier. For example: Public.

topic: VSX peer switches and REST API access

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the guide say about Configuration maximums?

Configuration maximums: • Maximum number of subscribers per switch: 50 • Maximum number of topics in one subscription message: 2000 Public Authorization 165 Enabling the notifications subsystem on a switch The AOS-CX real-time notifications subsystem relies on the REST API, so the REST API must be enabled on the switch and VRF from which you want to receive notifications. HTTPS server must be enabled on the specified VRF. The VRF you specify determines from which network the HTTPS server can be accessed. You can enable access on multiple VRFs, including user-defined VRFs. Establishing a secure Web Socket connection through a web browser Prerequisites • Access to the switch REST API must be enabled. The REST API access mode can be either read-only or read/write. • The web browser you use must support the secure Web Socket Protocol. Procedure 1. Open a web browser page and log in to the switch Web UI or the REST API. The session cookie is managed by the browser and is shared among browser tabs. 2.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the diag-dump client-insight basic command do?

The diag-dump client-insight basic command displays the status of the Client Insight feature-whether enabled or disabled globally. It also displays latencies for all active clients that are onboarded. Syntax: diag-dump client-insight basic.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the diag-dump client-insight basic command?
The syntax of the diag-dump client-insight basic command is: diag-dump client-insight basic.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the crypto pki application command do?

The crypto pki application command configures an application to associate with a user-configured certificate. By default, all applications are associated with the default certificate "local-cert". Note that applications "captive-portal" and "hsc" are only available on certain platforms. The certificate associated with EST client is global to all EST profiles. Among the available applications, only dot1x-supplicant, est-client, and radsec-client are allowed to associate with device-identity certificate. Also note that device-identity certificate on a switch using a TPM of legacy version 1.2 only works with TLS version 1.2. 1. Syntax: crypto pki application <APP-NAME> certificate <CERT-NAME>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the crypto pki application command?
The syntax of the crypto pki application command is: crypto pki application <APP-NAME> certificate <CERT-NAME>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the crypto pki est-profile command do?

The crypto pki est-profile command creates a certificate Enrollment over Secure Transport (EST) profile and changes to the config-est-<EST- NAME> context for the profile. Each EST profile stores information about the EST service, including EST server URL Up to 16 profiles can be created. If the specified EST profile exists, this command changes to the config-est-<EST-NAME> context for the profile. The no form of this command deletes the specified EST profile. It also deletes the TA profiles whose CA certificates were downloaded from the corresponding EST server, and the leaf certificates that were enrolled using this EST profile. NOTE The deletion of the related TA profiles and enrolled certificates is permanent. Syntax: crypto pki est-profile <EST-NAME>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the crypto pki est-profile command?
The syntax of the crypto pki est-profile command is: crypto pki est-profile <EST-NAME>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show crypto pki est-profile command do?

The show crypto pki est-profile command shows a list of all configured EST profiles, or detailed information for a specific profile. Specifies the EST profile name. Range: Up to 32 alphanumeric c haracters (excluding "). <EST-NAME> Within the EST profile context, configures the URL of the certificate enrollment EST server. This is not configured by default. Any existing URL is replaced by this command. The no form of this command removes the EST server URL within the selected EST profile. The removal of the URL does not affect the TA profiles and enrolled certificates from the EST server. Specifies the EST server URL. Range: Up to 192 characters. Public url 481. Syntax: show crypto pki est-profile [<EST-NAME>].
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show crypto pki est-profile command?
The syntax of the show crypto pki est-profile command is: show crypto pki est-profile [<EST-NAME>]
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the apply macsec policy command do?

The apply macsec policy command within the selected interface context, applies the specified MAC sec policy to the selected port. When a MAC sec policy is applied to a port, MAC sec is enabled on the port and all data traffic is blocked on the port until a secure channel is successfully established. NOTE A MAC sec policy can be applied to a physical interface port that is not part of any LAG ports or to a lag port. It can also be applied to an interface that is configured as an MCLAG, VSX keep-alive, or VSX inter-switch-link. Public MAC sec commands 517 If a MAC sec policy is already applied to the selected port, this command replaces the existing policy application. NOTE For MAC sec to work, an MKA policy must also be configured and applied to the same ports. Syntax: apply macsec policy <MACSEC-POLICY-NAME>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the apply macsec policy command?
The syntax of the apply macsec policy command is: apply macsec policy <MACSEC-POLICY-NAME>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the clear macsec statistics command do?

The clear macsec statistics command clears MAC sec statistics on all MAC sec-enabled interfaces or on a specific interface or interface range. MAC sec statistics are cleared for the entire switch rather than just in the current user session. interface <IF-RANGE> Specifies one or more interfaces for which MAC sec statistics in formation is to be cleared. Syntax: clear macsec statistics [interface <IF-RANGE>].
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the clear macsec statistics command?
The syntax of the clear macsec statistics command is: clear macsec statistics [interface <IF-RANGE>]
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the clear tag mode command do?

The clear tag mode command configures the part of the Ethernet payload in a MAC sec protected frame that must precede the Security TAG (SecTAG) header in clear text. The dot1q mode allows the 802.1q tag of a MAC sec protected frame to be sent in clear text and placed before the MAC sec SecTAG header. This enables the establishment of a MAC sec tunnel between two MAC sec endpoints over a non-MAC sec Layer 2 network. The no form of the command will configure the device to place the SecTAG header immediately after the destination and source MAC addresses. MAC sec WAN extension is only supported on the following switches: Public clear tag mode 523 Platform 9300S SKU S0F96A Interfaces 1/1/1 - 1/1/8, 1/1/33 - 1/1/40 NOTE Untagged traffic is not supported on a MAC sec channel running clear-tag mode as dot1q. Syntax: clear tag mode {dot1q | none}.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the clear tag mode command?
The syntax of the clear tag mode command is: clear tag mode {dot1q | none}.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the apply mka policy command do?

The apply mka policy command within the selected interface context, applies the specified MKA policy to the selected port. To start the MKA protocol on the port, a MAC sec policy must also be applied to the port. NOTE An MKA policy can be applied to a physical interface port that is not part of any LAG ports or to a lag port. It can also be applied to an interface that is configured as an MCLAG, VSX keep-alive, or VSX inter-switch-link. If an MKA policy is already applied to the selected port, this command replaces the existing policy application. The no form of this command dissociates the specified policy from the port. Specifies the MKA policy name. Range: 1 to 32 alphanumeric ch aracters including only the three special characters "." (period), " -" (hyphen), and "_" (underscore). <MKA-POLICY-NAME>. Syntax: apply mka policy <MKA-POLICY-NAME>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the apply mka policy command?
The syntax of the apply mka policy command is: apply mka policy <MKA-POLICY-NAME>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the clear mka statistics command do?

The clear mka statistics command clears MKA statistics on all MAC sec-enabled interfaces or on a specific interface or interface range. MKA statistics are cleared for the entire switch rather than just in the current user session. Public clear mka statistics 541 interface <IF-RANGE> Specifies one or more interfaces (ports) for which MKA statisti cs information is to be cleared. Configures the MAC sec policy to use data delay protection. Data delay protection allows MKA participants to ensure that the data frames protected by MAC sec are not delayed by more than 2 seconds. Syntax: clear mka statistics [interface <IF-RANGE>].
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the clear mka statistics command?
The syntax of the clear mka statistics command is: clear mka statistics [interface <IF-RANGE>]
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenti command?
The syntax of the aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenti command is: aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator {enable | disable}.

syntax: aaa authentication port-access dot1x authenticator {enable | disable}

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the clear dot1x authenticator statistics interfac command do?

The clear dot1x authenticator statistics interfac command clears the 802.1X authentication statistics associated with the port and all the authenticator clients attached to this port. If no interface is specified, the statistics is cleared for all 802.1X enabled ports. Specifies the interface name. <IF-NAME>. Syntax: clear dot1x authenticator statistics [interface <IF-NAME>].
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the clear dot1x authenticator statistics interfac command?
The syntax of the clear dot1x authenticator statistics interfac command is: clear dot1x authenticator statistics [interface <IF-NAME>]
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth command do?
The aaa authentication port-access mac-auth command enables or disables MAC authentication globally or at the port-level. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth {enable | disable}.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth command?
The syntax of the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth command is: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth {enable | disable}.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth auth command do?

The aaa authentication port-access mac-auth auth command configures the RADIUS authentication method for MAC authentication. Public aaa authentication port-access mac-auth auth-metho... 597 a a a a u t h e n t i c a t i o n p o r t - a c c e s s m a c - a u t h a u t h - m e t h o... Following are the MAC authentication methods supported: • CHAP • PAP NOTE The PEAP-MSCHAPv2 method of authentication is not supported. The no form of the command resets the authentication method to the default, chap. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth auth-method {chap | pap}.

syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth auth-method {chap | pap}

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth auth command?
The syntax of the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth auth command is: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth auth-method {chap | pap}.

syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth auth-method {chap | pap}

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth cache command do?

The aaa authentication port-access mac-auth cache command enables cached reauthentication on a port. Cached reauthentication allows MAC reauthentications to succeed when the RADIUS server is unavailable. Users who are already authenticated, retain their currently assigned RADIUS attributes. The no form of the command disables cached reauthentication. Configures the period during which an authenticated client, which has failed to reauthenticate because the RADIUS server is unreachable, remains authenticated. The no form of the command resets the cached reauthentication period to the default, 3600 seconds. Specifies the cached reauthentication period (in seconds). Defa ult: 3600. Range: 1 to 4294967295. <PERIOD>. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth cached-reauth.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth cache command?
The syntax of the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth cache command is: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth cached-reauth.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth quiet command do?

The aaa authentication port-access mac-auth quiet command configures the period during which the switch does not try to authenticate a rejected client. The no form of the command resets the quiet period to the default, 60 seconds. Specifies the quiet period (in seconds). Default: 60. Range: 0 to 65535. <PERIOD>. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth quiet-period <PERIOD>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth quiet command?
The syntax of the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth quiet command is: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth quiet-period <PERIOD>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth radiu command do?

The aaa authentication port-access mac-auth radiu command configures the MAC authentication server group globally or for a particular port. The no form of the command resets the authentication server group to the default value, radius. When configured on a port, the no form of the command resets the server group on that port to the globally configured group. If no global RADIUS server group is configured, the no form of the command resets the configuration to the default group, radius. NOTE When the RADIUS server group for MAC authentication is updated on a port, any existing clients on the port that were authenticated using the previous globally configured group will associate with the new group for the port during the next re-authentication cycle. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth radius server-group <GROUP-NAME>.

syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth radius server-group <GROUP-NAME>

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth radiu command?
The syntax of the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth radiu command is: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth radius server-group <GROUP-NAME>

syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth radius server-group <GROUP-NAME>

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth reaut command do?

The aaa authentication port-access mac-auth reaut command enables periodic MAC reauthentication of authenticated clients on the port. The no form of the command disables periodic MAC reauthentication on the port. Configures the period after which MAC authenticated clients must be reauthenticated on the port. You must first enable MAC reauthentication on the port before configuring the MAC reauthentication period. The no form of the command resets the MAC reauthentication period to the default, 3600 seconds. Specifies the MAC reauthentication period (in seconds). Default: 3600. Range: 1 to 4294967295. <PERIOD>. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth reauth.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth reaut command?
The syntax of the aaa authentication port-access mac-auth reaut command is: aaa authentication port-access mac-auth reauth.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the aaa authentication port-access allow-cdp-auth command do?

The aaa authentication port-access allow-cdp-auth command by default authentication is allowed via CDP packets which are received on the port. Use the no version of this command to prevent authentication using CDP packets received on the port. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access allow-cdp-auth.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the aaa authentication port-access allow-cdp-auth command?
The syntax of the aaa authentication port-access allow-cdp-auth command is: aaa authentication port-access allow-cdp-auth.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the aaa authentication port-access auth-mode command do?

The aaa authentication port-access auth-mode command configures the authentication mode for the port. By default, client mode is enabled. Selects client mode. In this mode, all clients connecting to the port are sent for authentication. The maximum number of clien ts allowed to connect to the port is limited by the client limit va Public aaa authentication port-access auth-mode 617 lue configured with the aaa authentication port-access clien t-limit command. Selects device mode. In this mode, only the first client connecti ng to the port is sent for authentication. Once this client is auth enticated, the port is considered as open and all subsequent cli ents trying to connect on that port are not sent for authenticati on. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access auth-mode {client-mode | device-mode | }.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the aaa authentication port-access auth-mode command?
The syntax of the aaa authentication port-access auth-mode command is: aaa authentication port-access auth-mode {client-mode | device-mode | }.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the aaa authentication port-access client-limit command do?

The aaa authentication port-access client-limit command configures the maximum number of clients that can simultaneously connect to a port. Public aaa authentication port-access client-limit 624 The no form of this command resets the number of clients to the default. Specifies the maximum number of clients. Default: 1. Range: 1 to 32 (8325, 10000). <CLIENTS>. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access client-limit <CLIENTS>.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the aaa authentication port-access client-limit command?
The syntax of the aaa authentication port-access client-limit command is: aaa authentication port-access client-limit <CLIENTS>
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show aaa authentication port-access interface command do?

The show aaa authentication port-access interface command shows information about the status of the role applied on ports. RADIUS overridden user roles are suffixed with *. The role name is not displayed for clients that do not use local, downloaded, or RADIUS overridden role. Specifies all interfaces. Specifies the interface name. Specifies the client MAC address. Syntax: show aaa authentication port-access interface {all | <IFRANGE>}.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what is the syntax of the show aaa authentication port-access interface command?
The syntax of the show aaa authentication port-access interface command is: show aaa authentication port-access interface {all | <IFRANGE>}.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show port-access port-security violation clie command do?

The show port-access port-security violation clie command shows information on the number of client-limit-exceeded security violations that have occurred. The output can be filtered by interface. Specifies all interfaces. Specifies the interface name or a comma-separated list of inte rfaces, or a hyphen-separated interface range. Syntax: show port-access port-security violation client-limit-exceeded interface.

syntax: show port-access port-security violation client-limit-exceeded interface

The show port-access port-security interface clie command shows port security clients status information for the ports. The output can be filtered by interface or MAC address. Selects all interfaces. Specifies the interface name. Specifies the client MAC address. Public show port-access port-security interface client-st... 741 s h o w p o r t - a c c e s s p o r t - s e c u r i t y i n t e r f a c e c l i e n t - s t... Syntax: show port-access port-security interface {all|<IF-NAME>}.

The syntax of the show port-access port-security interface clie command is: show port-access port-security interface {all|<IF-NAME>}.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the show port-access port-security interface port command do?

The show port-access port-security interface port command shows port security statistics for the ports in a switch. The output can be filtered by interface. Selects all interfaces. Specifies the interface name. Syntax: show port-access port-security interface {all|<IF-NAME>} port-statistics.

The syntax of the show port-access port-security interface port command is: show port-access port-security interface {all|<IF-NAME>} port-statistics.
For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the apply fault-monitor profile command do?

The apply fault-monitor profile command applies a fault monitoring profile to the selected interface or interface range. Public apply fault-monitor profile 756 The no form of this command removes the fault monitoring profile from the selected interface or interface range. Specifies the fault monitor profile name. Range: Up to 64 alpha numeric and special characters. <PROFILE-NAME> Creates a fault monitoring profile and enters its context which is indicated as (config-fault-monitor- profile). If the profile already exists, this command enters the profile context. A maximum of 16 fault monitoring profiles are supported. For information on enabling a fault within a fault monitor profile, see (Fault enabling/disabling). For information on configuring actions and thresholds for a fault, respectively see action and threshold. Syntax: apply fault-monitor profile <PROFILE-NAME>.
